Chapter 6

Baptism is in similitude of the death, burial, and resurrection of Christ—The wages of sin is death—Christ brings eternal life.

1 What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin, that [agrace](#note1a) may abound?

2 [aGod forbid](#note2a). How shall we, that are dead to [bsin](#note2b), live any longer therein?

3 Know ye not, that so many of us as were [abaptized](#note3a) into Jesus Christ were baptized into his death?

4 Therefore we are [aburied](#note4a) with him by [bbaptism](#note4b) into death: that like as Christ was raised up from the [cdead](#note4c) by the glory of the Father, even so we also should [dwalk](#note4d) in [enewness](#note4e) of life.

5 For if we have been planted together in the [alikeness](#note5a) of his [bdeath](#note5b), we shall be also in the likeness of his [cresurrection](#note5c):

6 Knowing this, that our [aold](#note6a) man is crucified with him, that the [bbody](#note6b) of sin might be [cdestroyed](#note6c), that henceforth we should not serve [dsin](#note6d).

7 For he that is [adead](#note7a) is [bfreed](#note7b) from sin.

8 Now if we be [adead](#note8a) with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with him:

9 Knowing that Christ being [araised](#note9a) from the dead dieth no more; death hath no more dominion over him.

10 For in that he died, he died unto [asin](#note10a) [bonce](#note10b): but in that he liveth, he liveth unto God.

11 Likewise reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but [aalive](#note11a) unto God through Jesus Christ our Lord.

12 Let not sin therefore reign in your [amortal](#note12a) body, that ye should obey it in the [blusts](#note12b) thereof.

13 Neither [ayield](#note13a) ye your [bmembers](#note13b) as [cinstruments](#note13c) of unrighteousness unto sin: but [dyield](#note13d) yourselves unto God, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of [erighteousness](#note13e) unto God.

14 [aFor sin](#note14a) shall not have [bdominion](#note14b) over you: for ye are not under the law, but under grace.

15 What then? shall we sin, because we are not under the law, but under [agrace](#note15a)? [bGod forbid](#note15b).

16 Know ye not, that to whom ye yield yourselves [aservants](#note16a) to obey, his servants ye are to whom ye [bobey](#note16b); whether of sin unto death, or of obedience unto righteousness?

17 But God be thanked, that ye were the servants of sin, but ye have [aobeyed](#note17a) from the heart that form of doctrine which was delivered you.

18 Being then made [afree](#note18a) from [bsin](#note18b), ye became the servants of righteousness.

19 I speak after the manner of men because of the infirmity of your flesh: for as ye have yielded your members servants to uncleanness and to iniquity unto iniquity; even so now yield your members servants to righteousness unto holiness.

20 For when ye were the servants of sin, ye were [afree from](#note20a) righteousness.

21 What [afruit](#note21a) had ye then in those things whereof ye are now ashamed? for the end of those things is death.

22 But now being made [afree](#note22a) from sin, and become servants to God, ye have your [bfruit](#note22b) unto [choliness](#note22c), and the end everlasting life.

23 For the [awages](#note23a) of [bsin](#note23b) is [cdeath](#note23c); but the [dgift](#note23d) of God is [eeternal life](#note23e) through Jesus Christ our Lord.

Chapter 7

The law of Moses is fulfilled in Christ—Paul delights in the law of God after the inward man.

1 Know ye not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) how that the [alaw](#note1a) hath dominion over a man as long as he liveth?

2 For the [awoman](#note2a) which hath an [bhusband](#note2b) is bound by the law to her husband so long as he liveth; but if the husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of her husband.

3 So then if, while her husband liveth, she be married to another man, she shall be called an adulteress: but if her husband be dead, she is free from that law; so that she is no adulteress, though she be married to another man.

4 Wherefore, my brethren, ye also are become [adead](#note4a) to the [blaw](#note4b) by the body of Christ; that ye should be married to another, even to him who is raised from the dead, that we should bring forth fruit unto God.

5 [aFor](#note5a) when we were in the flesh, the [bmotions of sins](#note5b), which were by the law, did work in our members to bring forth fruit unto [cdeath](#note5c).

6 But now we are [adelivered from](#note6a) the law, that being [bdead](#note6b) wherein we were held; that we should serve in newness of [cspirit](#note6c), and not in the oldness of the letter.

7 What shall we say then? Is the law sin? [aGod forbid](#note7a). Nay, I had not known sin, but by the [blaw](#note7b): for I had not known [clust](#note7c), except the law had said, Thou shalt not [dcovet](#note7d).

8 But sin, taking occasion by the commandment, wrought in me all manner of concupiscence. For without the [alaw](#note8a) sin was dead.

9 For I was alive without the law once: but when the commandment came, sin revived, and I died.

10 And the commandment, which was ordained to life, I found to be unto [adeath](#note10a).

11 For sin, [ataking occasion](#note11a) by the commandment, deceived me, and by it slew me.

12 Wherefore the law is holy, and the commandment holy, and just, and good.

13 Was then that which is good made death unto me? [aGod forbid](#note13a). But sin, that it might appear sin, working death in me by that which is good; that sin by the commandment might become exceeding sinful.

14 For we know that the [alaw](#note14a) is spiritual: but I am [bcarnal](#note14b), [csold under](#note14c) sin.

15 [aFor](#note15a) [bthat which I do](#note15b) I [callow](#note15c) not: for what I [dwould](#note15d), that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.

16 If then I do that which I [awould](#note16a) not, I consent unto the law that it is good.

17 Now then it is no more I that [ado](#note17a) it, but sin that dwelleth in me.

18 For I know that in me (that is, in my [aflesh](#note18a),) [bdwelleth](#note18b) no good thing: for to will is present with me; but how to [cperform](#note18c) that which is good I find not.

19 For the [agood](#note19a) that I [bwould](#note19b) I do not: but the evil which I would not, that I do.

20 Now if I do [athat I would not](#note20a), it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.

21 I find then a law, that, when I would do good, evil is [apresent](#note21a) with me.

22 For I [adelight](#note22a) in the law of God after the inward man:

23 But I see another law in my members, [awarring](#note23a) against the law of my mind, and bringing me into [bcaptivity](#note23b) to the law of sin which is in my members.

24 O [awretched](#note24a) man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

25 I thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord. So then with the mind I myself serve the law of God; but with the flesh the law of sin.

Chapter 8

The law of Christ brings life and peace—Those adopted as children of God become joint heirs with Christ—God’s elect are foreordained to eternal life—Christ makes intercession for man.

1 There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who [awalk](#note1a) not after the [bflesh](#note1b), but after the [cSpirit](#note1c).

2 For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me [afree](#note2a) from the law of sin and death.

3 For what the law could not do, in that it was [aweak](#note3a) through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful [bflesh](#note3b), and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:

4 That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the [aSpirit](#note4a).

5 For they that are after the flesh do [amind](#note5a) the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.

6 For to be [acarnally](#note6a) minded is [bdeath](#note6b); but to be [cspiritually](#note6c) minded is life and [dpeace](#note6d).

7 Because the [acarnal](#note7a) mind is [benmity](#note7b) against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.

8 So then they that are [ain the flesh](#note8a) cannot please God.

9 [aBut](#note9a) ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the [bSpirit](#note9b) of God [cdwell](#note9c) in you. Now if any man have not the [dSpirit of Christ](#note9d), he is none of his.

10 And if [aChrist](#note10a) be in you, [bthe](#note10b) body is dead because of sin; but the Spirit is life because of [crighteousness](#note10c).

11 But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also [aquicken](#note11a) your [bmortal](#note11b) bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.

12 Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.

13 For if ye live after the flesh, ye [ashall die](#note13a): but if ye through the Spirit do [bmortify](#note13b) the deeds of the body, ye shall live.

14 For as many as are [aled](#note14a) by the [bSpirit](#note14b) of God, they are the [csons](#note14c) of God.

15 For ye have not received the spirit of [abondage](#note15a) again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of [badoption](#note15b), whereby we cry, Abba, Father.

16 The Spirit itself beareth [awitness](#note16a) with our [bspirit](#note16b), that we are the [cchildren](#note16c) of God:

17 And if children, then heirs; [aheirs](#note17a) of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we [bsuffer](#note17b) with him, that we may be also glorified together.

18 For I reckon that the [asufferings](#note18a) of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the [bglory](#note18b) which shall be revealed [cin us](#note18c).

19 For the [aearnest expectation](#note19a) of the [bcreature](#note19b) waiteth for the [cmanifestation](#note19c) of the sons of God.

20 For the creature was made subject to [avanity](#note20a), not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subjected the same in hope,

21 Because the [acreature](#note21a) itself also shall be [bdelivered](#note21b) from the bondage of [ccorruption](#note21c) into the glorious [dliberty](#note21d) of the children of God.

22 For we know that the whole creation [agroaneth](#note22a) and travaileth in pain together until now.

23 And not only they, but ourselves also, [awhich](#note23a) have the [bfirstfruits](#note23b) of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the [cadoption](#note23c), to wit, the redemption of our body.

24 For we are saved by [ahope](#note24a): but hope that is [bseen](#note24b) is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?

25 But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.

26 Likewise the Spirit also [ahelpeth](#note26a) our infirmities: for we know not what we should [bpray](#note26b) for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh [cintercession](#note26c) for us with [dgroanings](#note26d) which cannot be uttered.

27 And he that [asearcheth](#note27a) the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh [bintercession](#note27b) for the saints according to the will of God.

28 And we know that all things work together for [agood](#note28a) to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.

29 [aFor](#note29a) whom he did [bforeknow](#note29b), he also [cdid predestinate](#note29c) [dto be conformed to](#note29d) the [eimage](#note29e) of his Son, that he might be the [ffirstborn](#note29f) among many brethren.

30 Moreover whom he [adid predestinate](#note30a), them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.

31 What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us, who can [abe against us](#note31a)?

32 He that spared not his own [aSon](#note32a), but [bdelivered](#note32b) him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us [call](#note32c) things?

33 Who shall [alay](#note33a) any thing to the charge of God’s elect? It is God that justifieth.

34 Who is he that condemneth? It is Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh [aintercession](#note34a) for us.

35 Who shall separate us from the [alove](#note35a) of Christ? shall [btribulation](#note35b), or distress, or [cpersecution](#note35c), or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?

36 As it is written, For thy sake we are [akilled](#note36a) all the day long; we are accounted as [bsheep](#note36b) for the slaughter.

37 Nay, in all these things we are [amore](#note37a) than [bconquerors](#note37b) through him that loved us.

38 For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,

39 Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to [aseparate](#note39a) us from the [blove](#note39b) of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

Chapter 9

Paul explains how the law of election (foreordination) operates—The people of Israel are chosen (foreordained) to receive the adoption, covenants, promises, and blessings of the gospel; yet they are not all Israel who are of Israel—They must seek their blessings by faith—The Gentiles also attain to righteousness and salvation by faith.

1 I say the truth in Christ, I [alie](#note1a) not, my [bconscience](#note1b) also bearing me witness in the Holy Ghost,

2 That I have great heaviness and continual sorrow in my heart.

3 [aFor](#note3a) I could wish that myself were [baccursed](#note3b) from Christ for my brethren, my kinsmen according to the flesh:

4 Who are Israelites; to whom pertaineth the [aadoption](#note4a), and the glory, and the [bcovenants](#note4b), and the giving of the law, and the service of God, and the promises;

5 Whose are the fathers, and of whom as concerning the [aflesh](#note5a) Christ came, who is over all, God blessed for ever. Amen.

6 Not as though the word of God [ahath taken none effect](#note6a). For they are not all Israel, which are of [bIsrael](#note6b):

7 [aNeither](#note7a), because they are the seed of Abraham, are they all children: but, In [bIsaac](#note7b) shall thy seed be called.

8 That is, They which are the children of the flesh, these are not the [achildren](#note8a) of God: but the children of the promise are counted for the seed.

9 For this is the word of promise, At this time will I come, and Sara shall have a [ason](#note9a).

10 And not only this; but when [aRebecca](#note10a) also had conceived by one, even by our father Isaac;

11 (For the children being not yet born, neither having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to [aelection](#note11a) might stand, not of works, but of him that calleth;)

12 It was said unto her, The [aelder](#note12a) shall serve the younger.

13 As it is written, Jacob have I [aloved](#note13a), but Esau have I hated.

14 What shall we say then? Is there [aunrighteousness](#note14a) with God? [bGod forbid](#note14b).

15 For he saith to Moses, I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have [acompassion](#note15a) on whom I will have compassion.

16 So then it is not of him that willeth, nor of him that runneth, but of God that sheweth [amercy](#note16a).

17 For the scripture saith unto Pharaoh, Even for this same [apurpose](#note17a) have I raised thee up, that I might shew my power in thee, and that my name might be declared throughout all the earth.

18 Therefore hath he [amercy](#note18a) on whom he will have mercy, and whom he will he [bhardeneth](#note18b).

19 Thou wilt say then unto me, Why doth he yet find fault? For who hath resisted his will?

20 Nay but, O man, who art thou that [arepliest against](#note20a) God? Shall the thing formed say to him that formed it, Why hast thou [bmade](#note20b) me thus?

21 Hath not the [apotter](#note21a) power over the clay, of the same lump to make one vessel unto honour, and another unto dishonour?

22 What if God, willing to shew his wrath, and to make his power known, endured with much longsuffering the vessels of wrath fitted to [adestruction](#note22a):

23 And that he might make known the [ariches](#note23a) of his [bglory](#note23b) on the vessels of mercy, which he had afore prepared unto glory,

24 Even us, whom he hath called, not of the Jews only, but also of the Gentiles?

25 As he saith also in [aOsee](#note25a), I will call them my people, which were not my [bpeople](#note25b); and her beloved, which was not beloved.

26 And it shall come to pass, that in the place where it was said unto them, Ye are not my people; there shall they be called the [achildren](#note26a) of the living God.

27 [aEsaias](#note27a) also crieth concerning Israel, Though the [bnumber](#note27b) of the children of [cIsrael](#note27c) be as the sand of the sea, a [dremnant](#note27d) shall be saved:

28 For he will finish the work, and cut it short in [arighteousness](#note28a): because a short work will the Lord make upon the earth.

29 And as Esaias said before, Except the Lord of [aSabaoth](#note29a) had left us a [bseed](#note29b), we had been as Sodoma, and been made like unto Gomorrha.

30 What shall we say then? That the Gentiles, which followed not after righteousness, have attained to righteousness, even the [arighteousness](#note30a) which is of faith.

31 But [aIsrael](#note31a), which followed after the law of righteousness, hath not attained to the law of righteousness.

32 [aWherefore](#note32a)? Because they sought it not by faith, but as it were by the works of the law. For they [bstumbled](#note32b) at that stumblingstone;

33 As it is written, Behold, I lay in Sion a [astumblingstone](#note33a) and [brock](#note33b) of [coffence](#note33c): and whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.

Chapter 10

Salvation comes through righteousness to those who believe in Christ—Faith comes by hearing the gospel taught by legal administrators sent of God.

1 Brethren, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for Israel is, that they might be [asaved](#note1a).

2 For I bear them record that they have a [azeal](#note2a) of God, but not according to knowledge.

3 For they being ignorant of God’s [arighteousness](#note3a), and [bgoing about](#note3b) to establish their own righteousness, have not [csubmitted](#note3c) themselves unto the righteousness of God.

4 For Christ is the [aend](#note4a) of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.

5 For Moses describeth the righteousness which is of the law, That the man which doeth those things shall live by them.

6 But the [arighteousness](#note6a) which is of faith speaketh on this wise, Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven? (that is, to bring Christ down from above:)

7 Or, Who shall descend into the deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.)

8 But what saith it? The word is nigh thee, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart: that is, the word of faith, which we preach;

9 That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.

10 For with the [aheart](#note10a) man [bbelieveth](#note10b) unto righteousness; and with the mouth confession is made unto [csalvation](#note10c).

11 For the scripture saith, Whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.

12 For there is no [adifference](#note12a) between the Jew and the Greek: for the same Lord over all is [brich](#note12b) unto all that [ccall](#note12c) upon him.

13 For whosoever shall [acall](#note13a) upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.

14 How then shall they call on him in whom they have not [abelieved](#note14a)? and how shall they believe in him of whom they have not heard? and how shall they hear without a [bpreacher](#note14b)?

15 And how shall they [apreach](#note15a), except they be [bsent](#note15b)? as it is written, How beautiful are the [cfeet](#note15c) of them that preach the [dgospel](#note15d) of [epeace](#note15e), and bring glad tidings of good things!

16 But they have not all [aobeyed](#note16a) the gospel. For Esaias saith, Lord, who hath [bbelieved](#note16b) our report?

17 So then [afaith](#note17a) cometh by [bhearing](#note17b), and hearing by the word of God.

18 But I say, Have they not heard? Yes verily, their sound went into all the earth, and their [awords](#note18a) unto the ends of the [bworld](#note18b).

19 But I say, Did not Israel know? First Moses saith, I will provoke you to [ajealousy](#note19a) by them that are no people, and by a foolish nation I will anger you.

20 But [aEsaias](#note20a) is very bold, and saith, I was found of them that [bsought](#note20b) me not; I was made manifest unto them that asked not after me.

21 But to Israel he saith, All day long I have stretched forth my hands unto a [adisobedient](#note21a) and [bgainsaying](#note21b) people.

Chapter 11

Israel was chosen (foreordained) according to the election of grace—But some harden their hearts against it—The Gentiles are adopted into the house of Israel—The gospel goes preferentially to the Gentiles until the fulness of the Gentiles.

1 I say then, Hath God cast away his people? [aGod forbid](#note1a). For I also am an [bIsraelite](#note1b), of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.

2 God hath not cast away his [apeople](#note2a) which he [bforeknew](#note2b). [cWot](#note2c) ye not what the scripture saith of [dElias](#note2d)? how he maketh intercession to God against Israel, saying,

3 Lord, they have [akilled](#note3a) thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am [bleft](#note3b) alone, and they seek my life.

4 But what saith the answer of God unto him? I have [areserved](#note4a) to myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee to the image of Baal.

5 Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the [aelection](#note5a) of grace.

6 And if by [agrace](#note6a), then is it no more of [bworks](#note6b): otherwise grace is no more grace. But if it be of works, then is it no more grace: otherwise work is no more work.

7 What then? Israel hath not obtained that which he seeketh for; but the election hath obtained it, and the rest [awere blinded](#note7a)

8 (According as it is written, God hath given them the spirit of [aslumber](#note8a), eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not [bhear](#note8b);) unto this day.

9 And David saith, Let their table be made a [asnare](#note9a), and a trap, and a stumblingblock, and a [brecompence](#note9b) unto them:

10 Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, and bow down their back alway.

11 I say then, Have they stumbled that they should fall? [aGod forbid](#note11a): but rather through their fall salvation is come unto the Gentiles, for to provoke them to [bjealousy](#note11b).

12 Now if the fall of them be the riches of the world, and the [adiminishing](#note12a) of them the riches of the Gentiles; how much more their fulness?

13 For I speak to you [aGentiles](#note13a), inasmuch as I am the apostle of the Gentiles, I [bmagnify](#note13b) mine office:

14 If by any means I may provoke to emulation them which are my flesh, and might save some of them.

15 For if the casting away of them be the [areconciling](#note15a) of the world, what shall the receiving of them be, but life from the dead?

16 For if the firstfruit be holy, the lump is also holy: and if the [aroot](#note16a) be holy, so are the branches.

17 And if some of the branches be broken off, and thou, being a wild olive [atree](#note17a), wert grafted in among them, and with them partakest of the root and fatness of the olive tree;

18 [aBoast](#note18a) not against the branches. But if thou boast, thou bearest not the root, but the root thee.

19 Thou wilt say then, The branches were broken off, that I might be grafted in.

20 Well; because of [aunbelief](#note20a) they were [bbroken](#note20b) off, and thou standest by [cfaith](#note20c). Be not highminded, but [dfear](#note20d):

21 For if God spared not the [anatural](#note21a) branches, take heed lest he also [bspare](#note21b) not thee.

22 Behold therefore the goodness and severity of God: on them which fell, severity; but toward thee, goodness, if thou continue in his goodness: otherwise thou also shalt be cut off.

23 And they also, if they abide not still in unbelief, shall be grafted in: for God is able to graft them in again.

24 For if thou wert cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature, and wert grafted contrary to nature into a good olive tree: how much more shall these, which be the natural branches, be grafted into their own olive tree?

25 For I would not, brethren, that ye should be ignorant of this mystery, lest ye should be wise in your own [aconceits](#note25a); that [bblindness](#note25b) in part is happened to Israel, until the [cfulness](#note25c) of the [dGentiles](#note25d) be come in.

26 And so all [aIsrael](#note26a) shall be saved: as it is written, There shall come out of [bSion](#note26b) the [cDeliverer](#note26c), and shall turn away ungodliness from Jacob:

27 For this is my [acovenant](#note27a) unto them, when I shall take away their sins.

28 As concerning the gospel, they are enemies for your sakes: but as touching the election, they are beloved for the fathers’ sakes.

29 For the gifts and calling of God are without repentance.

30 For as ye in times past [ahave not believed](#note30a) God, yet have now obtained mercy through their [bunbelief](#note30b):

31 Even so have these also now not [abelieved](#note31a), that through your mercy they also may obtain mercy.

32 For God hath [aconcluded](#note32a) them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.

33 O the depth of the [ariches](#note33a) both of the [bwisdom](#note33b) and [cknowledge](#note33c) of God! how [dunsearchable](#note33d) are his judgments, and his ways past [efinding](#note33e) out!

34 For who hath known the [amind](#note34a) of the Lord? or who hath been his [bcounsellor](#note34b)?

35 Or who hath first given to him, and it shall be recompensed unto him again?

36 For of him, and [athrough](#note36a) him, and to him, are all things: to whom be glory for ever. Amen.

Chapter 12

Paul counsels the Saints to present their bodies as a living sacrifice, to use their own grace-given gifts, and to live as Saints should live.

1 I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your [abodies](#note1a) a living [bsacrifice](#note1b), holy, [cacceptable](#note1c) unto God, which is your reasonable [dservice](#note1d).

2 And be not conformed to this [aworld](#note2a): but be ye [btransformed](#note2b) by the [crenewing](#note2c) of your [dmind](#note2d), that ye may [eprove](#note2e) what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, [fwill](#note2f) of God.

3 For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to [athink](#note3a) of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think [bsoberly](#note3b), according as [cGod](#note3c) hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.

4 For as we have many members in one body, and all members have not the same [aoffice](#note4a):

5 So we, being many, are [aone](#note5a) [bbody](#note5b) in Christ, and every one members one of another.

6 Having then gifts differing according to the [agrace](#note6a) that is given to us, whether prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of [bfaith](#note6b);

7 Or ministry, let us wait on our ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching;

8 Or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, let him do it with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with [acheerfulness](#note8a).

9 Let love be [awithout dissimulation](#note9a). Abhor that which is [bevil](#note9b); cleave to that which is good.

10 Be kindly [aaffectioned](#note10a) one to another with [bbrotherly](#note10b) [clove](#note10c); in honour preferring one another;

11 Not slothful in [abusiness](#note11a); [bfervent](#note11b) in spirit; serving the Lord;

12 Rejoicing in hope; [apatient](#note12a) in [btribulation](#note12b); [ccontinuing instant in](#note12c) prayer;

13 Distributing to the necessity of saints; given to [ahospitality](#note13a).

14 Bless them which [apersecute](#note14a) you: bless, and [bcurse](#note14b) not.

15 Rejoice with them that do rejoice, and [aweep](#note15a) with them that weep.

16 Be of the same mind one toward another. Mind not high things, but [acondescend](#note16a) to men of low estate. Be not wise in your own [bconceits](#note16b).

17 [aRecompense](#note17a) to no man [bevil](#note17b) for evil. Provide things [chonest](#note17c) in the [dsight](#note17d) of all men.

18 If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live [apeaceably](#note18a) with all men.

19 Dearly beloved, [aavenge](#note19a) not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.

20 Therefore if thine [aenemy](#note20a) hunger, feed him; if he thirst, give him drink: for in so doing thou shalt heap coals of fire on his head.

21 Be not overcome of evil, but [aovercome](#note21a) evil with good.

Chapter 13

Paul counsels, Be subject unto God’s ministers; keep the commandments; love one another; righteousness leads to salvation.

1 Let every soul [abe subject](#note1a) unto the higher [bpowers](#note1b). For there is no [cpower](#note1c) [dbut of God](#note1d): the [epowers](#note1e) that be are ordained of God.

2 Whosoever therefore resisteth the power, resisteth the ordinance of God: and they that [aresist](#note2a) shall receive to themselves [bdamnation](#note2b).

3 For rulers are not a terror to good works, but to the evil. Wilt thou then not be afraid of the [apower](#note3a)? do that which is [bgood](#note3b), and thou shalt have praise of the same:

4 For he is [athe minister](#note4a) of God to thee for good. But if thou do that which is evil, be afraid; for he beareth not the sword in vain: for he is the minister of God, a revenger to execute wrath upon him that doeth evil.

5 Wherefore ye must needs be subject, not only for wrath, but also for [aconscience](#note5a) sake.

6 [aFor](#note6a) for this cause pay ye tribute also: for they are God’s ministers, attending continually upon this very thing.

7 Render therefore to all their dues: tribute to whom tribute is due; custom to whom custom; fear to whom fear; [ahonour](#note7a) to whom honour.

8 [aOwe](#note8a) no man any thing, but to love one another: for he that [bloveth](#note8b) another hath fulfilled the law.

9 For this, Thou shalt not commit [aadultery](#note9a), Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not [bsteal](#note9b), Thou shalt not bear false witness, Thou shalt not [ccovet](#note9c); and if there be any other commandment, it is briefly comprehended in this saying, namely, Thou shalt love thy [dneighbour](#note9d) as thyself.

10 [aLove](#note10a) worketh no ill to his neighbour: therefore love is the fulfilling of the [blaw](#note10b).

11 And that, knowing the time, that now it is high [atime](#note11a) to awake out of [bsleep](#note11b): for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed.

12 The night is far spent, the [aday](#note12a) is at hand: let us therefore cast off the works of [bdarkness](#note12b), and let us put on the [carmour](#note12c) of [dlight](#note12d).

13 Let us walk [ahonestly](#note13a), as in the day; not in [brioting](#note13b) and [cdrunkenness](#note13c), not in [dchambering](#note13d) and wantonness, not in strife and [eenvying](#note13e).

14 But [aput](#note14a) ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the [blusts](#note14b) thereof.

Chapter 14

Avoid quarreling about opinions and making unrighteous judgment of each other—Every knee will bow to Christ—The kingdom of God embraces righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.

1 Him that is [aweak](#note1a) in the faith receive ye, but not to [bdoubtful](#note1b) [cdisputations](#note1c).

2 For one believeth that he may eat all things: another, who is weak, eateth [aherbs](#note2a).

3 Let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not; and let not him which eateth not [ajudge](#note3a) him that eateth: for God hath received him.

4 Who art thou that [ajudgest](#note4a) another man’s servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.

5 One man esteemeth one [aday](#note5a) above another: another esteemeth every day alike. Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.

6 He that regardeth the day, regardeth it unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard it. He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.

7 For none of us [aliveth](#note7a) to himself, and no man dieth to himself.

8 For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we [adie](#note8a) unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord’s.

9 For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that he might be [aLord](#note9a) both of the [bdead](#note9b) and living.

10 But why dost thou [ajudge](#note10a) thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the [bjudgment](#note10b) seat of Christ.

11 For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every [aknee](#note11a) shall bow to me, and every tongue shall [bconfess](#note11b) to God.

12 So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.

13 Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a [astumblingblock](#note13a) or an occasion to fall in his brother’s way.

14 I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that there is nothing [aunclean](#note14a) of itself: but to him that [besteemeth](#note14b) any thing to be unclean, to him it is unclean.

15 But if thy brother be grieved with thy meat, [anow](#note15a) walkest thou not [bcharitably](#note15b). Destroy not him [cwith thy meat](#note15c), for whom Christ died.

16 Let not then your good be evil spoken of:

17 For the kingdom of God is not [ameat](#note17a) and drink; but [brighteousness](#note17b), and [cpeace](#note17c), and [djoy](#note17d) in the Holy Ghost.

18 For he that in these things serveth Christ is acceptable to God, and approved of men.

19 Let us therefore follow after the things which make for [apeace](#note19a), and things wherewith one may [bedify](#note19b) another.

20 [aFor meat](#note20a) destroy not the [bwork](#note20b) of God. All things indeed are pure; but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence.

21 It is good neither to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor any thing whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is [aoffended](#note21a), or is made weak.

22 Hast thou faith? have it to thyself before God. Happy is he that condemneth not himself [ain](#note22a) that thing which he alloweth.

23 And he that [adoubteth](#note23a) is damned if he eat, because he eateth not of faith: for whatsoever is not of [bfaith](#note23b) is [csin](#note23c).

Chapter 15

True Saints fellowship one another—Paul recounts his diligence in preaching the gospel—The gifts of the Spirit are poured out upon the Gentiles.

1 We then that are strong ought to [abear](#note1a) the infirmities of the [bweak](#note1b), and not to please ourselves.

2 Let every one of us please his [aneighbour](#note2a) for his good to [bedification](#note2b).

3 For even Christ pleased not himself; but, as it is written, The [areproaches](#note3a) of them that reproached thee fell on me.

4 For whatsoever things were [awritten](#note4a) aforetime were [bwritten](#note4b) for our [clearning](#note4c), that we through [dpatience](#note4d) and comfort of the [escriptures](#note4e) might have [fhope](#note4f).

5 Now the God of patience and [aconsolation](#note5a) grant you to be [blikeminded](#note5b) one toward another according to Christ Jesus:

6 That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify God, even the [aFather](#note6a) of our Lord Jesus Christ.

7 Wherefore receive ye one another, as Christ also received us to the glory of God.

8 Now I say that Jesus Christ was a minister of the [acircumcision](#note8a) for the truth of God, to [bconfirm](#note8b) the [cpromises](#note8c) made unto the fathers:

9 And that the Gentiles might glorify God for his [amercy](#note9a); as it is written, For this cause I will [bconfess to](#note9b) thee among the [cGentiles](#note9c), and sing unto thy name.

10 And again he saith, Rejoice, ye [aGentiles](#note10a), with his people.

11 And again, Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and laud him, all ye people.

12 And again, [aEsaias](#note12a) saith, There shall be a root of [bJesse](#note12b), and he that shall rise to reign over the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles trust.

13 Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and [apeace](#note13a) in believing, that ye may abound in [bhope](#note13b), through the power of the Holy Ghost.

14 And I myself also am persuaded of you, my brethren, that ye also are full of goodness, filled with all [aknowledge](#note14a), able also to [badmonish](#note14b) one another.

15 Nevertheless, brethren, I have written the more boldly unto you in [asome sort](#note15a), as putting you in mind, because of the [bgrace](#note15b) that is given to me of God,

16 That I should be the [aminister](#note16a) of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, that the [boffering up](#note16b) of the Gentiles might be acceptable, being [csanctified](#note16c) by the Holy Ghost.

17 I have therefore whereof I may [aglory](#note17a) through Jesus Christ in those things which pertain to God.

18 For I will not dare to speak of any of those things which Christ hath not wrought by me, to make the Gentiles obedient, by word and deed,

19 Through mighty [asigns](#note19a) and wonders, by the power of the [bSpirit](#note19b) of God; so that from Jerusalem, and round about unto Illyricum, I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.

20 Yea, so have I strived to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man’s [afoundation](#note20a):

21 But as it is written, To whom he was not [aspoken](#note21a) of, they shall see: and they that have not heard shall understand.

22 For which cause also I have been much [ahindered](#note22a) from coming to you.

23 But now having no more [aplace](#note23a) in these parts, and having a great desire these many years to come unto you;

24 Whensoever I take my journey into Spain, I will come to you: for I trust to see you in my journey, and to be brought on my way thitherward by you, if first I be somewhat filled with your company.

25 But now I go unto Jerusalem to minister unto the saints.

26 For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain [acontribution](#note26a) for the poor saints which are at Jerusalem.

27 It hath pleased them verily; and their debtors they are. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their [aspiritual](#note27a) things, their [bduty](#note27b) is also to minister unto them in [ccarnal](#note27c) things.

28 When therefore I have performed this, and have sealed to them this [afruit](#note28a), I will come by you into Spain.

29 And I am sure that, when I come unto you, I shall come in the [afulness](#note29a) of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.

30 Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Jesus Christ’s sake, and for the love of the Spirit, that ye strive together with me in your prayers to God for me;

31 That I may be delivered from them that [ado not believe](#note31a) in Judæa; and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be accepted of the saints;

32 That I may come unto you with joy by the will of God, and may with you be [arefreshed](#note32a).

33 Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

Chapter 16

Paul sends greetings to various Saints—He counsels the Saints to avoid those who cause divisions—The Saints should be wise concerning good and innocent concerning evil.

1 I commend unto you Phebe our sister, which is a servant of the church which is at Cenchrea:

2 That ye receive her in the Lord, as becometh saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you: for she hath been a succourer of many, and of myself also.

3 Greet Priscilla and Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus:

4 Who have for my life laid down their own necks: unto whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.

5 Likewise greet the church that is in their house. Salute my wellbeloved Epænetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia unto Christ.

6 Greet Mary, who bestowed much labour on us.

7 Salute Andronicus and Junia, my kinsmen, and my fellowprisoners, who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.

8 Greet Amplias my beloved in the Lord.

9 Salute Urbane, our helper in Christ, and Stachys my beloved.

10 Salute Apelles approved in Christ. Salute them which are of Aristobulus’ [ahousehold](#note10a).

11 Salute Herodion my kinsman. Greet them that be of the [ahousehold](#note11a) of Narcissus, which are in the Lord.

12 Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute the beloved Persis, which laboured much in the Lord.

13 Salute Rufus chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.

14 Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren which are with them.

15 Salute Philologus, and Julia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints which are with them.

16 Salute one another with an holy [akiss](#note16a). The [bchurches](#note16b) of Christ salute you.

17 Now I beseech you, brethren, [amark](#note17a) them which cause [bdivisions](#note17b) and [coffences](#note17c) contrary to the [ddoctrine](#note17d) which ye have learned; and [eavoid](#note17e) them.

18 For they that are such serve not our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by good words and [afair](#note18a) speeches deceive the hearts of the [bsimple](#note18b).

19 For your [aobedience](#note19a) is come abroad unto all men. I am glad therefore on your behalf: but yet I would have you [bwise](#note19b) unto that which is good, and [csimple](#note19c) concerning evil.

20 And the God of [apeace](#note20a) shall [bbruise](#note20b) [cSatan](#note20c) under your feet shortly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

21 Timotheus my workfellow, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen, salute you.

22 I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.

23 Gaius mine host, and of the whole church, saluteth you. Erastus the chamberlain of the city saluteth you, and Quartus a brother.

24 The [agrace](#note24a) of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

25 Now to him that is of power to [astablish](#note25a) you according to my gospel, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the [bmystery](#note25b), which was kept secret since the world began,

26 But now is made manifest, and by the scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the [aeverlasting](#note26a) God, made known to all nations for the [bobedience](#note26b) of faith:

27 To God only wise, be glory through Jesus Christ for ever. Amen.

¶ Written to the Romans from Corinthus, and sent by Phebe servant of the church at Cenchrea.

Chapter 1

True Saints are perfectly united in the same mind and in the same judgment—Preach the gospel and save souls—The gospel is preached by the weak and the simple.

1 Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,

2 Unto the church of God which is at Corinth, to them that are [asanctified](#note2a) in Christ Jesus, called to be [bsaints](#note2b), with all that in every place call upon the name of Jesus Christ our Lord, both theirs and ours:

3 Grace be unto you, and [apeace](#note3a), from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

4 I thank my God always on your behalf, for the grace of God which is given you by Jesus Christ;

5 That in every thing ye are enriched by him, in all utterance, and in all knowledge;

6 Even as the [atestimony](#note6a) of Christ was [bconfirmed](#note6b) in you:

7 So that ye [acome behind in](#note7a) no gift; waiting for the [bcoming](#note7b) of our Lord Jesus Christ:

8 Who shall also [aconfirm](#note8a) you unto the end, that ye may be [bblameless](#note8b) in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.

9 God is faithful, by whom ye were called unto the [afellowship](#note9a) of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord.

10 Now I beseech you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no [adivisions](#note10a) among you; but that ye be perfectly joined together in the same [bmind](#note10b) and in the same judgment.

11 For it hath been declared unto me [aof](#note11a) you, my brethren, by them which are of the house of Chloe, that there are [bcontentions](#note11b) among you.

12 Now this I say, that every one of you saith, I am of Paul; and I of Apollos; and I of Cephas; and I of [aChrist](#note12a).

13 Is Christ [adivided](#note13a)? was Paul crucified for you? or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?

14 I thank God that I baptized none of you, [abut](#note14a) [bCrispus](#note14b) and Gaius;

15 Lest any should say that I had baptized in mine own name.

16 And I baptized also the household of [aStephanas](#note16a): besides, I know not whether I baptized any other.

17 For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the gospel: not with [awisdom](#note17a) of words, lest the cross of Christ should be made of none effect.

18 For the [apreaching](#note18a) of the cross is to them that perish foolishness; but unto us which are saved it is the [bpower](#note18b) of God.

19 For it is written, I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and will bring to nothing the [aunderstanding](#note19a) of the [bprudent](#note19b).

20 Where is the wise? where is the [ascribe](#note20a)? where is the [bdisputer](#note20b) of this [cworld](#note20c)? hath not God made foolish the [dwisdom](#note20d) of this [eworld](#note20e)?

21 For after that in the wisdom of God the world [aby](#note21a) wisdom knew not God, it pleased God by the foolishness of [bpreaching](#note21b) to save them that believe.

22 For the Jews require a [asign](#note22a), and the Greeks seek after wisdom:

23 But we [apreach](#note23a) Christ [bcrucified](#note23b), unto the Jews a [cstumblingblock](#note23c), and unto the Greeks foolishness;

24 But unto them [awhich are called](#note24a), both Jews and Greeks, Christ the [bpower](#note24b) of God, and the [cwisdom](#note24c) of God.

25 Because the [afoolishness](#note25a) of God is wiser than men; and the weakness of God is stronger than men.

26 For ye see your calling, brethren, how that not many wise men after the [aflesh](#note26a), not many mighty, not many noble, are [bcalled](#note26b):

27 But God hath chosen the [afoolish](#note27a) things of the world to [bconfound](#note27b) the wise; and God hath chosen the [cweak](#note27c) things of the world to confound the things which are mighty;

28 And base things of the world, and things which are despised, hath God chosen, yea, and things which are not, to bring to nought things that are:

29 That no flesh should glory in his presence.

30 But of him are ye in Christ Jesus, who of God is made unto us wisdom, and righteousness, and [asanctification](#note30a), and redemption:

31 That, according as it is written, He that glorieth, let him [aglory](#note31a) in the Lord.

Chapter 2

The gospel is preached by the power of the Spirit—The Spirit reveals all things to the Saints—The unrepentant natural man cannot receive the things of the Spirit of God.

1 And I, brethren, when I came to you, came not with excellency of speech or of wisdom, declaring unto you the [atestimony](#note1a) of God.

2 For I determined not to know any thing among you, [asave](#note2a) Jesus Christ, and him [bcrucified](#note2b).

3 And I was with you in [aweakness](#note3a), and in fear, and in much trembling.

4 And my [aspeech](#note4a) and my preaching was not with [benticing](#note4b) words of man’s wisdom, but in demonstration of the [cSpirit](#note4c) and of [dpower](#note4d):

5 That your faith should not stand in the wisdom of men, but in the [apower](#note5a) of God.

6 Howbeit we speak wisdom among them that are [aperfect](#note6a): yet not the wisdom of this world, nor of the princes of this world, that come to nought:

7 But we speak the [awisdom](#note7a) of God in a [bmystery](#note7b), even the hidden [cwisdom](#note7c), which God [dordained](#note7d) before the world unto our glory:

8 Which none of the princes of this world knew: for had they known it, they would not have [acrucified](#note8a) the Lord of glory.

9 But as it is written, [aEye](#note9a) hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath [bprepared](#note9b) for them that love him.

10 But God hath [arevealed](#note10a) them unto us by his [bSpirit](#note10b): for the [cSpirit](#note10c) [dsearcheth](#note10d) all things, yea, the deep things of God.

11 For what man [aknoweth](#note11a) the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him? even so the things of God [bknoweth](#note11b) no man, [cbut](#note11c) the [dSpirit](#note11d) of God.

12 Now we have received, not the [aspirit](#note12a) of the world, but the spirit which is of God; that we might know the things that are freely given to us of God.

13 Which things also we speak, not in the words which man’s [awisdom](#note13a) teacheth, but which the Holy Ghost [bteacheth](#note13b); comparing spiritual things with spiritual.

14 But the [anatural man](#note14a) [breceiveth](#note14b) not the things of the [cSpirit](#note14c) of God: for they are [dfoolishness](#note14d) unto him: neither can he [eknow](#note14e) them, because they are [fspiritually](#note14f) [gdiscerned](#note14g).

15 But he that is spiritual [ajudgeth](#note15a) all things, yet he himself is judged of no man.

16 For who hath known the mind of the Lord, that he may [ainstruct](#note16a) him? But we have the [bmind](#note16b) of Christ.

Chapter 3

Milk comes before meat in the Church—Men’s works will be tried by fire—The Saints are the temple of God, and if they are faithful, they will inherit all things.

1 And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ.

2 I have fed you with [amilk](#note2a), and not with meat: for hitherto ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able.

3 For ye are yet [acarnal](#note3a): for whereas there is among you [benvying](#note3b), and [cstrife](#note3c), and [ddivisions](#note3d), are ye not carnal, and walk as men?

4 For while one saith, I am of Paul; and another, I am of Apollos; are ye not carnal?

5 Who then is Paul, and who is Apollos, but [aministers](#note5a) by whom ye believed, even as the Lord gave to every man?

6 I have [aplanted](#note6a), Apollos watered; but God [bgave](#note6b) the [cincrease](#note6c).

7 So then neither is he that planteth any thing, neither he that watereth; but God that giveth the increase.

8 Now he that planteth and he that watereth are one: and every man shall receive his own [areward](#note8a) according to his own labour.

9 For we are [alabourers](#note9a) together with God: ye are God’s [bhusbandry](#note9b), ye are God’s building.

10 According to the [agrace](#note10a) of God which is given unto me, as a wise masterbuilder, I have laid the foundation, and another buildeth thereon. But let every man take heed how he buildeth thereupon.

11 For other [afoundation](#note11a) can no man lay than that is laid, which is Jesus Christ.

12 Now if any man build upon this foundation gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, stubble;

13 Every man’s work shall be made manifest: for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed by fire; and the [afire](#note13a) shall [btry](#note13b) every man’s work of what sort it is.

14 If any man’s work abide which he hath built thereupon, he shall receive a reward.

15 If any man’s work shall be burned, he shall suffer loss: but he himself [ashall](#note15a) be saved; yet so as by fire.

16 Know ye not that ye are the [atemple](#note16a) of God, and that the [bSpirit](#note16b) of God dwelleth [cin](#note16c) you?

17 If any man [adefile](#note17a) the temple of God, him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is [bholy](#note17b), which temple ye are.

18 Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you [aseemeth](#note18a) to be wise in this world, let him become a [bfool](#note18b), that he may be [cwise](#note18c).

19 For the [awisdom](#note19a) of this [bworld](#note19b) is [cfoolishness](#note19c) with God. For it is written, He taketh the wise in their own [dcraftiness](#note19d).

20 And again, The Lord [aknoweth](#note20a) the thoughts of the wise, that they are [bvain](#note20b).

21 Therefore let no man [aglory](#note21a) in men. For all things are yours;

22 Whether [aPaul](#note22a), or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come; [ball](#note22b) are yours;

23 And ye are [aChrist’s](#note23a); and Christ is God’s.

Chapter 4

Christ’s ministers must be faithful—The Apostles suffer, minister, and keep the faith—The kingdom of God is not in word but in power.

1 Let a man so account of us, as of the ministers of Christ, and stewards of the [amysteries](#note1a) of God.

2 Moreover it is required in [astewards](#note2a), that a man be found [bfaithful](#note2b).

3 But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged of you, or of man’s judgment: yea, I judge not mine own self.

4 For I know nothing [aby myself](#note4a); yet am I not hereby justified: but he that [bjudgeth](#note4b) me is the Lord.

5 Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord come, who both will bring to light the [ahidden things](#note5a) of darkness, and will make [bmanifest](#note5b) the counsels of the hearts: and then shall every man have praise of God.

6 And these things, brethren, I have in a figure transferred to myself and to Apollos for your sakes; that ye might learn in us not to think of men above that which is written, that no one of you be [apuffed up for](#note6a) one against another.

7 For who maketh thee [ato differ from](#note7a) another? and what hast thou that thou didst not [breceive](#note7b)? now if thou didst receive it, why dost thou glory, as if thou hadst not received it?

8 Now ye are full, now ye are rich, ye have reigned as kings without us: and I would to God ye did reign, that we also might reign with you.

9 For I think that God hath set forth us the [aapostles last](#note9a), as it were [bappointed](#note9b) to death: for we are made a spectacle unto the world, and to angels, and to men.

10 We are fools for Christ’s sake, but ye are wise in Christ; we are weak, but ye are strong; ye are honourable, but we are despised.

11 Even unto this present hour we both hunger, and thirst, and are naked, and are [abuffeted](#note11a), and have no certain dwellingplace;

12 And [alabour](#note12a), working with our [bown](#note12b) [chands](#note12c): being [dreviled](#note12d), we bless; being [epersecuted](#note12e), we [fsuffer it](#note12f):

13 Being defamed, we entreat: we are made as the filth of the world, and are the offscouring of all things unto this day.

14 I write not these things to [ashame](#note14a) you, but as my beloved [bsons](#note14b) I [cwarn](#note14c) you.

15 For though ye have ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet have ye not many fathers: for in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the [agospel](#note15a).

16 Wherefore I beseech you, be ye [afollowers](#note16a) of me.

17 For this cause have I sent unto you [aTimotheus](#note17a), who is my beloved son, and faithful in the Lord, who shall bring you into remembrance of my ways which be in Christ, as I teach every where in every church.

18 Now some are [apuffed up](#note18a), as though I would not come to you.

19 But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord will, and will know, not the speech of them which are puffed up, but the power.

20 For the kingdom of God is not in word, but in [apower](#note20a).

21 [aWhat will ye](#note21a)? shall I come unto you with a rod, or in love, and in the spirit of [bmeekness](#note21b)?

Chapter 5

The Church cannot fellowship sinners—Christ, our passover, was sacrificed for us.

1 It is reported commonly that there is [afornication](#note1a) among you, and such fornication as is not so much as named among the Gentiles, that one should have his father’s wife.

2 And ye are [apuffed up](#note2a), and have not rather mourned, that he that hath done this deed might be taken away from among you.

3 For I verily, [aas](#note3a) absent in body, but present in spirit, have judged already, as though I were present, concerning him that hath so done this deed,

4 In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when ye are gathered together, [aand my spirit, with](#note4a) the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,

5 To [adeliver](#note5a) such an one unto Satan for the [bdestruction](#note5b) of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the [cday](#note5c) of the Lord Jesus.

6 Your [aglorying](#note6a) is not good. Know ye not that a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump?

7 [aPurge](#note7a) out therefore the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump, as ye are [bunleavened](#note7b). For even Christ our [cpassover](#note7c) is sacrificed for us:

8 Therefore let us keep the [afeast](#note8a), not with old [bleaven](#note8b), neither with the leaven of malice and wickedness; but with the unleavened bread of [csincerity](#note8c) and truth.

9 I wrote unto you in an [aepistle](#note9a) not to [bcompany](#note9b) with [cfornicators](#note9c):

10 Yet not altogether with the fornicators of this world, or with the [acovetous](#note10a), or [bextortioners](#note10b), or with idolaters; for then must ye needs go out of the world.

11 But now I have written unto you not to keep [acompany](#note11a), if any man that is called a brother be a fornicator, or covetous, or an idolater, or a railer, or a [bdrunkard](#note11b), or an extortioner; with such an one [cno not](#note11c) to [deat](#note11d).

12 For what have I to do to judge them also that are [awithout](#note12a)? do not ye [bjudge](#note12b) them that are within?

13 But them that are without God judgeth. Therefore put away from among yourselves that wicked person.

Chapter 6

Church members should not fight one another in the courts—The unrighteous will not be saved—True Saints are the temple of the Holy Ghost.

1 Dare any of you, having a [amatter](#note1a) against another, go to [blaw](#note1b) before the unjust, and not before the saints?

2 Do ye not know that the saints shall [ajudge](#note2a) the [bworld](#note2b)? and if the world shall be judged by you, are ye unworthy to judge the smallest matters?

3 Know ye not that we shall judge angels? how much more things that pertain to this life?

4 If then ye have judgments of things pertaining to this life, set them to judge who are least esteemed in the church.

5 I speak to your [ashame](#note5a). Is it so, that there is not a wise man among you? no, not one that shall be able to judge between his brethren?

6 But brother goeth to law with brother, and that before the unbelievers.

7 Now therefore there is utterly a fault among you, because ye go to law one with another. Why do ye not rather take [awrong](#note7a)? why do ye not rather [bsuffer](#note7b) yourselves to be [cdefrauded](#note7c)?

8 Nay, ye do wrong, and [adefraud](#note8a), and that your brethren.

9 Know ye not that the [aunrighteous](#note9a) shall not [binherit](#note9b) the kingdom of God? Be not deceived: neither [cfornicators](#note9c), nor idolaters, nor [dadulterers](#note9d), nor [eeffeminate](#note9e), nor [fabusers](#note9f) of themselves with mankind,

10 Nor [athieves](#note10a), nor covetous, nor [bdrunkards](#note10b), nor [crevilers](#note10c), nor [dextortioners](#note10d), shall inherit the kingdom of God.

11 And such were some of you: but ye are [awashed](#note11a), but ye are [bsanctified](#note11b), but ye are justified in the [cname](#note11c) of the Lord Jesus, and by the [dSpirit](#note11d) of our God.

12 [aAll](#note12a) things are lawful unto me, but all things are not [bexpedient](#note12b): all things are lawful for me, but I will not be brought under the power of any.

13 Meats for the belly, and the belly for meats: but God shall destroy both it and them. Now the body is not for [afornication](#note13a), but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body.

14 And God hath both raised up the Lord, and will also [araise up](#note14a) us by his own power.

15 Know ye not that your bodies are the [amembers](#note15a) of Christ? shall I then take the members of Christ, and make them the members of an harlot? God forbid.

16 What? know ye not that he which is joined to an harlot is one body? for two, saith he, shall be [aone](#note16a) flesh.

17 But he that is joined unto the Lord is [aone](#note17a) spirit.

18 Flee fornication. Every sin that a man doeth is without the body; but he that committeth fornication sinneth against his own body.

19 What? know ye not that your [abody](#note19a) is the [btemple](#note19b) of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your [cown](#note19c)?

20 For ye are [abought](#note20a) with a price: therefore glorify God in your body, and in your spirit, which are God’s.

Chapter 7

Paul answers special questions about marriage among those called on missions—Paul praises self-discipline.

1 Now concerning the things whereof ye wrote unto me: [aIt is good](#note1a) for a man not to touch a woman.

2 Nevertheless, [ato avoid](#note2a) [bfornication](#note2b), let every man have his own [cwife](#note2c), and let every woman have her own husband.

3 Let the [ahusband](#note3a) render unto the [bwife](#note3b) due [cbenevolence](#note3c): and likewise also the wife unto the husband.

4 The wife hath not power of her own body, but the husband: and likewise also the husband hath not power of his own body, but the wife.

5 [aDefraud](#note5a) ye not one the other, except it be with consent for a time, that ye may give yourselves to fasting and prayer; and come together again, that [bSatan](#note5b) [ctempt](#note5c) you not for your incontinency.

6 But I speak this by permission, and not of commandment.

7 For I would that all men were even as I myself. But every man hath his [aproper](#note7a) [bgift](#note7b) of God, one after this manner, and another after that.

8 I say therefore to the unmarried and [awidows](#note8a), It is good for them if they abide even as I.

9 [aBut](#note9a) if they cannot [bcontain](#note9b), let them marry: for it is better to marry than to [cburn](#note9c).

10 And unto the married I command, yet not I, but the Lord, Let not the [awife](#note10a) [bdepart](#note10b) from her husband:

11 But and if she depart, let her remain unmarried, or be [areconciled](#note11a) to her husband: and let not the husband [bput away](#note11b) his wife.

12 But to the rest speak I, not the Lord: If any brother hath a wife that believeth not, and she be pleased to dwell with him, let him not put her away.

13 And the woman which hath an [ahusband](#note13a) that believeth not, and if he be pleased to dwell with her, let her not leave him.

14 [aFor](#note14a) the unbelieving [bhusband](#note14b) is [csanctified](#note14c) by the wife, and the unbelieving wife is [dsanctified](#note14d) by the husband: else were your children unclean; but now are they holy.

15 But if the unbelieving depart, let him depart. A brother or a sister is not under bondage in such cases: but God hath called us [ato](#note15a) peace.

16 For what knowest thou, O wife, whether thou shalt [asave](#note16a) thy husband? or how knowest thou, O man, whether thou shalt save thy wife?

17 But as [aGod](#note17a) hath distributed to every man, as the Lord hath called every one, so let him walk. And so [bordain](#note17b) I in all churches.

18 Is any man called being circumcised? let him not [abecome uncircumcised](#note18a). Is any called in uncircumcision? let him not be circumcised.

19 [aCircumcision](#note19a) is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but the keeping of the commandments of God.

20 Let every man abide in the same calling wherein he was called.

21 Art thou called being a servant? care not for it: but if thou mayest be made free, use it rather.

22 For he that is called in the Lord, being a servant, is the Lord’s [afreeman](#note22a): likewise also he that is called, being free, is Christ’s [bservant](#note22b).

23 Ye are [abought](#note23a) with a price; be not ye the [bservants](#note23b) of men.

24 Brethren, let every man, wherein he is called, therein abide with God.

25 Now concerning virgins I have no commandment of the Lord: yet I give my judgment, as one that hath obtained [amercy](#note25a) of the Lord to be faithful.

26 I suppose therefore that this is good for the present [adistress](#note26a), I say, that it is good for a man so to be.

27 Art thou bound unto a wife? seek not to be loosed. Art thou loosed from a wife? seek not a wife.

28 But and if thou marry, thou hast not sinned; and if a virgin marry, she hath not sinned. Nevertheless such shall have trouble in the flesh: but I spare you.

29 [aBut](#note29a) this I say, brethren, the time is short: it remaineth, that both they that have wives be as though they had none;

30 And they that weep, as though they wept not; and they that rejoice, as though they rejoiced not; and they that buy, as though they possessed not;

31 And they that use this world, as not abusing it: for the [afashion](#note31a) of this [bworld](#note31b) passeth away.

32 But I would have you without [acarefulness](#note32a). He that is unmarried careth for the things that belong to the Lord, how he may please the Lord:

33 But he that is married careth for the things that are of the world, how he may please his wife.

34 There is difference also between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman careth for the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit: but she that is married careth for the things of the world, how she may please her husband.

35 And this I speak for your own profit; not that I may cast a snare upon you, but for that which is comely, and that ye may attend upon the Lord without distraction.

36 But if any man think that he behaveth himself uncomely toward his virgin, if she pass the flower of her age, and need so require, let him do what he will, he sinneth not: let them marry.

37 Nevertheless he that standeth steadfast in his heart, having no necessity, but hath power over his own will, and hath so decreed in his heart that he will keep his virgin, doeth well.

38 [aSo](#note38a) then he that giveth her in marriage doeth well; but he that giveth her not in marriage doeth better.

39 The [awife](#note39a) is bound by the [blaw](#note39b) as long as her husband liveth; but if her husband be dead, she is at liberty to be married to whom she will; only in the Lord.

40 But she is happier if she so abide, after my judgment: and I think also that I have the Spirit of God.

Chapter 8

There are many gods and many lords—To us there is one God (the Father) and one Lord, who is Christ.

1 Now [aas touching](#note1a) things offered unto [bidols](#note1b), we know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffeth up, but [ccharity](#note1c) [dedifieth](#note1d).

2 And if any man think that he knoweth any thing, he knoweth nothing yet as he ought to [aknow](#note2a).

3 But if any man love God, the same is [aknown](#note3a) of him.

4 As concerning therefore the eating of those things [athat](#note4a) are offered in sacrifice unto [bidols](#note4b), we know that an [cidol](#note4c) is nothing in the world, and that there is none other God but one.

5 For though there be that are called gods, whether in heaven or in earth, (as there be gods many, and lords many,)

6 But to us there is but one [aGod](#note6a), the [bFather](#note6b), of whom are all things, and we in him; and one [cLord Jesus Christ](#note6c), by whom are [dall](#note6d) things, and we by him.

7 Howbeit there is not in every man that knowledge: for some with conscience of the idol unto this hour eat it as a thing offered unto an idol; and their [aconscience](#note7a) being weak is defiled.

8 But [ameat](#note8a) commendeth us not to God: for neither, if we eat, are we the better; neither, if we eat not, are we the worse.

9 But take heed lest by any means this [aliberty](#note9a) of yours become a [bstumblingblock](#note9b) to them that are [cweak](#note9c).

10 For if any man see thee which hast knowledge sit at meat in the idol’s temple, shall not the conscience of him which is weak be emboldened to eat those things which are offered to idols;

11 And through thy knowledge shall the weak brother perish, for whom Christ died?

12 But when ye sin so against the [abrethren](#note12a), and wound their weak conscience, ye sin against Christ.

13 Wherefore, if meat [amake](#note13a) my brother to [boffend](#note13b), I will [ceat no](#note13c) flesh while the world standeth, lest I make my brother to offend.

Chapter 9

Paul rejoices in his Christian liberty—He preaches the gospel to all without charge—He is all things to all men to gain converts.

1 Am I not an [aapostle](#note1a)? am I not free? have I not [bseen](#note1b) Jesus Christ our Lord? are not ye my [cwork](#note1c) in the Lord?

2 If I be not an apostle unto others, yet doubtless I am to you: for the [aseal](#note2a) of mine apostleship are ye in the Lord.

3 Mine [aanswer](#note3a) to them that do [bexamine](#note3b) me is this,

4 Have we not power to eat and to drink?

5 Have we not power to lead about a sister, a [awife](#note5a), as well as other apostles, and as the [bbrethren](#note5b) of the Lord, and Cephas?

6 Or I only and Barnabas, have not we power to forbear working?

7 Who goeth a [awarfare](#note7a) any time at his own charges? who planteth a [bvineyard](#note7b), and [ceateth](#note7c) not of the fruit thereof? or who feedeth a flock, and eateth not of the milk of the flock?

8 Say I these things as a man? or saith not the law the same also?

9 For it is written in the law of Moses, Thou shalt not [amuzzle](#note9a) the mouth of the ox that treadeth out the corn. Doth God take care for oxen?

10 Or saith he it altogether for our sakes? For our sakes, no doubt, this is [awritten](#note10a): that he that ploweth should plow in hope; and that he that thresheth in hope should be [bpartaker](#note10b) of his hope.

11 If we have sown unto you [aspiritual](#note11a) things, is it a great thing if we shall reap your carnal things?

12 If others be partakers of this power over you, are not we rather? Nevertheless we have not used this [apower](#note12a); but suffer all things, lest we should hinder the gospel of Christ.

13 Do ye not know that they which [aminister](#note13a) about holy things [blive](#note13b) of the things of the temple? and they which wait at the altar are partakers with the altar?

14 Even so hath the Lord ordained that they which [apreach](#note14a) the [bgospel](#note14b) should [clive](#note14c) of the gospel.

15 But I have used none of these things: neither have I written these things, that it should be so done unto me: for it were better for me to die, than that any man should make my glorying void.

16 For though I preach the [agospel](#note16a), I have nothing to glory of: for [bnecessity](#note16b) is laid upon me; yea, woe is unto me, if I [cpreach](#note16c) not the gospel!

17 For if I do this thing [awillingly](#note17a), I have a [breward](#note17b): but if against my will, a [cdispensation](#note17c) of the gospel is committed unto me.

18 What is my reward then? Verily that, when I preach the gospel, I may make the gospel of Christ without [acharge](#note18a), that I abuse not my power in the gospel.

19 For though I be free from all men, yet have I made myself [aservant](#note19a) unto all, that I might gain the more.

20 And unto the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain the Jews; to them that are under the law, as under the law, that I might gain them that are under the law;

21 To them that are without law, as without law, (being not without law to God, but under the law to Christ,) that I might gain them that are without law.

22 To the weak became I as [aweak](#note22a), that I might gain the weak: I am made [ball](#note22b) things to all men, that I might by all means save some.

23 And this I do for the gospel’s sake, that I might be partaker thereof with you.

24 Know ye not that they which run in a race run all, but one receiveth the prize? So run, that ye may obtain.

25 And every man that striveth for the [amastery](#note25a) is [btemperate](#note25b) in all things. Now they do it to obtain a corruptible [ccrown](#note25c); but we an [dincorruptible](#note25d).

26 I therefore so run, not as uncertainly; so fight I, not as one that beateth the air:

27 But I [akeep under](#note27a) my [bbody](#note27b), and bring it into subjection: lest that by any means, when I have preached to others, I myself should be a castaway.

Chapter 10

Christ is the God of Israel and the spiritual Rock that guided them—Ancient Israel rebelled against Christ—Paul contrasts true and false sacraments.

1 Moreover, brethren, I would not that ye should be ignorant, how that all our fathers were under the [acloud](#note1a), and all passed through the [bsea](#note1b);

2 And were all [abaptized](#note2a) unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;

3 And did all eat the same spiritual [ameat](#note3a);

4 And did all drink the same spiritual drink: for they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them: and that [aRock](#note4a) was Christ.

5 But with many of them [aGod](#note5a) was not well [bpleased](#note5b): for they were [coverthrown](#note5c) in the [dwilderness](#note5d).

6 Now these things were our [aexamples](#note6a), to the intent we should not [blust after](#note6b) evil things, as they also lusted.

7 Neither be ye idolaters, as were some of them; as it is written, The [apeople](#note7a) sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.

8 Neither let us commit [afornication](#note8a), as some of them committed, and fell in one day three and twenty thousand.

9 Neither let us [atempt](#note9a) Christ, as some of them also tempted, and were destroyed of serpents.

10 Neither [amurmur](#note10a) ye, as some of them also murmured, and were [bdestroyed](#note10b) of the destroyer.

11 Now all these things happened unto them for [aensamples](#note11a): [band](#note11b) they are [cwritten](#note11c) for our [dadmonition](#note11d), upon whom the ends of the world are come.

12 Wherefore let him that thinketh he [astandeth](#note12a) take heed lest he [bfall](#note12b).

13 There hath no temptation [ataken](#note13a) you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be [btempted](#note13b) above that ye are able; but will with the [ctemptation](#note13c) also make a way to [descape](#note13d), that ye may be able to [ebear](#note13e) it.

14 Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from [aidolatry](#note14a).

15 I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.

16 The [acup](#note16a) of [bblessing](#note16b) which we bless, is it not the [ccommunion](#note16c) of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?

17 For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.

18 Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices [apartakers](#note18a) of the altar?

19 What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to [aidols](#note19a) is any thing?

20 But I say, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they [asacrifice](#note20a) to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.

21 Ye cannot drink the [acup](#note21a) of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord’s table, and of the table of devils.

22 Do we provoke the Lord to [ajealousy](#note22a)? are we stronger than he?

23 [aAll](#note23a) things are lawful for me, but all things are not [bexpedient](#note23b): all things are lawful for me, but all things [cedify](#note23c) not.

24 Let no man seek his [aown](#note24a), but every man another’s [bwealth](#note24b).

25 Whatsoever is sold in the [ashambles](#note25a), that eat, asking no question for conscience sake:

26 For the [aearth](#note26a) is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof.

27 If any of them that believe not bid you to a [afeast](#note27a), and ye be disposed to go; whatsoever is set before you, eat, asking no question for conscience sake.

28 But if any man say unto you, This is offered in sacrifice unto idols, eat not for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience sake: for the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof:

29 Conscience, I say, not thine own, but of the other: for why is my liberty judged of another man’s [aconscience](#note29a)?

30 For if I [aby grace](#note30a) be a partaker, why am I evil spoken of for that for which I give thanks?

31 Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, [ado](#note31a) all to the glory of God.

32 Give none [aoffence](#note32a), neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the church of God:

33 Even as I please all men in all things, not [aseeking](#note33a) mine own profit, but the [bprofit](#note33b) of many, that they may be [csaved](#note33c).
