Chapter 11

Paul speaks of certain customs of hair and grooming—Heresies will arise that test and prove the faithful—The sacramental emblems are partaken in remembrance of the flesh and blood of Christ—Beware of partaking unworthily.

1 Be ye [afollowers](#note1a) of me, even as I also am of Christ.

2 Now I praise you, brethren, [athat](#note2a) ye remember me in all things, and keep the [bordinances](#note2b), as I delivered them to you.

3 But I would have you know, that the head of every man is Christ; and the [ahead](#note3a) of the [bwoman](#note3b) is the man; and the [chead](#note3c) of Christ is God.

4 Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonoureth his head.

5 But every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head uncovered dishonoureth her head: for that is [aeven all one as](#note5a) if she were shaven.

6 For if the woman be not covered, let her also be shorn: but if it be a [ashame](#note6a) for a woman to be shorn or shaven, let her be covered.

7 For a man indeed ought not to cover his head, forasmuch as he is the image and glory of God: but the woman is the glory of the man.

8 For the man is not of the woman; but the woman of the man.

9 Neither was the man created for the woman; but the woman for the man.

10 For this cause ought the woman to have power on her head because of the angels.

11 Nevertheless neither is the [aman](#note11a) without the woman, neither the [bwoman](#note11b) without the man, in the Lord.

12 For as the woman is of the man, even so is the man also by the woman; but all things of God.

13 Judge in yourselves: is it [acomely](#note13a) that a woman pray unto God uncovered?

14 Doth not even nature itself teach you, that, if a man have long [ahair](#note14a), it is a shame unto him?

15 But if a woman have long hair, it is a glory to her: for her hair is given her for a covering.

16 But if any man seem to be [acontentious](#note16a), we have no such custom, neither the churches of God.

17 Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.

18 For first of all, when ye come together in the [achurch](#note18a), I hear that there be [bdivisions](#note18b) among you; and I partly believe it.

19 For there must be also [aheresies](#note19a) among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.

20 When ye come together therefore into one place, [athis](#note20a) is not to eat the Lord’s supper.

21 For in eating every one taketh before other his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.

22 What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the [achurch](#note22a) of God, and [bshame](#note22b) them that have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise you not.

23 For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took [abread](#note23a):

24 And when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in [aremembrance](#note24a) of me.

25 After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.

26 For as often as ye eat this [abread](#note26a), and drink this cup, ye do [bshew](#note26b) the Lord’s [cdeath](#note26c) till he come.

27 Wherefore whosoever shall eat this [abread](#note27a), and drink this cup of the Lord, [bunworthily](#note27b), shall [cbe](#note27c) [dguilty](#note27d) of the body and blood of the Lord.

28 But let a man [aexamine](#note28a) himself, and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of that cup.

29 For he that eateth and drinketh [aunworthily](#note29a), eateth and drinketh [bdamnation](#note29b) to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.

30 For this cause many are weak and [asickly](#note30a) among you, and many sleep.

31 For if we would [ajudge](#note31a) ourselves, we should not be judged.

32 But when we are judged, we are [achastened](#note32a) of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.

33 Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.

34 And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.

Chapter 12

The Holy Ghost reveals that Jesus is the Christ—Spiritual gifts are present among the Saints—Apostles, prophets, and miracles are found in the true Church.

1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I would not have you ignorant.

2 Ye know that ye were Gentiles, [acarried away](#note2a) unto these dumb [bidols](#note2b), even as ye were led.

3 Wherefore I give you to understand, that no man speaking by the Spirit of God calleth Jesus accursed: and that no man can [asay](#note3a) that Jesus is the [bLord](#note3b), but by the [cHoly Ghost](#note3c).

4 Now there are diversities of [agifts](#note4a), but the same Spirit.

5 And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.

6 And there are diversities of operations, but it is the same God which worketh all in all.

7 But the manifestation of the [aSpirit](#note7a) is given to every man to profit withal.

8 For to one is [agiven](#note8a) by the [bSpirit](#note8b) the [cword](#note8c) of [dwisdom](#note8d); to another the word of [eknowledge](#note8e) by the same Spirit;

9 To another [afaith](#note9a) by the same Spirit; to another the gifts of [bhealing](#note9b) by the same Spirit;

10 To another the working of [amiracles](#note10a); to another [bprophecy](#note10b); to another [cdiscerning](#note10c) of spirits; to another divers kinds of tongues; to another the interpretation of [dtongues](#note10d):

11 But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.

12 For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.

13 For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one [abody](#note13a), whether we be Jews or [bGentiles](#note13b), whether we be [cbond](#note13c) or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.

14 For the body is not one member, but many.

15 If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?

16 And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?

17 If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?

18 But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.

19 And if they were all one member, where were the body?

20 But now are they many members, yet but one body.

21 And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the [ahead](#note21a) to the feet, I have no need of you.

22 Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be [amore feeble](#note22a), are necessary:

23 And those members of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.

24 For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked:

25 That there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same [acare](#note25a) one for another.

26 And whether one member [asuffer](#note26a), all the members [bsuffer](#note26b) with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.

27 Now ye are the body of [aChrist](#note27a), and [bmembers](#note27b) in particular.

28 And God hath set some in the church, first [aapostles](#note28a), secondarily [bprophets](#note28b), thirdly [cteachers](#note28c), after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, [dgovernments](#note28d), diversities of tongues.

29 Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of [amiracles](#note29a)?

30 Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?

31 But [acovet earnestly](#note31a) the best [bgifts](#note31b): and yet shew I unto you a more [cexcellent](#note31c) way.

Chapter 13

Paul discusses the high status of charity—Charity, a pure love, excels and exceeds almost all else.

1 Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not [acharity](#note1a), I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.

2 And though I have the gift of [aprophecy](#note2a), and understand all [bmysteries](#note2b), and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.

3 And though I bestow all my goods to feed the [apoor](#note3a), and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.

4 [aCharity](#note4a) [bsuffereth](#note4b) long, and is [ckind](#note4c); charity [denvieth](#note4d) not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,

5 Doth not behave itself [aunseemly](#note5a), [bseeketh not](#note5b) her own, is not easily [cprovoked](#note5c), thinketh no evil;

6 Rejoiceth not in [ainiquity](#note6a), but rejoiceth in the [btruth](#note6b);

7 Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.

8 Charity never [afaileth](#note8a): but whether there be prophecies, they shall fail; whether there be tongues, they shall cease; whether there be knowledge, it shall vanish away.

9 For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.

10 But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.

11 When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.

12 For now we see through a [aglass](#note12a), [bdarkly](#note12b); but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.

13 And now abideth [afaith](#note13a), [bhope](#note13b), [ccharity](#note13c), these three; but the greatest of these is charity.

Chapter 14

People should desire spiritual gifts—Tongues and prophecy are compared—Prophecy is the greater gift—Paul says, You may all prophesy; covet to prophesy.

1 [aFollow](#note1a) after charity, and desire spiritual [bgifts](#note1b), but rather that ye may prophesy.

2 For he that speaketh in [aan unknown tongue](#note2a) speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth him; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries.

3 But he that [aprophesieth](#note3a) speaketh unto men to [bedification](#note3b), and exhortation, and comfort.

4 He that speaketh in an unknown tongue edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth edifieth the church.

5 I [awould](#note5a) that ye all spake with tongues, but rather that ye [bprophesied](#note5b): for greater is he that prophesieth than he that speaketh with tongues, except he interpret, that the church may receive edifying.

6 Now, brethren, if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, [aexcept](#note6a) I shall speak to you either by [brevelation](#note6b), or by knowledge, or by prophesying, or by doctrine?

7 And even things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, except they give a distinction in the sounds, how shall it be known what is piped or harped?

8 For if the trumpet give an uncertain sound, who shall prepare himself to the battle?

9 So likewise ye, except ye utter by the tongue words easy to be understood, how shall it be known what is spoken? for ye shall speak into the air.

10 There are, it may be, so many kinds of [avoices](#note10a) in the world, and none of them is without signification.

11 Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voice, I shall be unto him that speaketh a barbarian, and he that speaketh shall be a barbarian unto me.

12 Even so ye, [aforasmuch as](#note12a) ye are zealous of spiritual [bgifts](#note12b), seek that ye may excel to the [cedifying](#note12c) of the church.

13 Wherefore let him that speaketh in an unknown tongue pray that he may interpret.

14 For if I pray in an unknown tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my [aunderstanding](#note14a) is unfruitful.

15 What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also: I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.

16 Else when thou shalt bless with the spirit, how shall he that occupieth the room of the unlearned say Amen at thy giving of thanks, seeing he understandeth not what thou sayest?

17 For thou verily givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.

18 I thank my God, I speak with tongues more than ye all:

19 Yet in the church I had rather [aspeak](#note19a) five words with my understanding, that by my voice I might teach others also, than ten thousand words in an unknown tongue.

20 Brethren, be not [achildren](#note20a) in [bunderstanding](#note20b): howbeit in [cmalice](#note20c) be ye children, but in [dunderstanding](#note20d) be men.

21 In the law it is written, With men of other tongues and other lips will I speak unto this people; and yet for all that will they not [ahear](#note21a) me, saith the Lord.

22 Wherefore [atongues](#note22a) are for a [bsign](#note22b), not to them that believe, but to them that believe not: but [cprophesying](#note22c) serveth not for them that believe not, but for them which believe.

23 If therefore the whole church be come together into one place, and all speak with tongues, and there come in those that are unlearned, or [aunbelievers](#note23a), will they not say that ye are mad?

24 But if all prophesy, and there come in one that believeth not, or one unlearned, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all:

25 And thus are the [asecrets](#note25a) of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on his face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.

26 How is it then, brethren? when ye come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a tongue, hath a revelation, hath an interpretation. Let all things be done unto [aedifying](#note26a).

27 If any man speak in an unknown tongue, let it be by two, or at the most by three, and that by course; and let one interpret.

28 But if there be no interpreter, let him keep [asilence](#note28a) in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God.

29 Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the other judge.

30 If any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.

31 For ye may all prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be comforted.

32 And the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets.

33 For God is not the [aauthor](#note33a) of [bconfusion](#note33b), but of [cpeace](#note33c), as in all [dchurches](#note33d) of the [esaints](#note33e).

34 Let your [awomen](#note34a) keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to [bspeak](#note34b); but they are commanded [cto](#note34c) be under [dobedience](#note34d), as also saith the law.

35 And if they will learn any thing, let them ask their husbands at home: for it is a shame for women to [aspeak](#note35a) in the church.

36 What? came the word of God out from you? or came it unto you only?

37 If any man think himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him acknowledge that the things that I write unto you are the commandments of the Lord.

38 But if any man be ignorant, let him be ignorant.

39 Wherefore, brethren, [acovet](#note39a) to prophesy, and forbid not to speak with tongues.

40 Let all things be done decently and in [aorder](#note40a).

Chapter 15

Christ died for our sins—He rose from the dead and was seen by many—All men will be resurrected—Paul speaks of baptism for the dead—The three degrees of glory are described—Victory over death comes through Christ.

1 Moreover, brethren, I [adeclare](#note1a) unto you the [bgospel](#note1b) which I preached unto you, which also ye have received, and wherein ye stand;

2 By which also ye are [asaved](#note2a), if ye [bkeep in memory](#note2b) what I preached unto you, unless ye have believed in vain.

3 For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received, how that Christ [adied](#note3a) for our [bsins](#note3b) according to the scriptures;

4 And that he was buried, and that he [arose](#note4a) again the third day according to the scriptures:

5 And that he was [aseen](#note5a) of [bCephas](#note5b), then of the twelve:

6 After that, he was [aseen](#note6a) of [babove](#note6b) five hundred brethren at once; of whom the greater part remain unto this present, but some are fallen asleep.

7 After that, he was seen of James; then of all the apostles.

8 And last of all he was [aseen](#note8a) of me also, as of one born out of due time.

9 For I am the least of the apostles, that am not [ameet](#note9a) to be called an apostle, because I [bpersecuted](#note9b) the church of God.

10 But by the [agrace](#note10a) of God I am what I am: and his grace which was bestowed upon me was not in vain; but I [blaboured](#note10b) more abundantly than they all: yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.

11 Therefore whether it were I or they, so we preach, and so ye believed.

12 Now if Christ be preached that he rose from the dead, how say some among you that there is no resurrection of the dead?

13 But if there be no resurrection of the dead, then is Christ not risen:

14 And if Christ be not risen, then is our preaching vain, and your faith is also vain.

15 Yea, and we are found false witnesses of God; because we have [atestified](#note15a) of God that he raised up Christ: whom he raised not up, if so be that the dead rise not.

16 For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ raised:

17 And if Christ be not raised, your faith is [avain](#note17a); ye are yet in your sins.

18 Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are [aperished](#note18a).

19 If in this [alife](#note19a) only we have [bhope](#note19b) in Christ, we are of all men most miserable.

20 But now is [aChrist](#note20a) [brisen](#note20b) from the dead, and become the [cfirstfruits](#note20c) of them that slept.

21 For since by man came [adeath](#note21a), by man came also the [bresurrection](#note21b) of the dead.

22 For as in [aAdam](#note22a) all [bdie](#note22b), even so in [cChrist](#note22c) shall all be made [dalive](#note22d).

23 But every man in his own [aorder](#note23a): Christ the [bfirstfruits](#note23b); afterward they that are Christ’s at his [ccoming](#note23c).

24 Then cometh the end, when he shall have delivered up the kingdom to God, even the Father; when he shall have [aput down](#note24a) all [brule](#note24b) and all [cauthority](#note24c) and [dpower](#note24d).

25 For he must [areign](#note25a), till he hath put all [benemies](#note25b) under his [cfeet](#note25c).

26 The last [aenemy](#note26a) that shall be [bdestroyed](#note26b) is [cdeath](#note26c).

27 For he hath put all things under his feet. But when he saith all things are put under him, it is [amanifest](#note27a) that he is excepted, which did put all things under him.

28 And when all things shall be [asubdued](#note28a) unto him, then shall the Son also himself be [bsubject](#note28b) unto him that put all things under him, that God may be all in all.

29 Else what shall they do which are [abaptized](#note29a) [bfor](#note29b) the dead, if the dead [crise](#note29c) not at all? why are they then baptized for the [ddead](#note29d)?

30 And why stand we in [ajeopardy](#note30a) every hour?

31 [aI](#note31a) protest by your rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.

32 If after the manner of men I have fought with beasts at Ephesus, what advantageth it me, if the dead rise not? let us [aeat](#note32a) and drink; for [bto morrow](#note32b) we die.

33 Be not deceived: evil [acommunications](#note33a) corrupt good manners.

34 Awake to righteousness, and sin not; for some have not the knowledge of God: I speak this to your [ashame](#note34a).

35 But some man will say, How are the dead raised up? and with what body do they come?

36 Thou fool, that which thou sowest is not quickened, except it [adie](#note36a):

37 And that which thou sowest, thou sowest not that body that shall be, but bare grain, [ait may chance](#note37a) of wheat, or of some other grain:

38 But God giveth it a body as it hath pleased him, and to every seed his own body.

39 All flesh is not the same flesh: but there is one kind of flesh of men, another flesh of beasts, another of fishes, and another of birds.

40 [aThere](#note40a) are also celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial: but the glory of the [bcelestial](#note40b) is one, and the glory of the [cterrestrial](#note40c) is another.

41 There is one glory of the [asun](#note41a), and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the [bstars](#note41b): for one star [cdiffereth](#note41c) from another star in [dglory](#note41d).

42 So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in [acorruption](#note42a); it is raised in incorruption:

43 It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in [aglory](#note43a): it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:

44 It is sown a [anatural](#note44a) body; it is raised a [bspiritual body](#note44b). There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.

45 And so it is written, The [afirst](#note45a) man [bAdam](#note45b) was made a living soul; the last [cAdam](#note45c) was made a [dquickening](#note45d) spirit.

46 Howbeit that was not [afirst](#note46a) which is spiritual, but that which is natural; and afterward that which is spiritual.

47 The first man is of the earth, earthy: the second man is the Lord from heaven.

48 As is the earthy, such are they also that are earthy: and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.

49 And as we have borne the image of the earthy, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly.

50 Now this I say, brethren, that [aflesh](#note50a) and [bblood](#note50b) cannot [cinherit](#note50c) the kingdom of God; neither doth [dcorruption](#note50d) inherit incorruption.

51 Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all [asleep](#note51a), but we shall all be [bchanged](#note51b),

52 In a moment, in the [atwinkling](#note52a) of an eye, at the last trump: for the [btrumpet](#note52b) shall sound, and the [cdead](#note52c) shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be [dchanged](#note52d).

53 For this [acorruptible](#note53a) must put on incorruption, and this [bmortal](#note53b) must put on immortality.

54 So when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on [aimmortality](#note54a), then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, [bDeath](#note54b) is swallowed up in victory.

55 O [adeath](#note55a), where is thy sting? O [bgrave](#note55b), where is thy victory?

56 The sting of [adeath](#note56a) is sin; and the [bstrength](#note56b) of sin is the law.

57 But [athanks](#note57a) be to God, which giveth us the [bvictory](#note57b) through our Lord Jesus Christ.

58 Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye [asteadfast](#note58a), unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.

Chapter 16

Paul counsels, Stand fast in the faith; let all things be done with charity.

1 Now concerning the [acollection](#note1a) for the saints, as I have [bgiven order to](#note1b) the churches of Galatia, even so do ye.

2 Upon the [afirst](#note2a) day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath [bprospered](#note2b) him, that there be no gatherings when I come.

3 And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by your letters, them will I send to bring your [aliberality](#note3a) unto Jerusalem.

4 And if it be [ameet](#note4a) that I go also, they shall go with me.

5 Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.

6 And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.

7 For I will not see you now [aby the way](#note7a); but I [btrust](#note7b) to [ctarry](#note7c) a while with you, if the Lord permit.

8 But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.

9 For a great [adoor](#note9a) and [beffectual](#note9b) is opened unto me, and there are many adversaries.

10 Now if [aTimotheus](#note10a) come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also do.

11 Let no man therefore [adespise](#note11a) him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.

12 As touching our brother [aApollos](#note12a), I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient time.

13 [aWatch](#note13a) ye, [bstand fast](#note13b) in the [cfaith](#note13c), [dquit](#note13d) you like [emen](#note13e), be strong.

14 Let all your things be done with [acharity](#note14a).

15 I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of [aStephanas](#note15a), that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have [baddicted](#note15b) themselves to the ministry of the saints,)

16 That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with us, and laboureth.

17 I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was [alacking](#note17a) on your part they have supplied.

18 For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.

19 The churches of Asia salute you. [aAquila](#note19a) and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.

20 All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy [akiss](#note20a).

21 The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand.

22 If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema [aMaran-atha](#note22a).

23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

24 My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

¶ The first epistle to the Corinthians was written from Philippi by Stephanas, and Fortunatus, and Achaicus, and Timotheus.

Chapter 1

God comforts and cares for His Saints—The Saints are sealed and given assurance by the Spirit in their hearts.

1 Paul, an [aapostle](#note1a) of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, unto the church of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints which are in all Achaia:

2 Grace be to you and peace from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 Blessed be God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of [amercies](#note3a), and the God of all [bcomfort](#note3b);

4 Who comforteth us in all our [atribulation](#note4a), that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the [bcomfort](#note4b) wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.

5 For as the [asufferings](#note5a) of Christ abound in us, so our consolation also aboundeth by Christ.

6 And whether we be afflicted, it is for your consolation and salvation, which is [aeffectual](#note6a) in the [benduring](#note6b) of the same sufferings which we also suffer: or whether we be comforted, it is for your consolation and salvation.

7 And our hope of you is steadfast, knowing, that as ye are partakers of the [asufferings](#note7a), so shall ye be also of the consolation.

8 For we would not, brethren, have you ignorant of our trouble which came to us in Asia, that we were pressed out of measure, above strength, insomuch that we [adespaired](#note8a) even of life:

9 But we had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God which [araiseth](#note9a) the dead:

10 Who [adelivered](#note10a) us from so great a death, and doth deliver: in whom we trust that he will yet deliver us;

11 Ye also helping together by [aprayer](#note11a) for us, that for the gift bestowed upon us by the means of many persons thanks may be given by many on our behalf.

12 For our rejoicing is this, the [atestimony](#note12a) of our conscience, that in [bsimplicity](#note12b) and godly sincerity, not with fleshly [cwisdom](#note12c), but by the grace of God, we have had our conversation in the world, and more abundantly to you-ward.

13 For we [awrite](#note13a) none other things unto you, than what ye read or acknowledge; and I trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end;

14 As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also are ours in the [aday](#note14a) of the Lord Jesus.

15 And in this confidence I was minded to [acome](#note15a) unto you before, that ye might have a second benefit;

16 And to pass by you into Macedonia, and to come again out of Macedonia unto you, and of you to be brought on my way toward Judæa.

17 When I therefore was thus minded, did I use [alightness](#note17a)? or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be yea yea, and nay nay?

18 But as God is true, our word toward you was not yea and nay.

19 For the [aSon of God](#note19a), Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, even by me and [bSilvanus](#note19b) and [cTimotheus](#note19c), was not yea and nay, but in him was yea.

20 For all the [apromises](#note20a) of God in him are yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us.

21 Now he which stablisheth us with you in Christ, and hath [aanointed](#note21a) us, is God;

22 Who hath also [asealed](#note22a) us, and given the earnest of the Spirit in our hearts.

23 Moreover I call God for a record upon my soul, that to spare you I came not as yet unto Corinth.

24 Not for that we have dominion over your faith, but are helpers of your joy: for by [afaith](#note24a) ye stand.

Chapter 2

Saints should love and forgive one another—They always triumph in Christ.

1 But I determined this with myself, that I would not come again to you in heaviness.

2 For if I make you sorry, who is he then that maketh me glad, but the same which is made sorry by me?

3 And I wrote this same unto you, lest, when I came, I should have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having [aconfidence](#note3a) in you all, that my joy is the joy of you all.

4 For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote unto you with many tears; not that ye should be grieved, but that ye might know the love which I have more abundantly unto you.

5 But if any have caused grief, he hath not grieved me, but in part: that I may not overcharge you all.

6 Sufficient to such a man is this punishment, which was inflicted of many.

7 So that contrariwise ye ought rather to [aforgive](#note7a) him, and comfort him, lest perhaps such a one should be swallowed up with overmuch sorrow.

8 Wherefore I beseech you that ye would confirm your love toward him.

9 For to this end also did I [awrite](#note9a), that I might know the proof of you, whether ye be [bobedient](#note9b) in all things.

10 To whom ye forgive any thing, I forgive also: for if I forgave any thing, to whom I forgave it, for your sakes forgave I it in the [aperson](#note10a) of Christ;

11 Lest [aSatan](#note11a) should get an advantage of us: for we are not ignorant of his devices.

12 Furthermore, when I came to Troas to preach Christ’s gospel, and a [adoor](#note12a) was opened unto me of the Lord,

13 I had no rest in my spirit, because I found not Titus my brother: but taking my leave of them, I went from thence into Macedonia.

14 Now thanks be unto God, which always causeth us to [atriumph](#note14a) in Christ, and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place.

15 For we are unto God a sweet savour of Christ, in them that are saved, and in them that perish:

16 To the one we are the savour of death unto death; and to the other the savour of life unto life. And who is [asufficient](#note16a) for these things?

17 For we are not as many, which [acorrupt](#note17a) the [bword](#note17b) of God: but as of [csincerity](#note17c), but as of God, in the sight of God speak we in Christ.

Chapter 3

The gospel surpasses the law of Moses—Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.

1 Do we begin again to commend ourselves? or need we, as some others, epistles of commendation to you, or letters of commendation from you?

2 Ye are our [aepistle](#note2a) written in our hearts, known and read of all men:

3 Forasmuch as ye are manifestly declared to be the epistle of Christ ministered by us, [awritten](#note3a) not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in [btables](#note3b) of stone, but in [cfleshy](#note3c) tables of the [dheart](#note3d).

4 And such trust have we through Christ to God-ward:

5 Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves; but our [asufficiency](#note5a) is of God;

6 Who also hath made us able [aministers](#note6a) of the new testament; not of the letter, but of the spirit: for the letter killeth, but the [bspirit](#note6b) giveth life.

7 But if the ministration of [adeath](#note7a), written and engraven in stones, was glorious, so that the children of Israel could not steadfastly behold the face of Moses for the [bglory](#note7b) of his countenance; which glory was to be done away:

8 How shall not the ministration of the spirit be rather glorious?

9 For if the ministration of condemnation be glory, much more doth the ministration of righteousness exceed in glory.

10 For even that which was made glorious had no glory in this respect, by reason of the glory that excelleth.

11 For if that which is done away was glorious, much more that which remaineth is glorious.

12 Seeing then that we have such hope, we use great [aplainness](#note12a) of speech:

13 And not as Moses, which put a veil over his face, that the children of Israel could not steadfastly look to the end of that which is abolished:

14 But their [aminds](#note14a) were [bblinded](#note14b): for until this day remaineth the same veil untaken away in the [creading](#note14c) of the old testament; which [dveil](#note14d) is done away in Christ.

15 But even unto this day, when Moses is read, the veil is upon their [aheart](#note15a).

16 Nevertheless when it shall turn to the Lord, the veil shall be taken away.

17 Now the Lord is [athat Spirit](#note17a): and where the [bSpirit](#note17b) of the Lord is, there is [cliberty](#note17c).

18 But we all, with open face beholding as in a [aglass](#note18a) the [bglory](#note18b) of the Lord, are changed into the same [cimage](#note18c) from [dglory](#note18d) to glory, even as by the Spirit of the Lord.

Chapter 4

Gospel light shines on the Saints—Mortal trials are nothing as contrasted with eternal glory.

1 Therefore seeing we have this ministry, as we have received [amercy](#note1a), we faint not;

2 But have [arenounced](#note2a) the hidden things of [bdishonesty](#note2b), not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God [cdeceitfully](#note2c); but by manifestation of the [dtruth](#note2d) commending ourselves to every man’s [econscience](#note2e) in the sight of God.

3 But if our gospel be hid, it is hid to them that are lost:

4 In whom the [agod](#note4a) of this [bworld](#note4b) hath [cblinded](#note4c) the [dminds](#note4d) of them which [ebelieve not](#note4e), lest the [flight](#note4f) of the glorious gospel of Christ, who is the [gimage](#note4g) of [hGod](#note4h), should shine unto them.

5 For we [apreach](#note5a) not ourselves, but Christ Jesus the Lord; and ourselves your [bservants](#note5b) for Jesus’ sake.

6 For God, who [acommanded](#note6a) the light to shine out of darkness, hath [bshined](#note6b) in our hearts, to give the [clight](#note6c) of the knowledge of the [dglory](#note6d) of God in the face of Jesus Christ.

7 But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the [apower](#note7a) may be of God, and not of us.

8 We are [atroubled](#note8a) on every side, yet not distressed; we are perplexed, but not in [bdespair](#note8b);

9 [aPersecuted](#note9a), but not forsaken; cast down, but not [bdestroyed](#note9b);

10 Always bearing about in the body the dying of the Lord Jesus, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our body.

11 For we which live are alway delivered unto [adeath](#note11a) for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our [bmortal](#note11b) flesh.

12 So then death worketh in us, but life in you.

13 We having the same spirit of faith, according as it is written, I [abelieved](#note13a), and therefore have I spoken; we also believe, and therefore [bspeak](#note13b);

14 Knowing that he which raised up the Lord Jesus shall raise up us also by Jesus, and shall present us with you.

15 For all things are for your sakes, that the abundant grace might through the [athanksgiving](#note15a) of many redound to the glory of God.

16 For which cause we faint not; but though our outward man perish, yet the [ainward](#note16a) man is renewed day by day.

17 For our light [aaffliction](#note17a), which is but for a moment, worketh for us a far more exceeding and [beternal](#note17b) [cweight](#note17c) of glory;

18 While we look not at the things which are [aseen](#note18a), but at the things which are not seen: for the things which are seen are temporal; but the things which are not [bseen](#note18b) are [ceternal](#note18c).

Chapter 5

Saints walk by faith and seek tabernacles of immortal glory—The gospel reconciles man to God—God’s ministers carry the word of reconciliation to the world.

1 For we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.

2 For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be [aclothed](#note2a) upon with our house which is from heaven:

3 If so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.

4 For we that are in this tabernacle do [agroan](#note4a), being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that [bmortality](#note4b) might be swallowed up of life.

5 Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.

6 Therefore we are always [aconfident](#note6a), knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:

7 (For we walk by [afaith](#note7a), not by [bsight](#note7b):)

8 We are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.

9 Wherefore we [alabour](#note9a), that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.

10 For we must all appear before the [ajudgment](#note10a) seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, [baccording](#note10b) to that he hath [cdone](#note10c), whether it be good or bad.

11 Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are made manifest unto God; and I trust also are made manifest in your [aconsciences](#note11a).

12 For we commend not ourselves again unto you, but give you occasion to glory on our behalf, that ye may have somewhat to answer them which glory in appearance, and not in heart.

13 [aFor](#note13a) whether we be beside ourselves, it is to God: or whether we be sober, it is for your cause.

14 For the love of Christ constraineth us; because we thus judge, that if [aone](#note14a) [bdied](#note14b) for all, then were all dead:

15 And that he died for all, that they which live should not henceforth [alive](#note15a) unto themselves, but unto him which died for them, and rose again.

16 [aWherefore](#note16a) henceforth know we no man after the flesh: yea, though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we him no more.

17 Therefore if any man be [ain Christ](#note17a), he is a [bnew](#note17b) creature: [cold](#note17c) things are [dpassed](#note17d) away; behold, all things are become [enew](#note17e).

18 And all things are of God, who hath [areconciled](#note18a) us to himself by Jesus Christ, and hath given to us the ministry of reconciliation;

19 To wit, that God was in Christ, [areconciling](#note19a) the world unto himself, not imputing their trespasses unto them; and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation.

20 Now then we are [aambassadors](#note20a) for Christ, as though God did beseech you by us: we pray you in Christ’s stead, be ye reconciled to God.

21 For he hath made him to be [asin](#note21a) for us, who knew no [bsin](#note21b); that we might be made the [crighteousness](#note21c) of God in him.

Chapter 6

Now is the day of salvation—God’s ministers must walk uprightly and bear all things—Saints should not be unequally yoked with unbelievers.

1 We then, as [aworkers](#note1a) together with [bhim](#note1b), beseech you also that ye receive not the [cgrace](#note1c) of God in vain.

2 (For he saith, I have heard thee in a [atime](#note2a) accepted, and in the [bday](#note2b) of [csalvation](#note2c) have I succoured thee: behold, now is the accepted time; behold, now is the day of salvation.)

3 Giving no [aoffence](#note3a) in any thing, that the ministry be not blamed:

4 But in all things approving ourselves as the [aministers](#note4a) of God, in much [bpatience](#note4b), in [cafflictions](#note4c), in necessities, in distresses,

5 In stripes, in imprisonments, in [atumults](#note5a), in labours, in watchings, in fastings;

6 By [apureness](#note6a), by knowledge, by [blongsuffering](#note6b), by [ckindness](#note6c), by the Holy Ghost, by [dlove](#note6d) unfeigned,

7 By the word of truth, by the power of God, by the [aarmour](#note7a) of [brighteousness](#note7b) on the right hand and on the left,

8 By honour and dishonour, by evil report and good report: as deceivers, and yet true;

9 As unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and, behold, we live; as chastened, and not killed;

10 As [asorrowful](#note10a), yet alway rejoicing; as [bpoor](#note10b), yet making many [crich](#note10c); as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.

11 O ye Corinthians, our mouth is open unto you, our heart is [aenlarged](#note11a).

12 Ye are not straitened in us, but ye are straitened in your own [abowels](#note12a).

13 Now for a recompence in the same, (I speak as unto my children,) be ye also enlarged.

14 Be ye not unequally [ayoked](#note14a) together with unbelievers: for what [bfellowship](#note14b) hath righteousness with [cunrighteousness](#note14c)? and what communion hath [dlight](#note14d) with darkness?

15 And what concord hath Christ with Belial? or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel?

16 And what [aagreement](#note16a) hath the temple of God with idols? for ye are the [btemple](#note16b) of the living God; as God hath said, I will [cdwell](#note16c) in them, and walk in them; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.

17 Wherefore [acome](#note17a) out from among them, and be ye [bseparate](#note17b), saith the Lord, and touch not the [cunclean thing](#note17c); and I will receive you,

18 And will be a [aFather](#note18a) unto you, and ye shall be my [bsons](#note18b) and daughters, saith the Lord [cAlmighty](#note18c).

Chapter 7

Godly sorrow for sin leads to repentance—The sorrow of the world leads to death.

1 Having therefore these promises, dearly beloved, let us [acleanse](#note1a) ourselves from all [bfilthiness](#note1b) of the flesh and spirit, perfecting [choliness](#note1c) in the fear of God.

2 Receive us; we have [awronged](#note2a) no man, we have corrupted no man, we have [bdefrauded](#note2b) no man.

3 I speak not this to condemn you: for I have said before, that ye are in our hearts to die and live with you.

4 Great is my boldness of speech toward you, great is my glorying of you: I am filled with [acomfort](#note4a), I am exceeding [bjoyful](#note4b) in all our [ctribulation](#note4c).

5 For, when we were come into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we were [atroubled](#note5a) on every side; without were fightings, within were [bfears](#note5b).

6 Nevertheless God, that comforteth those that are cast down, comforted us by the coming of Titus;

7 And not by his coming only, but by the consolation wherewith he was [acomforted](#note7a) in you, when he told us your earnest desire, your mourning, your fervent mind toward me; so that I rejoiced the more.

8 For though I made you sorry with a letter, I do not repent, though I did repent: for I perceive that the same epistle hath made you sorry, though it were but for a season.

9 Now I rejoice, not that ye were made sorry, but that ye sorrowed to repentance: for ye were made [asorry](#note9a) after a godly manner, that ye might receive damage by us in nothing.

10 For [agodly](#note10a) [bsorrow](#note10b) worketh [crepentance](#note10c) to [dsalvation](#note10d) not to be repented of: but the [esorrow](#note10e) of the world worketh death.

11 For behold this selfsame thing, that ye sorrowed after a godly sort, what carefulness it wrought in you, yea, what clearing of yourselves, yea, what indignation, yea, what fear, yea, what vehement desire, yea, what zeal, yea, what revenge! In all things ye have approved yourselves to be clear in this matter.

12 Wherefore, though I wrote unto you, I did it not for his cause that had done the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered wrong, but that our care for you in the sight of God might appear unto you.

13 Therefore we were comforted in your comfort: yea, and exceedingly the more joyed we for the joy of Titus, because his spirit was [arefreshed](#note13a) by you all.

14 For if I have [aboasted](#note14a) any thing to him of you, I am not ashamed; but as we spake all things to you in truth, even so our [bboasting](#note14b), which I made before Titus, is found a truth.

15 And his [ainward affection](#note15a) is more abundant toward you, whilst he remembereth the [bobedience](#note15b) of you all, how with fear and trembling ye received him.

16 I rejoice therefore that I have confidence in you in all things.

Chapter 8

True Saints impart of their substance to the poor—Christ, out of His poverty, brought eternal riches.

1 Moreover, brethren, [awe](#note1a) do you to wit of the grace of God bestowed on the churches of Macedonia;

2 How that in a great [atrial](#note2a) of [baffliction](#note2b) the abundance of their joy and their deep [cpoverty](#note2c) abounded unto the riches of their [dliberality](#note2d).

3 For to their power, I bear record, yea, and beyond their power they were willing of themselves;

4 Praying us with much entreaty that we would receive the gift, and take upon us the [afellowship](#note4a) of the ministering to the saints.

5 And this they did, not as we hoped, but first [agave](#note5a) their own selves to the Lord, and unto us by the will of God.

6 Insomuch that we desired Titus, that as he had begun, so he would also finish in you the same grace also.

7 Therefore, as ye abound in every thing, in faith, and utterance, and knowledge, and in all [adiligence](#note7a), and in your love to us, see that ye abound in this grace also.

8 I speak [anot](#note8a) by commandment, but by occasion of the forwardness of others, and to prove the [bsincerity](#note8b) of your love.

9 For ye know the [agrace](#note9a) of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was [brich](#note9b), yet for your sakes he became [cpoor](#note9c), that ye through his [dpoverty](#note9d) might be rich.

10 And herein I give my advice: for this is expedient for you, who have begun before, not only to do, but also to be forward a year ago.

11 Now therefore perform the doing of it; that as there was a readiness to will, so there may be a performance also out of that which ye have.

12 For if there be first a [awilling](#note12a) [bmind](#note12b), it is accepted according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not.

13 For I mean not that other men be eased, and ye burdened:

14 But by an equality, that now at this time your abundance may be a supply for their want, that their [aabundance](#note14a) also may be a supply for your want: that there may be [bequality](#note14b):

15 As it is written, He that had [agathered](#note15a) much had nothing over; and he that had gathered little had no lack.

16 But thanks be to God, which put the same earnest care into the heart of Titus for you.

17 For indeed he accepted the exhortation; but being more forward, of his own accord he went unto you.

18 And we have sent with him the brother, whose praise is in the gospel throughout all the churches;

19 And not that only, but who was also chosen of the churches to travel with us with this grace, which is administered by us to the glory of the same Lord, and declaration of your ready mind:

20 Avoiding this, that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us:

21 Providing for [ahonest](#note21a) things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the [bsight](#note21b) of men.

22 And we have sent with them our brother, whom we have oftentimes proved diligent in many things, but now much more diligent, upon the great [aconfidence](#note22a) which I have in you.

23 Whether any do inquire of Titus, he is my partner and fellowhelper concerning you: or our brethren be inquired of, they are the messengers of the churches, and the glory of Christ.

24 Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the churches, the proof of your love, and of our boasting on your behalf.

Chapter 9

God loves and rewards a cheerful giver—Thanks be to God for His unspeakable gift.

1 For as touching the ministering to the saints, it is superfluous for me to write to you:

2 For I know the forwardness of your mind, for which I [aboast](#note2a) of you to them of Macedonia, that Achaia was ready a year ago; and your zeal hath [bprovoked](#note2b) very many.

3 Yet have I sent the brethren, lest our boasting of you should be in vain in this behalf; that, as I said, ye may be ready:

4 Lest haply if they of Macedonia come with me, and find you unprepared, we (that we say not, ye) should be ashamed in this same confident boasting.

5 Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren, that they would go before unto you, and make up beforehand your [abounty](#note5a), whereof ye had notice before, that the same might be ready, as a matter of bounty, and not as of covetousness.

6 But this I say, He which soweth sparingly shall [areap](#note6a) also sparingly; and he which [bsoweth](#note6b) bountifully shall reap also bountifully.

7 Every man according as he [apurposeth](#note7a) in his heart, so let him give; not [bgrudgingly](#note7b), or of necessity: for God loveth a [ccheerful](#note7c) [dgiver](#note7d).

8 And God is able to make all [agrace](#note8a) abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work:

9 (As it is written, He hath dispersed abroad; he hath given to the poor: his righteousness remaineth for ever.

10 Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food, and multiply your seed sown, and increase the fruits of your [arighteousness](#note10a);)

11 Being enriched in every thing to all bountifulness, which causeth through us [athanksgiving](#note11a) to God.

12 For the [aadministration](#note12a) of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints, but is abundant also by many thanksgivings unto God;

13 Whiles by the experiment of this ministration they glorify God for your professed subjection unto the gospel of Christ, and for your liberal distribution unto them, and unto all men;

14 And by their prayer for you, which long after you for the exceeding grace of God in you.

15 [aThanks](#note15a) be unto God for his unspeakable [bgift](#note15b).

Chapter 10

Bring every thought into obedience—Paul glories in the Lord.

1 Now I Paul myself beseech you by the [ameekness](#note1a) and gentleness of Christ, who in presence am base among you, but being absent am bold toward you:

2 But I beseech you, that I may not be bold when I am present with that confidence, wherewith I think to be bold against some, which think of us as if we walked according to the flesh.

3 For though we walk in the flesh, we do not [awar](#note3a) after the flesh:

4 (For the [aweapons](#note4a) of our [bwarfare](#note4b) are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds;)

5 Casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every [athought](#note5a) to the [bobedience](#note5b) of Christ;

6 And having in a readiness to revenge all [adisobedience](#note6a), when your [bobedience](#note6b) is fulfilled.

7 Do ye look on things after the outward [aappearance](#note7a)? If any man trust to himself that he is Christ’s, let him of himself think this again, that, as he is Christ’s, even so are we [bChrist’s](#note7b).

8 For though I should boast somewhat more of our [aauthority](#note8a), which the Lord hath given us for [bedification](#note8b), and not for your destruction, I should not be ashamed:

9 That I may not seem as if I would terrify you by letters.

10 For his letters, say they, are weighty and powerful; but his [abodily](#note10a) presence is weak, and his speech [bcontemptible](#note10b).

11 Let such an one think this, that, such as we are in word by letters when we are absent, such will we be also in deed when we are present.

12 For we dare not make ourselves of the number, or compare ourselves with some that commend themselves: but they measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves among themselves, are not wise.

13 But we will not [aboast](#note13a) of things without our measure, but according to the measure of the rule which God hath distributed to us, a measure to reach even unto you.

14 For we stretch not ourselves beyond our measure, as though we reached not unto you: for we are come as far as to you also in preaching the gospel of Christ:

15 Not boasting of things without our measure, that is, of other men’s [alabours](#note15a); but having hope, when your faith is increased, that we shall be enlarged by you according to our rule abundantly,

16 To preach the gospel in the regions beyond you, and not to boast in another man’s line of things made ready to our hand.

17 But he that glorieth, let him [aglory](#note17a) in the Lord.

18 For not he that [acommendeth](#note18a) himself is approved, but whom the Lord commendeth.

Chapter 11

Maintain the simplicity that is in Christ—Satan sends forth false apostles—Paul glories in his sufferings for Christ.

1 Would to God ye could bear with me a little in my folly: and indeed bear with me.

2 For I am [ajealous](#note2a) over you with godly jealousy: for I have [bespoused](#note2b) you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.

3 But I fear, lest by any means, as the [aserpent](#note3a) [bbeguiled](#note3b) Eve through his [csubtilty](#note3c), so your minds should be [dcorrupted](#note3d) from the [esimplicity](#note3e) that is in Christ.

4 For if he that cometh preacheth another [aJesus](#note4a), whom we have not preached, or if ye receive another spirit, which ye have not received, or another [bgospel](#note4b), which ye have not accepted, ye might well bear with him.

5 For I suppose I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.

6 But though I be [arude](#note6a) in speech, yet not in knowledge; but we have been throughly made manifest among you in all things.

7 Have I committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted, because I have preached to you the gospel of God [afreely](#note7a)?

8 I [arobbed other churches](#note8a), taking [bwages](#note8b) of them, to do you service.

9 And when I was present with you, and wanted, I was [achargeable](#note9a) to no man: for that which was [blacking](#note9b) to me the brethren which came from Macedonia [csupplied](#note9c): and in all things I have kept myself from being burdensome unto you, and so will I keep myself.

10 As the truth of Christ is in me, no man shall stop me of this boasting in the regions of Achaia.

11 Wherefore? because I love you not? God knoweth.

12 But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from them which desire occasion; that wherein they [aglory](#note12a), they may be found even as we.

13 For such are [afalse apostles](#note13a), deceitful workers, transforming themselves into the apostles of Christ.

14 And no marvel; for [aSatan](#note14a) himself is transformed into an [bangel](#note14b) of light.

15 Therefore it is no great thing if his ministers also be transformed as the ministers of righteousness; whose [aend](#note15a) shall be according to their works.

16 I say again, Let no man think me a fool; if otherwise, yet as a fool [areceive](#note16a) me, that I may [bboast](#note16b) myself a little.

17 That which I speak, I speak it not after the Lord, but as it were foolishly, in this confidence of boasting.

18 Seeing that many glory after the flesh, I will glory also.

19 For ye suffer fools gladly, seeing ye yourselves are wise.

20 For ye suffer, if a man bring you into [abondage](#note20a), if a man devour you, if a man take of you, if a man exalt himself, if a man smite you on the face.

21 I speak as concerning [areproach](#note21a), as though we had been weak. Howbeit whereinsoever any is bold, (I speak foolishly,) I am bold also.

22 Are they [aHebrews](#note22a)? so am I. Are they [bIsraelites](#note22b)? so am I. Are they the seed of Abraham? so am I.

23 Are they [aministers](#note23a) of Christ? (I speak as a fool) [bI](#note23b) am more; in [clabours](#note23c) more abundant, in [dstripes](#note23d) above measure, in [eprisons](#note23e) more frequent, in deaths oft.

24 Of the Jews five times received I [aforty](#note24a) stripes save one.

25 Thrice was I [abeaten](#note25a) with rods, once was I stoned, thrice I suffered shipwreck, a night and a day I have been in the deep;

26 In journeyings often, in perils of waters, in perils of robbers, in perils by mine own [acountrymen](#note26a), in perils by the [bheathen](#note26b), in [cperils](#note26c) in the city, in perils in the wilderness, in perils in the sea, in perils among false brethren;

27 In [aweariness](#note27a) and [bpainfulness](#note27b), in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.

28 Beside those things that are without, that which cometh upon me daily, the care of all the churches.

29 Who is weak, and I am not [aweak](#note29a)? who is offended, and I [bburn not](#note29b)?

30 If I must needs glory, I will glory of the things which concern mine infirmities.

31 The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which is blessed for evermore, knoweth that I lie not.

32 In Damascus the governor under Aretas the king kept the city of the Damascenes with a garrison, desirous to [aapprehend](#note32a) me:

33 And through a window in a basket was I let down by the wall, and escaped his hands.

  32a

  [Acts 9:23 (23–25)](../acts/9.xhtml#p23).

Chapter 12

Paul is caught up to the third heaven—The Lord gives men weaknesses that they may triumph over them—Paul manifests the signs of an Apostle.

1 It is not expedient for me doubtless to [aglory](#note1a). I will come to [bvisions](#note1b) and [crevelations](#note1c) of the Lord.

2 [aI](#note2a) knew a man in Christ above fourteen years ago, (whether in the [bbody](#note2b), I cannot tell; or whether out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;) such an one caught up to the [cthird](#note2c) [dheaven](#note2d).

3 And I knew such a man, (whether in the body, or out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;)

4 How that he was caught up into [aparadise](#note4a), and heard [bunspeakable](#note4b) words, which it is [cnot](#note4c) [dlawful](#note4d) for a man to utter.

5 Of such an one will I glory: yet of myself I will not [aglory](#note5a), but in mine infirmities.

6 For though I would desire to glory, I shall not be a fool; for I will say the truth: but now I forbear, lest any man should think of me above that which he seeth me to be, or that he heareth of me.

7 And lest I should be [aexalted](#note7a) above measure through the abundance of the revelations, there was given to me a [bthorn](#note7b) in the flesh, the messenger of Satan to buffet me, lest I should be exalted above measure.

8 For this thing I besought the Lord thrice, that it might depart from me.

9 And he said unto me, My [agrace](#note9a) is sufficient for thee: for my [bstrength](#note9b) is made perfect in [cweakness](#note9c). Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may [drest](#note9d) upon me.

10 Therefore I take pleasure in [ainfirmities](#note10a), in reproaches, in necessities, in [bpersecutions](#note10b), in distresses for Christ’s sake: for when I am [cweak](#note10c), then am I [dstrong](#note10d).

11 I am become a fool in glorying; ye have compelled me: for I ought to have been commended of you: for in nothing am I behind the very [achiefest](#note11a) apostles, though I be nothing.

12 Truly the [asigns](#note12a) of an apostle were wrought among you in all patience, in signs, and wonders, and mighty deeds.

13 For what is it wherein ye were inferior to other churches, except it be that I myself was not [aburdensome](#note13a) to you? forgive me this wrong.

14 Behold, the third time I am ready to come to you; and I will not be burdensome to you: for I [aseek](#note14a) not yours, but you: for the children ought not to lay up for the parents, but the parents for the children.

15 And I will very gladly spend and be spent for you; though the more abundantly I love you, the less I be loved.

16 But be it so, I did not burden you: nevertheless, being crafty, I caught you with guile.

17 Did I make a gain of you by any of them whom I sent unto you?

18 I desired Titus, and with him I sent a brother. Did Titus make a gain of you? walked we not in the same spirit? walked we not in the same steps?

19 Again, think ye that we excuse ourselves unto you? we speak before God in Christ: but we do all things, dearly beloved, for your [aedifying](#note19a).

20 For I fear, lest, when I come, I shall not find you such as I would, and that I shall be found unto you such as ye would not: lest there be debates, envyings, wraths, [astrifes](#note20a), [bbackbitings](#note20b), whisperings, swellings, [ctumults](#note20c):

21 And lest, when I come again, my God will humble me among you, and that I shall bewail many which have sinned already, and have not repented of the uncleanness and fornication and lasciviousness which they have committed.

Chapter 13

Saints should test themselves as to righteousness—Be perfect and of one mind; live in peace.

1 This is the third time I am coming to you. In the mouth of two or three [awitnesses](#note1a) shall every word be established.

2 I told you before, and foretell you, as if I were present, the second time; and being absent now I write to them which heretofore have sinned, and to all other, that, if I come again, I will not spare:

3 Since ye seek a proof of Christ speaking in me, which to you-ward is not weak, but is mighty in you.

4 For though he was [acrucified](#note4a) through weakness, yet he liveth by the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but we shall live with him by the power of God toward you.

5 [aExamine](#note5a) yourselves, whether ye be in the faith; prove your own selves. Know ye not your own selves, how that [bJesus](#note5b) [cChrist](#note5c) is in you, except ye be reprobates?

6 But I trust that ye shall know that we are not reprobates.

7 Now I pray to God that ye do no evil; not that we should appear approved, but that ye should do that which is [ahonest](#note7a), though we be as reprobates.

8 For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.

9 For we are glad, when we are [aweak](#note9a), and ye are strong: and this also we wish, even your perfection.

10 Therefore I write these things being absent, lest being present I should use sharpness, according to the [apower](#note10a) which the Lord hath given me to edification, and not to destruction.

11 Finally, brethren, farewell. Be [aperfect](#note11a), be of good comfort, be of [bone](#note11b) [cmind](#note11c), live in [dpeace](#note11d); and the God of [elove](#note11e) and peace shall be with you.

12 Greet one another with an holy [akiss](#note12a).

13 All the saints salute you.

14 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the communion of the Holy Ghost, be with you all. Amen.

¶ The second epistle to the Corinthians was written from Philippi, a city of Macedonia, by Titus and Lucas.

# The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Galatians

* [Galatians 1](gal/1.xhtml)
* [Galatians 2](gal/2.xhtml)
* [Galatians 3](gal/3.xhtml)
* [Galatians 4](gal/4.xhtml)
* [Galatians 5](gal/5.xhtml)
* [Galatians 6](gal/6.xhtml)

Chapter 1

Preachers of false gospels are accursed—Paul received the gospel by revelation—He believed, was taught, and preached to the Gentiles.

1 Paul, an [aapostle](#note1a), (not of men, neither by man, but by Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead;)

2 And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:

3 Grace be to you and peace from God the Father, and from our Lord Jesus Christ,

4 Who [agave](#note4a) himself for our sins, that he might [bdeliver](#note4b) us from this present evil world, according to the [cwill](#note4c) of God and our Father:

5 To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

6 I marvel that ye are so soon [aremoved](#note6a) from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another [bgospel](#note6b):

7 Which is not another; but there be some that [atrouble](#note7a) you, and would [bpervert](#note7b) the [cgospel](#note7c) of Christ.

8 But though we, or an [aangel](#note8a) from heaven, preach any [bother](#note8b) [cgospel](#note8c) unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be [daccursed](#note8d).

9 As we said before, so say I now again, If any man preach any other [agospel](#note9a) unto you than that ye have received, let him be [baccursed](#note9b).

10 For do I now [apersuade](#note10a) men, or God? or do I seek to [bplease](#note10b) men? for if I yet [cpleased](#note10c) men, I should not be the servant of Christ.

11 But I [acertify](#note11a) you, brethren, that the gospel which was [bpreached](#note11b) of me is not after man.

12 For I neither received it [aof man](#note12a), neither was I taught it, but [bby](#note12b) the [crevelation](#note12c) of Jesus Christ.

13 For ye have heard of my [aconversation](#note13a) in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I [bpersecuted](#note13b) the church of God, and [cwasted](#note13c) it:

14 And [aprofited](#note14a) in the Jews’ religion above many [bmy equals](#note14b) in mine own nation, being more exceedingly [czealous](#note14c) of the [dtraditions](#note14d) of my fathers.

15 But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me by his [agrace](#note15a),

16 To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the [aheathen](#note16a); immediately I conferred not with [bflesh and blood](#note16b):

17 Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.

18 Then after three years I went up to [aJerusalem](#note18a) to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.

19 But other of the [aapostles](#note19a) saw I none, save [bJames](#note19b) the Lord’s [cbrother](#note19c).

20 Now the things which I [awrite](#note20a) unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.

21 Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;

22 And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judæa which were in Christ:

23 But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.

24 And they glorified God in me.

Chapter 2

Paul goes to Jerusalem—He contends for the true gospel—Salvation comes through Christ.

1 Then fourteen years after I went up again to [aJerusalem](#note1a) with [bBarnabas](#note1b), and took Titus with me also.

2 And I went up by [arevelation](#note2a), and [bcommunicated](#note2b) unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.

3 But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be [acircumcised](#note3a):

4 [aAnd](#note4a) that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our [bliberty](#note4b) which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into [cbondage](#note4c):

5 To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.

6 But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man’s person:) for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added nothing to me:

7 But contrariwise, when they saw that the [agospel](#note7a) of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as the gospel of the [bcircumcision](#note7b) was unto [cPeter](#note7c);

8 (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)

9 And when James, [aCephas](#note9a), and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the [bgrace](#note9b) that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of [cfellowship](#note9c); that we should go unto the [dheathen](#note9d), and they unto the circumcision.

10 Only they would that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.

11 But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.

12 For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.

13 And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their [adissimulation](#note13a).

14 But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before them all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?

15 We who are Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,

16 Knowing that a man is not [ajustified](#note16a) by the works of the [blaw](#note16b), but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the [cfaith](#note16c) of Christ, and not by the [dworks](#note16d) of the [elaw](#note16e): for by the works of the law shall no flesh be [fjustified](#note16f).

17 But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, is therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.

18 For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.

19 For I through the law am [adead](#note19a) to the law, that I might [blive](#note19b) unto God.

20 I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but [aChrist](#note20a) [bliveth](#note20b) in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the [cfaith](#note20c) of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.

21 I do not frustrate the [agrace](#note21a) of God: for if righteousness come by the [blaw](#note21b), then Christ is dead in vain.

Chapter 3

God gave the gospel to Abraham—The Mosaic law was added because of transgressions—The law was a schoolmaster until Christ—The Saints are children of God by faith—All who are of the faith and baptized into Christ become Abraham’s seed.

1 O foolish Galatians, who hath [abewitched](#note1a) you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?

2 This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the [alaw](#note2a), or by the [bhearing](#note2b) of faith?

3 Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?

4 Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.

5 He therefore that [aministereth](#note5a) to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?

6 Even as Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for [arighteousness](#note6a).

7 Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the [achildren of Abraham](#note7a).

8 And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the [aheathen](#note8a) through faith, preached before the [bgospel](#note8b) unto Abraham, saying, In thee shall all [cnations](#note8c) be [dblessed](#note8d).

9 So then they which be of faith are [ablessed](#note9a) with faithful Abraham.

10 For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, [aCursed](#note10a) is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them.

11 But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, The just shall live by [afaith](#note11a).

12 And the law is not of faith: but, The man that doeth them shall live in them.

13 Christ hath [aredeemed](#note13a) us from the [bcurse](#note13b) of the [claw](#note13c), being made a curse for us: for it is written, [dCursed](#note13d) is every one that hangeth on a tree:

14 That the [ablessing](#note14a) of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through [bJesus Christ](#note14b); that we might receive the [cpromise](#note14c) of the Spirit through faith.

15 Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though it be but a man’s covenant, yet if it be confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.

16 Now to [aAbraham](#note16a) and his seed were the [bpromises](#note16b) made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.

17 And this I say, that the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was [afour hundred and thirty](#note17a) years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the promise of none effect.

18 For if the [ainheritance](#note18a) be of the law, it is no more of promise: but God gave it to Abraham by promise.

19 [aWherefore](#note19a) then serveth the law? It was [badded](#note19b) because of transgressions, till the seed should come to whom the promise was made; and it was ordained by angels in the hand of a mediator.

20 Now a mediator is not a mediator of one, but God is one.

21 Is the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.

22 But the scripture hath concluded all under [asin](#note22a), that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.

23 But before faith came, we were kept under the law, shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed.

24 Wherefore the [alaw](#note24a) was our [bschoolmaster](#note24b) to bring us unto Christ, that we might be [cjustified](#note24c) by faith.

25 But after that faith is come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.

26 For ye are all the [achildren](#note26a) of God by [bfaith](#note26b) in Christ Jesus.

27 For as many of you as have been [abaptized](#note27a) into Christ have put on Christ.

28 There is neither Jew nor [aGreek](#note28a), there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all [bone](#note28b) in Christ Jesus.

29 And if ye be Christ’s, then are ye [aAbraham’s](#note29a) [bseed](#note29b), and [cheirs](#note29c) according to the promise.

Chapter 4

The Saints are children of God by adoption—Paul calls the Galatians back to Christ—He compares the two covenants.

1 Now I say, That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;

2 But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.

3 Even so we, when we were children, were in [abondage](#note3a) under the elements of the world:

4 But when the fulness of the time was come, God [asent](#note4a) forth his [bSon](#note4b), [cmade](#note4c) of a [dwoman](#note4d), made under the law,

5 To [aredeem](#note5a) them that were under the law, that we might receive the [badoption](#note5b) of sons.

6 And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.

7 Wherefore thou art no more a [aservant](#note7a), but a [bson](#note7b); and if a son, then an [cheir](#note7c) of God through Christ.

8 Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye [adid](#note8a) [bservice](#note8b) unto them which by nature are no [cgods](#note8c).

9 But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in [abondage](#note9a)?

10 Ye observe [adays](#note10a), and months, and times, and years.

11 I am afraid [aof](#note11a) you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.

12 Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all.

13 Ye know how through [ainfirmity](#note13a) of the [bflesh](#note13b) I preached the gospel unto you at the first.

14 And my [atemptation](#note14a) which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.

15 Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.

16 Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the [atruth](#note16a)?

17 They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.

18 But it is good to be [azealously](#note18a) affected always in a good thing, and not only when I am present with you.

19 My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,

20 I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I [astand in doubt of](#note20a) you.

21 Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?

22 For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a [abondmaid](#note22a), the other by a freewoman.

23 But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.

24 Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to [abondage](#note24a), which is [bAgar](#note24b).

25 For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.

26 But [aJerusalem](#note26a) which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.

27 For it is written, [aRejoice](#note27a), thou [bbarren](#note27b) that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband.

28 Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the [achildren](#note28a) of promise.

29 But as then he that was born after the [aflesh](#note29a) [bpersecuted](#note29b) him that was born after the Spirit, even so it is now.

30 Nevertheless what saith the scripture? [aCast](#note30a) out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.

31 So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free.
