Chapter 5

Stand fast in gospel liberty—Seek faith, love, Christ, and the Spirit—The works of the flesh and the fruits of the Spirit are named.

1 [aStand](#note1a) fast therefore in the [bliberty](#note1b) wherewith Christ hath made us [cfree](#note1c), and be not [dentangled](#note1d) again with the yoke of [ebondage](#note1e).

2 Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.

3 For I testify again to every man that is [acircumcised](#note3a), that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

4 Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the [alaw](#note4a); ye are fallen from [bgrace](#note4b).

5 For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by [afaith](#note5a).

6 For in Jesus Christ neither [acircumcision](#note6a) [bavaileth any thing](#note6b), nor uncircumcision; but [cfaith](#note6c) which worketh by [dlove](#note6d).

7 Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not [aobey](#note7a) the truth?

8 This persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you.

9 A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.

10 I have [aconfidence](#note10a) in you through the Lord, that ye [bwill be](#note10b) none otherwise minded: but he that [ctroubleth](#note10c) you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.

11 And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the [aoffence](#note11a) of the cross ceased.

12 I would they were even [acut off](#note12a) which [btrouble](#note12b) you.

13 For, brethren, ye have been called unto [aliberty](#note13a); only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love [bserve](#note13b) one another.

14 For all the [alaw](#note14a) is fulfilled in one word, even in this; Thou shalt [blove](#note14b) thy neighbour as thyself.

15 But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.

16 This I say then, [aWalk](#note16a) in the [bSpirit](#note16b), and ye shall not fulfil the [clust](#note16c) of the flesh.

17 For the flesh lusteth against the [aSpirit](#note17a), and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.

18 But if ye be [aled](#note18a) of the Spirit, ye are not under the [blaw](#note18b).

19 Now the works of the [aflesh](#note19a) are manifest, which are these; Adultery, [bfornication](#note19b), [cuncleanness](#note19c), lasciviousness,

20 Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, [astrife](#note20a), seditions, heresies,

21 [aEnvyings](#note21a), murders, [bdrunkenness](#note21b), [crevellings](#note21c), and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.

22 But the [afruit](#note22a) of the [bSpirit](#note22b) is [clove](#note22c), [djoy](#note22d), [epeace](#note22e), [flongsuffering](#note22f), [ggentleness](#note22g), goodness, [hfaith](#note22h),

23 [aMeekness](#note23a), [btemperance](#note23b): against such there is no law.

24 And they that are Christ’s have crucified the flesh with the [aaffections](#note24a) and lusts.

25 If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.

26 Let us not be desirous of vain glory, [aprovoking](#note26a) one another, envying one another.

Chapter 6

Bear one another’s burdens—As you sow, so shall you reap—Be not weary in well-doing.

1 Brethren, if a man be [aovertaken](#note1a) in a [bfault](#note1b), ye which are spiritual, [crestore](#note1c) such an one in the spirit of meekness; [dconsidering](#note1d) thyself, lest thou also be tempted.

2 Bear ye one another’s [aburdens](#note2a), and so fulfil the law of Christ.

3 For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.

4 But let every man [aprove](#note4a) his own [bwork](#note4b), and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.

5 For every man shall [abear](#note5a) his own burden.

6 Let him that is taught in the word [acommunicate](#note6a) unto him that teacheth in all good things.

7 Be not [adeceived](#note7a); God is not [bmocked](#note7b): for whatsoever a man [csoweth](#note7c), that shall he also [dreap](#note7d).

8 For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.

9 And let us not be [aweary](#note9a) in well doing: for in due season we shall [breap](#note9b), if we faint not.

10 As we have therefore opportunity, let us do [agood](#note10a) unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.

11 Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.

12 As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer [apersecution](#note12a) for the cross of Christ.

13 For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.

14 But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.

15 For in Christ Jesus neither [acircumcision](#note15a) availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.

16 And as many as walk according to this rule, [apeace](#note16a) be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.

17 From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.

18 Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

¶ Unto the Galatians written from Rome.

# The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Ephesians

* [Ephesians 1](eph/1.xhtml)
* [Ephesians 2](eph/2.xhtml)
* [Ephesians 3](eph/3.xhtml)
* [Ephesians 4](eph/4.xhtml)
* [Ephesians 5](eph/5.xhtml)
* [Ephesians 6](eph/6.xhtml)

Chapter 1

The Saints are foreordained to receive the gospel—The gospel is to be restored in the latter days—The Saints are sealed by the Holy Spirit of Promise—They know God and Christ by revelation.

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, to the [asaints](#note1a) which are at Ephesus, and to the faithful in Christ Jesus:

2 Grace be to you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who hath blessed us with all [aspiritual](#note3a) blessings in heavenly places in Christ:

4 According as he hath [achosen](#note4a) us in him [bbefore](#note4b) the foundation of the world, that we should be [choly](#note4c) and without blame before him in love:

5 Having [apredestinated](#note5a) us unto the [badoption](#note5b) of children by Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,

6 To the praise of the glory of his grace, wherein he hath made us accepted in the beloved.

7 In whom we have [aredemption](#note7a) through his blood, the [bforgiveness](#note7b) of sins, according to the [criches](#note7c) of his [dgrace](#note7d);

8 Wherein he hath abounded toward us in all [awisdom](#note8a) and [bprudence](#note8b);

9 Having made known unto us the [amystery](#note9a) of his [bwill](#note9b), according to his good pleasure which he hath purposed in himself:

10 That in the [adispensation](#note10a) of the fulness of times he might [bgather](#note10b) together in one [call](#note10c) things in Christ, both which are in heaven, and which are on earth; even in him:

11 In whom also we have obtained an inheritance, being [apredestinated](#note11a) according to the purpose of him who worketh all things after the counsel of his own will:

12 That we should be to the praise of his glory, who [afirst trusted](#note12a) in Christ.

13 In whom ye also trusted, after that ye heard the word of truth, the [agospel](#note13a) of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were [bsealed](#note13b) with that holy Spirit of [cpromise](#note13c),

14 Which is the earnest of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession, unto the praise of his glory.

15 Wherefore I also, after I heard of your [afaith](#note15a) in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,

16 Cease not to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers;

17 That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of [awisdom](#note17a) and [brevelation](#note17b) in the knowledge of him:

18 The eyes of your [aunderstanding](#note18a) being [benlightened](#note18b); that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the [criches](#note18c) of the glory of his [dinheritance](#note18d) in the saints,

19 And what is the exceeding greatness of his [apower](#note19a) to us-ward who believe, according to the working of his mighty power,

20 Which he wrought in Christ, when he [araised](#note20a) him from the dead, and set him at his own right hand in the [bheavenly](#note20b) places,

21 Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every [aname](#note21a) that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come:

22 And hath put [aall](#note22a) things under his [bfeet](#note22b), and gave him to be the [chead](#note22c) over all things to the [dchurch](#note22d),

23 Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all.

Chapter 2

We are saved by grace through faith—The blood of Christ saves Jew and Gentile alike—The Church is built upon the foundation of apostles and prophets.

1 And you hath he quickened, who were [adead](#note1a) in trespasses and sins;

2 Wherein in time past ye walked according to the course of this [aworld](#note2a), according to the prince of the power of the air, the spirit that now worketh in the children of [bdisobedience](#note2b):

3 Among whom also we all had our conversation in times past in the [alusts](#note3a) of our flesh, fulfilling the desires of the flesh and of the mind; and were by [bnature](#note3b) the children of wrath, even as others.

4 But God, who is rich in [amercy](#note4a), for his great love wherewith he loved us,

5 Even when we were dead in sins, hath [aquickened](#note5a) us together with Christ, (by grace ye are saved;)

6 And hath raised us up together, and made us sit together in heavenly places in Christ Jesus:

7 That in the ages to come he might shew the exceeding [ariches](#note7a) of his grace in his kindness toward us through Christ Jesus.

8 For by [agrace](#note8a) are ye [bsaved](#note8b) through [cfaith](#note8c); and that not of yourselves: it is the [dgift](#note8d) of God:

9 Not of works, lest any man should [aboast](#note9a).

10 For we are his [aworkmanship](#note10a), created in Christ Jesus unto good [bworks](#note10b), which God hath before ordained that we should [cwalk](#note10c) in them.

11 Wherefore remember, that ye being in time past Gentiles in the flesh, who are called [aUncircumcision](#note11a) by that which is called the Circumcision in the flesh made by hands;

12 That at that time ye were without Christ, being aliens from the commonwealth of Israel, and [astrangers](#note12a) from the [bcovenants](#note12b) of promise, having no [chope](#note12c), and [dwithout](#note12d) God in the world:

13 But now in Christ Jesus ye who sometimes were far off are made nigh by the blood of Christ.

14 For he is our [apeace](#note14a), who hath made both one, and hath broken down the middle [bwall](#note14b) of partition between us;

15 Having [aabolished](#note15a) in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments contained in [bordinances](#note15b); for to make in himself of twain one [cnew man](#note15c), so making peace;

16 And that he might [areconcile](#note16a) both unto God in one body by the cross, having slain the enmity thereby:

17 And came and preached peace to you which were afar off, and to them that were nigh.

18 For through him we both have access by one Spirit unto the Father.

19 Now therefore ye are no more [astrangers](#note19a) and foreigners, but [bfellowcitizens](#note19b) with the [csaints](#note19c), and of the [dhousehold](#note19d) of God;

20 And are built upon the foundation of the [aapostles](#note20a) and [bprophets](#note20b), Jesus Christ himself being the chief [ccorner stone](#note20c);

21 In whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord:

22 In whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of God through the Spirit.

Chapter 3

The Gentiles are fellow heirs with Israel—The love of Christ surpasses all understanding.

1 For this cause I Paul, the prisoner of Jesus Christ for you Gentiles,

2 If ye have heard of the [adispensation](#note2a) of the grace of God which is given me to you-ward:

3 How that by revelation he made [aknown](#note3a) unto me the mystery; (as I [bwrote](#note3b) afore in few words,

4 Whereby, when ye read, ye may understand my knowledge in the [amystery](#note4a) of Christ)

5 Which in other ages was not made known unto the sons of men, as it is now [arevealed](#note5a) unto his holy apostles and [bprophets](#note5b) by the Spirit;

6 That the [aGentiles](#note6a) should be fellowheirs, and of the same body, and partakers of his promise in Christ by the gospel:

7 Whereof I was made a minister, according to the gift of the grace of God given unto me by the effectual working of his power.

8 Unto me, who am less than the least of all saints, is this [agrace](#note8a) given, that I should preach among the Gentiles the unsearchable [briches](#note8b) of Christ;

9 And to make all men see what is the fellowship of the [amystery](#note9a), which from the beginning of the world hath been hid in God, who [bcreated](#note9b) all things by [cJesus Christ](#note9c):

10 To the intent that now unto the principalities and powers in heavenly places might be known by the church the manifold wisdom of God,

11 According to the eternal [apurpose](#note11a) which he purposed in Christ Jesus our Lord:

12 In whom we have boldness and access with confidence by the faith of him.

13 Wherefore I desire that ye faint not at my tribulations for you, which is your glory.

14 For this cause I bow my knees unto the [aFather](#note14a) of our Lord Jesus Christ,

15 Of whom the whole [afamily](#note15a) in heaven and earth is named,

16 That he would grant you, according to the [ariches](#note16a) of his glory, to be [bstrengthened](#note16b) with might by his Spirit in the inner man;

17 That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith; that ye, being [arooted](#note17a) and [bgrounded](#note17b) in love,

18 May be able to comprehend with all saints what is the breadth, and length, and depth, and height;

19 And to know the [alove](#note19a) of Christ, which passeth knowledge, that ye might be filled with all the fulness of God.

20 Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us,

21 Unto him be glory in the church by Christ Jesus throughout all ages, [aworld](#note21a) without end. Amen.

Chapter 4

There is one Lord, one faith, and one baptism—Apostles and prophets are essential to the Church—The Saints are exhorted to live righteously—They are sealed unto the day of redemption.

1 I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk [aworthy](#note1a) of the [bvocation](#note1b) wherewith ye are called,

2 With all lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering, [aforbearing](#note2a) one another in love;

3 Endeavouring to keep the [aunity](#note3a) of the Spirit in the bond of peace.

4 There is one [abody](#note4a), and one Spirit, even as ye are called in one hope of your calling;

5 One Lord, one [afaith](#note5a), one [bbaptism](#note5b),

6 One God and [aFather](#note6a) of all, who is above all, and through all, and in you all.

7 But unto every one of us is given [agrace](#note7a) according to the measure of the gift of Christ.

8 Wherefore he saith, When he [aascended](#note8a) up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men.

9 (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth?

10 He that descended is the same also that [aascended](#note10a) up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things.)

11 And he [agave](#note11a) some, [bapostles](#note11b); and some, [cprophets](#note11c); and some, [devangelists](#note11d); and some, [epastors](#note11e) and [fteachers](#note11f);

12 For the [aperfecting](#note12a) of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the [bedifying](#note12b) of the body of Christ:

13 Till we all come in the [aunity](#note13a) of the faith, and of the [bknowledge](#note13b) of the Son of God, unto a [cperfect](#note13c) man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:

14 That we henceforth be no more [achildren](#note14a), tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of [bdoctrine](#note14b), by the sleight of men, and cunning craftiness, whereby they lie in wait to deceive;

15 But speaking the [atruth](#note15a) in love, may grow up into him in all things, which is the [bhead](#note15b), even Christ:

16 From whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth, according to the effectual working in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love.

17 This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other [aGentiles](#note17a) walk, in the [bvanity](#note17b) of their [cmind](#note17c),

18 Having the [aunderstanding](#note18a) darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the [bignorance](#note18b) that is in them, because of the [cblindness](#note18c) of their [dheart](#note18d):

19 Who being [apast](#note19a) [bfeeling](#note19b) have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.

20 But ye have not so learned Christ;

21 If so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is in Jesus:

22 That ye [aput](#note22a) off concerning the former conversation the [bold](#note22b) man, which is corrupt according to the deceitful [clusts](#note22c);

23 And be renewed in the spirit of your mind;

24 And that ye put on the [anew man](#note24a), which after God is created in [brighteousness](#note24b) and true holiness.

25 Wherefore putting away [alying](#note25a), speak every man [btruth](#note25b) with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.

26 [aBe](#note26a) ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your [bwrath](#note26b):

27 Neither give place to the [adevil](#note27a).

28 Let him that stole [asteal](#note28a) no more: but rather let him [blabour](#note28b), working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to [cgive](#note28c) to him that needeth.

29 Let no corrupt [acommunication](#note29a) proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.

30 And [agrieve](#note30a) not the holy [bSpirit](#note30b) of God, whereby ye are [csealed](#note30c) unto the day of redemption.

31 Let all bitterness, and wrath, and [aanger](#note31a), and clamour, and [bevil](#note31b) [cspeaking](#note31c), be put away from you, with all [dmalice](#note31d):

32 And be ye [akind](#note32a) one to another, tenderhearted, [bforgiving](#note32b) one another, even as God for Christ’s sake hath forgiven you.

Chapter 5

The Saints are exhorted to avoid uncleanness and walk uprightly—Husbands and wives should love each other.

1 Be ye therefore [afollowers](#note1a) of God, as dear children;

2 And [awalk](#note2a) in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an [boffering](#note2b) and a [csacrifice](#note2c) to God for a sweetsmelling [dsavour](#note2d).

3 But [afornication](#note3a), and all uncleanness, or covetousness, let it not be once named among you, as becometh [bsaints](#note3b);

4 Neither [afilthiness](#note4a), nor [bfoolish](#note4b) [ctalking](#note4c), nor jesting, which are not convenient: but rather giving of thanks.

5 For this ye know, that no [awhoremonger](#note5a), nor [bunclean](#note5b) person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, hath any [cinheritance](#note5c) in the kingdom of Christ and of God.

6 Let no man [adeceive](#note6a) you with vain words: for because of these things cometh the [bwrath](#note6b) of God upon the children of [cdisobedience](#note6c).

7 Be not ye therefore partakers with them.

8 For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as [achildren](#note8a) of [blight](#note8b):

9 (For the fruit of the Spirit is in all [agoodness](#note9a) and righteousness and truth;)

10 Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord.

11 And have no [afellowship](#note11a) with the unfruitful works of [bdarkness](#note11b), but rather [creprove](#note11c) them.

12 For it is a [ashame](#note12a) even to speak of those things which are done of them in secret.

13 But all things that are reproved are made manifest by the light: for whatsoever doth make manifest is light.

14 Wherefore he saith, Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee [alight](#note14a).

15 See then that ye walk circumspectly, not as fools, but as wise,

16 Redeeming the time, because the days are evil.

17 Wherefore be ye not [aunwise](#note17a), but understanding what the will of the Lord is.

18 And be not [adrunk](#note18a) with wine, wherein is [bexcess](#note18b); but be filled with the Spirit;

19 Speaking to yourselves in psalms and [ahymns](#note19a) and spiritual songs, singing and making [bmelody](#note19b) in your heart to the Lord;

20 Giving [athanks](#note20a) always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ;

21 [aSubmitting](#note21a) yourselves one to another in the fear of God.

22 [aWives](#note22a), [bsubmit](#note22b) yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.

23 For the [ahusband](#note23a) is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the [bhead](#note23b) of the [cchurch](#note23c): and he is the saviour of the body.

24 Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.

25 [aHusbands](#note25a), [blove](#note25b) your [cwives](#note25c), even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;

26 That he might [asanctify](#note26a) and [bcleanse](#note26b) it with the washing of water by the word,

27 That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.

28 So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his [awife](#note28a) loveth himself.

29 For no man ever yet [ahated](#note29a) his own [bflesh](#note29b); but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:

30 For we are [amembers](#note30a) of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.

31 For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be [aone](#note31a) flesh.

32 This is a great [amystery](#note32a): but I speak concerning Christ and the church.

33 Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the [awife](#note33a) see that she [breverence](#note33b) her husband.

Chapter 6

Children should honor their parents—Servants and masters are judged by the same law—Saints should put on the whole armor of God.

1 [aChildren](#note1a), [bobey](#note1b) your parents in the Lord: for this is right.

2 [aHonour](#note2a) thy father and mother; (which is the first commandment with promise;)

3 That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.

4 And, ye [afathers](#note4a), [bprovoke](#note4b) not your [cchildren](#note4c) to wrath: but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the Lord.

5 [aServants](#note5a), be obedient to them that are your [bmasters](#note5b) according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as unto Christ;

6 Not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but as the [aservants](#note6a) of Christ, doing the [bwill](#note6b) of God from the heart;

7 With good will doing [aservice](#note7a), as to the Lord, and not to men:

8 Knowing that whatsoever good thing any man doeth, the same shall he [areceive](#note8a) of the Lord, whether he be bond or free.

9 And, ye [amasters](#note9a), do the same things unto them, forbearing threatening: knowing that your [bMaster](#note9b) also is in heaven; neither is there [crespect](#note9c) of persons with him.

10 Finally, my brethren, be [astrong](#note10a) in the Lord, and in the power of his might.

11 Put on the whole [aarmour](#note11a) of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

12 For we [awrestle](#note12a) not [bagainst](#note12b) [cflesh and blood](#note12c), but against principalities, against powers, against the [drulers](#note12d) of the [edarkness](#note12e) of this world, against spiritual [fwickedness](#note12f) in high places.

13 Wherefore take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.

14 Stand therefore, having your loins [agirt](#note14a) about with [btruth](#note14b), and having on the [cbreastplate](#note14c) of [drighteousness](#note14d);

15 And your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of [apeace](#note15a);

16 Above all, taking the shield of [afaith](#note16a), wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery [bdarts](#note16b) of the wicked.

17 And take the helmet of [asalvation](#note17a), and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God:

18 [aPraying](#note18a) always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and watching thereunto with all [bperseverance](#note18b) and supplication for all saints;

19 And for me, that utterance may be given unto me, that I may open my mouth boldly, to make known the [amystery](#note19a) of the gospel,

20 For which I am an ambassador in bonds: that therein I may speak [aboldly](#note20a), as I ought to speak.

21 But that ye also may know my affairs, and how I do, [aTychicus](#note21a), a beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, shall make known to you all things:

22 Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that ye might know our affairs, and that he might comfort your hearts.

23 Peace be to the brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

24 Grace be with all them that love our Lord Jesus Christ in [asincerity](#note24a). Amen.

¶ Written from Rome unto the Ephesians by Tychicus.

# The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Philippians

* [Philippians 1](philip/1.xhtml)
* [Philippians 2](philip/2.xhtml)
* [Philippians 3](philip/3.xhtml)
* [Philippians 4](philip/4.xhtml)

Chapter 1

All that happened to Paul furthered the gospel cause—Our conduct should be worthy of the gospel.

1 Paul and Timotheus, the [aservants](#note1a) of Jesus Christ, to all the saints in Christ Jesus which are at Philippi, with the [bbishops](#note1b) and [cdeacons](#note1c):

2 Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 I thank my God upon every remembrance of you,

4 Always in every prayer of mine for you all making request with joy,

5 For your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now;

6 Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you will [aperform](#note6a) it until the day of Jesus Christ:

7 Even as it is [ameet](#note7a) for me to think this of you all, because I have you in my heart; inasmuch as both in my bonds, and in the defence and [bconfirmation](#note7b) of the gospel, ye all are partakers of my [cgrace](#note7c).

8 For God is my [arecord](#note8a), how greatly I long after you all in the [bbowels](#note8b) of Jesus Christ.

9 And this I pray, that your [alove](#note9a) may abound yet more and more in knowledge and in all judgment;

10 That ye may [aapprove](#note10a) things that are excellent; that ye may be [bsincere](#note10b) and without offence till the day of Christ;

11 Being filled with the fruits of [arighteousness](#note11a), which are by Jesus Christ, unto the glory and praise of God.

12 But I would ye should understand, brethren, that the things which happened unto me have [afallen out](#note12a) rather unto the furtherance of the gospel;

13 So that my bonds in Christ are manifest in all the [apalace](#note13a), and in all other places;

14 And many of the brethren in the Lord, waxing confident by my bonds, are much more bold to speak the word without [afear](#note14a).

15 Some indeed preach Christ even of envy and [astrife](#note15a); and some also of good will:

16 The one preach Christ of contention, not sincerely, supposing to add affliction to my bonds:

17 But the other of love, knowing that I am set for the defence of the gospel.

18 What then? notwithstanding, every way, whether in pretence, or in truth, Christ is preached; and I therein do rejoice, yea, and will rejoice.

19 For I know that this shall turn to my salvation through your [aprayer](#note19a), and the supply of the [bSpirit](#note19b) of Jesus Christ,

20 According to my earnest expectation and my [ahope](#note20a), that in nothing I shall be ashamed, but that with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ shall be [bmagnified](#note20b) in my body, whether it be by life, or by death.

21 For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.

22 But if I live in the flesh, this is the fruit of my labour: yet what I shall choose I [awot](#note22a) not.

23 For I am [ain a strait](#note23a) betwixt two, having a desire to depart, and to be with Christ; which is far better:

24 Nevertheless to [aabide](#note24a) in the flesh is more needful for you.

25 And having this confidence, I know that I shall abide and continue with you all for your furtherance and joy of faith;

26 That your rejoicing may be more abundant in Jesus Christ for me by my coming to you again.

27 Only let your conversation be as it becometh the gospel of Christ: that whether I come and see you, or else be absent, I may hear of your affairs, that ye [astand fast](#note27a) in one spirit, with [bone](#note27b) [cmind](#note27c) [dstriving](#note27d) together for the faith of the gospel;

28 And in nothing terrified by your adversaries: [awhich is](#note28a) to them an evident token of perdition, but to you of salvation, and that of God.

29 For unto you it is given in the behalf of Christ, not only to believe on him, but also to [asuffer](#note29a) for his sake;

30 Having the same conflict which ye saw in me, and now hear to be in me.

Chapter 2

Saints should be of one mind and one spirit—Every knee will bow to Christ—Saints must work out their salvation—Paul faces martyrdom with joy.

1 If there be therefore any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any [abowels and mercies](#note1a),

2 Fulfil ye my joy, that ye be likeminded, having the same [alove](#note2a), being of [bone](#note2b) [caccord](#note2c), of one mind.

3 Let nothing be done through [astrife](#note3a) or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each [besteem](#note3b) other better than themselves.

4 Look not every man on his own things, but every man also on the things of others.

5 Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus:

6 Who, being in the [aform](#note6a) of God, thought it not robbery to be [bequal](#note6b) with God:

7 But made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a [aservant](#note7a), and was made in the likeness of men:

8 And being found in [afashion](#note8a) as a man, he [bhumbled](#note8b) himself, and became [cobedient](#note8c) unto [ddeath](#note8d), even the [edeath](#note8e) of the cross.

9 Wherefore God also hath highly [aexalted](#note9a) him, and given him a [bname](#note9b) which is above every name:

10 That at the [aname](#note10a) of Jesus every [bknee](#note10b) should bow, of things in heaven, and things in earth, and things under the earth;

11 And that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is [aLord](#note11a), to the glory of God the Father.

12 Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, [awork](#note12a) out your own [bsalvation](#note12b) with fear and trembling.

13 For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure.

14 Do all things without murmurings and [adisputings](#note14a):

15 That ye may be blameless and harmless, the [asons](#note15a) of God, without rebuke, in the midst of a crooked and perverse [bnation](#note15b), among whom ye [cshine](#note15c) as lights in the world;

16 Holding forth the word of life; that I may rejoice in the day of Christ, that I have not run in vain, neither laboured in vain.

17 Yea, and if I be [aoffered upon](#note17a) the sacrifice and service of your faith, I joy, and rejoice with you all.

18 For the same cause also do ye joy, and rejoice with me.

19 But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus shortly unto you, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know your state.

20 For I have no man likeminded, who will naturally care for your state.

21 For all [aseek](#note21a) their [bown](#note21b), not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.

22 But ye know the [aproof](#note22a) of him, that, as a son with the father, he hath served with me in the gospel.

23 Him therefore I hope to send presently, so soon as I shall see how it will go with me.

24 But I trust in the Lord that I also myself shall come shortly.

25 Yet I supposed it necessary to send to you [aEpaphroditus](#note25a), my brother, and companion in labour, and fellowsoldier, but your messenger, and he that ministered to my wants.

26 For he longed after you all, and was full of heaviness, because that ye had heard that he had been sick.

27 For indeed he was sick nigh unto death: but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.

28 I sent him therefore the more carefully, that, when ye see him again, ye may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.

29 Receive him therefore in the Lord with all [agladness](#note29a); and [bhold](#note29b) such in [creputation](#note29c):

30 Because for the work of Christ he was nigh unto death, not regarding his life, to supply your [alack](#note30a) of service toward me.

Chapter 3

Paul sacrifices all things for Christ—True ministers set examples of righteousness.

1 Finally, my brethren, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not grievous, but for you it is safe.

2 Beware of dogs, beware of evil workers, beware of the concision.

3 For we are the [acircumcision](#note3a), which [bworship](#note3b) God in the spirit, and rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the [cflesh](#note3c).

4 Though I might also have confidence in the flesh. If any other man thinketh that he hath whereof he might trust in the flesh, I more:

5 Circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, an [aHebrew](#note5a) of the Hebrews; as touching the law, a [bPharisee](#note5b);

6 Concerning zeal, persecuting the church; touching the righteousness which is in the law, blameless.

7 But what things were gain to me, those I counted loss for Christ.

8 Yea doubtless, and I count all things but loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord: for whom I have [asuffered](#note8a) the [bloss](#note8b) of all things, and do count them but [cdung](#note8c), that I may win Christ,

9 And be found in him, not having mine own righteousness, which is of the law, but that which is through the faith of Christ, the [arighteousness](#note9a) which is of God by faith:

10 That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the [afellowship](#note10a) of his sufferings, being made conformable unto his death;

11 If by any means I might attain unto the resurrection of the [adead](#note11a).

12 Not as though I had already attained, either were already [aperfect](#note12a): but I [bfollow after](#note12b), if that I may apprehend that for which also I am apprehended of Christ Jesus.

13 Brethren, I count not myself to have apprehended: but this one thing I do, forgetting those things which are behind, and reaching forth unto those things which are before,

14 I [apress](#note14a) toward the mark for the [bprize](#note14b) of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.

15 Let us therefore, as many as be [aperfect](#note15a), be thus minded: and if in any thing ye be otherwise minded, God shall reveal even this unto you.

16 Nevertheless, whereto we have already attained, let us walk by the same rule, let us mind the same thing.

17 Brethren, be [afollowers](#note17a) together of me, and mark them which walk so as ye have us for an [bensample](#note17b).

18 (For many walk, of whom I have told you often, and now tell you even weeping, that they are the enemies of the cross of Christ:

19 Whose [aend](#note19a) is destruction, whose God is their belly, [band](#note19b) whose glory is in their [cshame](#note19c), who mind earthly things.)

20 For our conversation is in heaven; from whence also we look for the [aSaviour](#note20a), the Lord Jesus Christ:

21 Who shall [achange](#note21a) our [bvile](#note21b) body, [cthat](#note21c) it may be fashioned like unto his glorious [dbody](#note21d), according to the working whereby he is able even to [esubdue](#note21e) all things unto himself.

Chapter 4

Stand fast in the Lord—We believe in being honest, true, and chaste.

1 Therefore, my brethren dearly beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, so [astand fast](#note1a) in the Lord, my dearly beloved.

2 I beseech Euodias, and beseech Syntyche, that they be of the same mind in the Lord.

3 And I entreat thee also, true [ayokefellow](#note3a), help those women which laboured with me in the gospel, with Clement also, and with other my fellowlabourers, whose names are in the [bbook of life](#note3b).

4 Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice.

5 Let your [amoderation](#note5a) be known unto all men. The Lord is at hand.

6 [aBe](#note6a) [bcareful](#note6b) for nothing; but in every thing by [cprayer](#note6c) and supplication with [dthanksgiving](#note6d) let your requests be made known unto God.

7 And the [apeace](#note7a) of God, which passeth all [bunderstanding](#note7b), shall [ckeep](#note7c) your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.

8 Finally, brethren, whatsoever things are [atrue](#note8a), whatsoever things are [bhonest](#note8b), whatsoever things are [cjust](#note8c), whatsoever things are [dpure](#note8d), whatsoever things are [elovely](#note8e), [fwhatsoever](#note8f) things are of good report; if there be any [gvirtue](#note8g), and if there be any praise, [hthink](#note8h) on these things.

9 Those things, which ye have both learned, and [areceived](#note9a), and heard, and seen in me, do: and the God of peace shall be with you.

10 But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly, that now at the last your [acare](#note10a) of me hath flourished again; wherein ye were also careful, but ye [blacked](#note10b) opportunity.

11 Not that I speak in respect of want: for I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be [acontent](#note11a).

12 I know both how to be [aabased](#note12a), and I know how to abound: every where and in all things I am instructed both to be full and to be hungry, both to abound and to suffer need.

13 I can do all things through [aChrist](#note13a) which [bstrengtheneth](#note13b) me.

14 Notwithstanding ye have well done, that ye did [acommunicate](#note14a) with my affliction.

15 Now ye Philippians know also, that in the beginning of the gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no church communicated with me as concerning giving and receiving, but ye only.

16 For even in Thessalonica ye sent once and again unto my necessity.

17 Not because I desire a gift: but I desire [afruit](#note17a) that may abound to your account.

18 But I have all, and [aabound](#note18a): I am full, having received of [bEpaphroditus](#note18b) the things which were sent from you, an odour of a sweet smell, a sacrifice acceptable, wellpleasing to God.

19 But my God shall supply all your [aneed](#note19a) according to his [briches](#note19b) in glory by Christ Jesus.

20 Now unto God and our Father be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

21 Salute every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren which are with me greet you.

22 All the saints salute you, chiefly they that are of [aCæsar’s](#note22a) household.

23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

¶ It was written to the Philippians from Rome by Epaphroditus.

# The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Colossians

* [Colossians 1](col/1.xhtml)
* [Colossians 2](col/2.xhtml)
* [Colossians 3](col/3.xhtml)
* [Colossians 4](col/4.xhtml)

Chapter 1

Redemption comes through Christ—He created all things, is in the image of God, and is the Firstborn of the Father.

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timotheus our brother,

2 To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,

4 Since we heard of your [afaith](#note4a) in Christ Jesus, and of the [blove](#note4b) which ye have to all the saints,

5 For the [ahope](#note5a) which is laid up for you in heaven, whereof ye heard before in the word of the truth of the [bgospel](#note5b);

6 Which is come unto you, [aas](#note6a) it is in all the world; and bringeth forth [bfruit](#note6b), as it doth also in you, since the day ye heard of it, and knew the [cgrace](#note6c) of God in truth:

7 As ye also learned of [aEpaphras](#note7a) our dear fellowservant, who is for you a faithful minister of Christ;

8 Who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.

9 For this cause we also, since the day we heard it, do not cease to pray for you, and to desire that ye might be filled with the [aknowledge](#note9a) of his [bwill](#note9b) in all wisdom and spiritual [cunderstanding](#note9c);

10 That ye might [awalk](#note10a) [bworthy](#note10b) of the Lord unto all [cpleasing](#note10c), being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the [dknowledge](#note10d) of God;

11 [aStrengthened](#note11a) with all might, according to his [bglorious](#note11b) [cpower](#note11c), unto all patience and longsuffering with [djoyfulness](#note11d);

12 Giving [athanks](#note12a) unto the Father, which hath [bmade us meet](#note12b) to be partakers of the [cinheritance](#note12c) of the saints in light:

13 Who hath delivered us from the power of [adarkness](#note13a), and hath translated us into the [bkingdom](#note13b) of his dear [cSon](#note13c):

14 In whom we have [aredemption](#note14a) through his blood, even the [bforgiveness](#note14b) of sins:

15 Who is the [aimage](#note15a) of the invisible [bGod](#note15b), the [cfirstborn](#note15c) of [devery creature](#note15d):

16 For by him were all [athings](#note16a) [bcreated](#note16b), that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether they be thrones, or dominions, or [cprincipalities](#note16c), or powers: all things were [dcreated](#note16d) by him, and for him:

17 And he is before all things, and by him all things [aconsist](#note17a).

18 And he is the [ahead](#note18a) of the body, the church: who is the beginning, the [bfirstborn](#note18b) from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.

19 For it pleased the Father that in him should all [afulness](#note19a) dwell;

20 And, having made peace through the blood of his cross, by him to [areconcile](#note20a) all things unto himself; by him, I say, whether they be things in earth, or things in heaven.

21 And you, that were [asometime](#note21a) [balienated](#note21b) and enemies in your mind by wicked works, yet now hath he reconciled

22 In the body of his flesh through [adeath](#note22a), to present you holy and [bunblameable](#note22b) and unreproveable in his sight:

23 If ye [acontinue](#note23a) in the faith [bgrounded and settled](#note23b), and be not [cmoved](#note23c) away from the [dhope](#note23d) of the gospel, which ye have heard, and which was preached to every creature which is under heaven; whereof I Paul am made a minister;

24 Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is behind of the [aafflictions](#note24a) of Christ in my flesh for his body’s sake, which is the church:

25 Whereof I am made a minister, according to the [adispensation](#note25a) of God which is given to me for you, to fulfil the word of God;

26 Even the [amystery](#note26a) which hath been hid from ages and from generations, but now is made [bmanifest](#note26b) to his saints:

27 To whom God would make [aknown](#note27a) what is the [briches](#note27b) of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you, the hope of glory:

28 Whom we [apreach](#note28a), [bwarning](#note28b) every man, and teaching every man in all wisdom; that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus:

29 Whereunto I also labour, striving according to his working, which worketh in me mightily.

Chapter 2

The fulness of the Godhead dwells in Christ—Beware of being deceived by the traditions of men—The handwriting against us was nailed to the cross of Christ.

1 For I would that ye knew what great [aconflict](#note1a) I have for you, and for them at [bLaodicea](#note1b), and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;

2 That their hearts might be comforted, being knit together in love, and unto all riches of the full assurance of [aunderstanding](#note2a), to the acknowledgement of the mystery of God, [band](#note2b) of the Father, and of Christ;

3 In whom are hid all the [atreasures](#note3a) of [bwisdom](#note3b) and [cknowledge](#note3c).

4 And this I say, lest any man should beguile you with enticing words.

5 For though I be absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, joying and beholding your [aorder](#note5a), and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.

6 As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so [awalk](#note6a) ye in him:

7 [aRooted](#note7a) and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, as ye have been taught, abounding therein with [bthanksgiving](#note7b).

8 Beware lest any man [aspoil](#note8a) you through [bphilosophy](#note8b) and vain [cdeceit](#note8c), after the [dtradition](#note8d) of men, after the rudiments of the [eworld](#note8e), and not after Christ.

9 For in him dwelleth all the [afulness](#note9a) of the Godhead bodily.

10 And ye are [acomplete](#note10a) in him, which is the head of all [bprincipality](#note10b) and power:

11 In whom also ye are circumcised with the [acircumcision](#note11a) made without hands, in putting off the [bbody](#note11b) of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ:

12 [aBuried](#note12a) with him in [bbaptism](#note12b), wherein also ye are [crisen](#note12c) with him through the faith of the [doperation](#note12d) of God, who hath raised him from the dead.

13 And you, being dead in your sins and the uncircumcision of your flesh, hath he [aquickened together](#note13a) with him, having forgiven you all trespasses;

14 [aBlotting](#note14a) out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us, which was contrary to us, and took it out of the way, nailing it to his cross;

15 And having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it.

16 Let no man therefore [ajudge](#note16a) you in [bmeat](#note16b), or in drink, or in respect of an holyday, or of the new moon, or of the [csabbath](#note16c) days:

17 Which are a [ashadow](#note17a) of things to come; but the body is of Christ.

18 Let no man [abeguile](#note18a) you of your reward in a voluntary [bhumility](#note18b) and worshipping of angels, intruding into those things which he hath not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,

19 And not holding the [aHead](#note19a), from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.

20 Wherefore if ye be [adead](#note20a) with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are ye subject to [bordinances](#note20b),

21 [a(Touch](#note21a) not; taste not; handle not;

22 Which all are to perish with the using;) after the [acommandments](#note22a) and [bdoctrines](#note22b) of men?

23 Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom in will worship, and humility, and neglecting of the body; not in any honour to the satisfying of the flesh.

Chapter 3

Some lives are hidden with God in Christ—The Saints are told to be holy and to serve the Lord Jesus Christ.

1 If ye then be [arisen](#note1a) with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.

2 Set your [aaffection](#note2a) on [bthings](#note2b) [cabove](#note2c), not on things on the [dearth](#note2d).

3 For ye are [adead](#note3a), and your life is hid with Christ in God.

4 When Christ, who is our [alife](#note4a), shall [bappear](#note4b), then shall ye also appear with him in [cglory](#note4c).

5 [aMortify](#note5a) therefore your [bmembers](#note5b) which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, [cinordinate affection](#note5c), evil concupiscence, and [dcovetousness](#note5d), which is [eidolatry](#note5e):

6 For which things’ sake the [awrath](#note6a) of God cometh on the children of [bdisobedience](#note6b):

7 In the which ye also walked [asome time](#note7a), when ye lived in them.

8 But now ye also [aput](#note8a) off all these; [banger](#note8b), wrath, [cmalice](#note8c), [dblasphemy](#note8d), filthy [ecommunication](#note8e) out of your mouth.

9 [aLie](#note9a) not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the [bold](#note9b) man with his deeds;

10 And have put on the [anew man](#note10a), which is renewed in knowledge after the [bimage](#note10b) of him that [ccreated](#note10c) him:

11 Where there is neither [aGreek](#note11a) nor Jew, [bcircumcision](#note11b) nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free: but Christ is all, and in all.

12 Put on therefore, as the [aelect](#note12a) of God, holy and beloved, [bbowels](#note12b) of [cmercies](#note12c), [dkindness](#note12d), [ehumbleness](#note12e) of mind, [fmeekness](#note12f), longsuffering;

13 [aForbearing](#note13a) one another, and [bforgiving](#note13b) one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.

14 And above all these things put on [acharity](#note14a), which is the bond of perfectness.

15 And let the [apeace](#note15a) of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye [bthankful](#note15b).

16 Let the [aword](#note16a) of Christ dwell in you richly in all [bwisdom](#note16b); [cteaching](#note16c) and admonishing one another in psalms and [dhymns](#note16d) and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.

17 And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, [ado](#note17a) all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving [bthanks](#note17b) to God and the Father by him.

18 [aWives](#note18a), submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.

19 [aHusbands](#note19a), [blove](#note19b) your wives, and be not bitter against them.

20 [aChildren](#note20a), [bobey](#note20b) your parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord.

21 [aFathers](#note21a), [bprovoke](#note21b) not your children to [canger](#note21c), lest they be discouraged.

22 [aServants](#note22a), obey in all things your masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in [bsingleness](#note22b) of heart, [cfearing](#note22c) God:

23 And whatsoever ye do, do it [aheartily](#note23a), as to the Lord, and not unto men;

24 Knowing that of the Lord ye shall [areceive](#note24a) the [breward](#note24b) of the [cinheritance](#note24c): for ye [dserve](#note24d) the Lord Christ.

25 But he that doeth [awrong](#note25a) shall receive for the wrong which he hath done: and there is no [brespect](#note25b) of persons.

Chapter 4

The Saints are told to be wise in all things—Luke and others greet the Colossians.

1 [aMasters](#note1a), give unto your servants that which is just and equal; knowing that ye also have a [bMaster](#note1b) in [cheaven](#note1c).

2 Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving;

3 Withal praying also for us, that God would open unto us a [adoor](#note3a) of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds:

4 That I may make it manifest, as I ought to speak.

5 [aWalk](#note5a) in [bwisdom](#note5b) toward them that are without, redeeming the time.

6 Let your [aspeech](#note6a) be alway with grace, seasoned with [bsalt](#note6b), that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.

7 All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, who is a beloved brother, and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the Lord:

8 Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that he might know your estate, and comfort your hearts;

9 With [aOnesimus](#note9a), a faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They shall make known unto you all things which are done here.

10 [aAristarchus](#note10a) my fellowprisoner saluteth you, and [bMarcus](#note10b), [csister’s son](#note10c) to Barnabas, (touching whom ye received commandments: if he come unto you, receive him;)

11 And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the [acircumcision](#note11a). These only are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God, which have been a comfort unto me.

12 [aEpaphras](#note12a), who is one of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers, that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.

13 For I bear him record, that he hath a great [azeal](#note13a) for you, and them that are in Laodicea, and them in Hierapolis.

14 Luke, the beloved physician, and [aDemas](#note14a), greet you.

15 Salute the brethren which are in [aLaodicea](#note15a), and Nymphas, and the church which is in his house.

16 And when this epistle is read among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and that ye likewise read the [aepistle](#note16a) from Laodicea.

17 And say to [aArchippus](#note17a), Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.

18 The salutation by the [ahand](#note18a) of me Paul. Remember my [bbonds](#note18b). Grace be with you. Amen.

¶ Written from Rome to the Colossians by Tychicus and Onesimus.

# The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Thessalonians

* [1 Thessalonians 1](1-thes/1.xhtml)
* [1 Thessalonians 2](1-thes/2.xhtml)
* [1 Thessalonians 3](1-thes/3.xhtml)
* [1 Thessalonians 4](1-thes/4.xhtml)
* [1 Thessalonians 5](1-thes/5.xhtml)

¶ The first epistle unto the Thessalonians was written from [Athens.\*](#note_asterisk)

* \*

  More recent scholarship concludes that 1 and 2 Thes. were most likely written from Corinth.

Chapter 1

The gospel comes both in word and in power.

1 Paul, and [aSilvanus](#note1a), and [bTimotheus](#note1b), [cunto](#note1c) the [dchurch](#note1d) of the [eThessalonians](#note1e) which is in God the Father and in the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.

2 We give [athanks](#note2a) to God always for you all, making mention of you in our prayers;

3 Remembering without ceasing your work of [afaith](#note3a), and [blabour](#note3b) of love, and [cpatience](#note3c) of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, in the sight of God and our Father;

4 Knowing, brethren [abeloved](#note4a), your [belection](#note4b) of God.

5 For our [agospel](#note5a) [bcame](#note5b) not unto you in [cword](#note5c) only, but also in [dpower](#note5d), and in the [eHoly Ghost](#note5e), and in much assurance; as ye know what [fmanner](#note5f) of men we were among you for your sake.

6 And ye became [afollowers](#note6a) of us, and of the Lord, having received the word in much [baffliction](#note6b), with [cjoy](#note6c) of the Holy Ghost:

7 So that ye were [aensamples](#note7a) to all that believe in Macedonia and Achaia.

8 For from you sounded out the word of the Lord not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every [aplace](#note8a) your faith to God-ward is spread abroad; so that we need not to speak any thing.

9 For they themselves shew of us what manner of entering in we had unto you, and how ye turned to God from [aidols](#note9a) to serve the [bliving](#note9b) and true God;

10 And to wait for his Son from [aheaven](#note10a), whom he raised from the dead, even Jesus, which [bdelivered](#note10b) us from the [cwrath](#note10c) to come.

Chapter 2

True ministers preach in a godly manner—Converts are the glory and joy of missionaries.

1 For yourselves, brethren, know our [aentrance](#note1a) in unto you, that it was not in vain:

2 But even after that we had suffered before, and were shamefully [aentreated](#note2a), as ye know, at Philippi, we were bold in our God to [bspeak](#note2b) unto you the gospel of God with much contention.

3 For our exhortation was not of [adeceit](#note3a), nor of uncleanness, nor in [bguile](#note3b):

4 But as we were [aallowed](#note4a) of God to be [bput](#note4b) in [ctrust](#note4c) with the gospel, even so we [dspeak](#note4d); not as [epleasing](#note4e) men, but God, which [ftrieth our hearts](#note4f).

5 For neither at any time used we [aflattering](#note5a) words, as ye know, nor a cloak of [bcovetousness](#note5b); God is witness:

6 Nor of men sought we [aglory](#note6a), neither of you, nor yet of others, when we might have been burdensome, as the apostles of Christ.

7 But we were gentle among you, even as a nurse cherisheth her children:

8 So being affectionately desirous of you, we were willing to have imparted unto you, not the gospel of God only, but also our own [asouls](#note8a), because ye were dear unto us.

9 For ye remember, brethren, our labour and [atravail](#note9a): for [blabouring](#note9b) night and day, because we would not be [cchargeable](#note9c) unto any of you, we preached unto you the gospel of God.

10 Ye are [awitnesses](#note10a), and God also, how holily and justly and unblameably we behaved ourselves among you that believe:

11 As ye know how we exhorted and comforted and charged every one of you, as a father doth his [achildren](#note11a),

12 That ye would [awalk](#note12a) [bworthy](#note12b) of God, who hath called you unto his kingdom and glory.

13 For this cause also [athank](#note13a) we God without ceasing, because, when ye received the word of God which ye heard of us, ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which effectually worketh also in you that believe.

14 For ye, brethren, became [afollowers](#note14a) of the churches of God which in Judæa are in Christ Jesus: for ye also have [bsuffered](#note14b) like things of your own countrymen, even as they have of the Jews:

15 Who both [akilled](#note15a) the Lord Jesus, and their own prophets, and have persecuted us; and they please not God, and are contrary to all men:

16 [aForbidding](#note16a) us to speak to the Gentiles that they might be saved, to fill up their sins [balway](#note16b): for the wrath is come upon them to the uttermost.

17 But we, brethren, being taken from you for a short time in presence, not in heart, endeavoured the more abundantly to see your [aface](#note17a) with great desire.

18 Wherefore we would have come unto you, even I Paul, once and again; but Satan hindered us.

19 For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Are not even ye in the presence of our Lord Jesus Christ at his [acoming](#note19a)?

20 For ye are our glory and joy.

Chapter 3

The Saints are told to perfect that which is lacking in their faith.

1 Wherefore when we could no longer forbear, we thought it good to be left at [aAthens](#note1a) alone;

2 And sent [aTimotheus](#note2a), our brother, and minister of God, and our fellowlabourer in the gospel of Christ, to [bestablish](#note2b) you, and to comfort you concerning your faith:

3 That no man should be [amoved](#note3a) by these [bafflictions](#note3b): for yourselves know that we are [cappointed](#note3c) thereunto.

4 For verily, when we were with you, we told you before that we should suffer tribulation; even as it came to pass, and ye know.

5 For this cause, when I could no longer forbear, I sent to know your faith, lest by some means the [atempter](#note5a) have [btempted](#note5b) you, and our labour be in vain.

6 But now when Timotheus came from you unto us, and brought us good tidings of your faith and charity, and that ye have good remembrance of us always, desiring greatly to see us, as we also to see you:

7 Therefore, brethren, we were [acomforted](#note7a) over you in all our [baffliction](#note7b) and distress by your faith:

8 For now we live, if ye stand fast in the Lord.

9 For what thanks can we render to God again for you, for all the [ajoy](#note9a) wherewith we joy for your sakes before our God;

10 Night and day praying exceedingly that we might see your [aface](#note10a), and might [bperfect](#note10b) that which is lacking in your faith?

11 Now God himself and our Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way unto you.

12 And the Lord make you to increase and [aabound](#note12a) in [blove](#note12b) one toward another, and toward all men, even as we do toward you:

13 To the end he may stablish your hearts unblameable in holiness before God, even our Father, at the [acoming](#note13a) of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his [bsaints](#note13b).

Chapter 4

The Saints are told to be holy, sanctify themselves, and love one another—The Lord will come, and the dead will rise.

1 Furthermore then we beseech you, brethren, and exhort you by the Lord Jesus, that as ye have [areceived](#note1a) of us how ye ought to [bwalk](#note1b) and to [cplease](#note1c) God, so ye would [dabound](#note1d) more and more.

2 For ye know what commandments [awe](#note2a) gave you by the Lord Jesus.

3 For this is the will of God, even your [asanctification](#note3a), that ye should [babstain](#note3b) from [cfornication](#note3c):

4 That every one of you should know how to possess his [avessel](#note4a) in [bsanctification](#note4b) and honour;

5 Not in the [alust of concupiscence](#note5a), even as the [bGentiles](#note5b) which [cknow not](#note5c) God:

6 That no man [ago beyond and defraud](#note6a) his brother in [bany matter](#note6b): because that the Lord is the [cavenger](#note6c) of all such, as we also have forewarned you and testified.

7 For God hath not called us unto [auncleanness](#note7a), but unto holiness.

8 He therefore that [adespiseth](#note8a), [bdespiseth](#note8b) not man, but God, who hath also given unto us his [choly Spirit](#note8c).

9 But as touching [abrotherly love](#note9a) ye need not that I write unto you: for ye yourselves are [btaught](#note9b) of God to [clove](#note9c) one another.

10 And indeed ye do it toward all the brethren which are in all Macedonia: but we beseech you, brethren, that ye increase more and more;

11 And that ye [astudy](#note11a) to be quiet, and to do your own business, and to [bwork](#note11b) with your own [chands](#note11c), as we commanded you;

12 That ye may walk [ahonestly](#note12a) toward them that are without, and that ye may have lack of nothing.

13 But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep, that ye [asorrow](#note13a) not, even as others which have no [bhope](#note13b).

14 For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so them also which [asleep](#note14a) in Jesus will God bring with him.

15 For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, [athat](#note15a) [bwe](#note15b) which are alive and remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not [cprevent](#note15c) them which are asleep.

16 For the Lord himself shall [adescend](#note16a) from heaven with [ba shout](#note16b), with the voice of the [carchangel](#note16c), and with the [dtrump](#note16d) of God: and the dead in Christ shall [erise](#note16e) first:

17 [aThen](#note17a) we which are alive and remain shall be [bcaught up](#note17b) together with them in the clouds, to meet the [cLord](#note17c) in the [dair](#note17d): and so shall we [eever](#note17e) be with the [fLord](#note17f).

18 Wherefore comfort one another with these words.

Chapter 5

The Saints will know the season of the Second Coming of Christ—Live the way Saints should live—Rejoice evermore—Do not despise prophesyings.

1 But of the times and the seasons, brethren, ye have no need that I write unto you.

2 For yourselves know perfectly that the [aday](#note2a) of the Lord so cometh as a [bthief](#note2b) in the night.

3 For when they shall say, Peace and [asafety](#note3a); then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not [bescape](#note3b).

4 But ye, brethren, are not in [adarkness](#note4a), that that [bday](#note4b) should overtake you as a thief.

5 Ye are all the [achildren](#note5a) of light, and the children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness.

6 Therefore let us not [asleep](#note6a), as do others; but let us [bwatch](#note6b) and be [csober](#note6c).

7 For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night.

8 But let us, who are of the day, be [asober](#note8a), putting on the [bbreastplate](#note8b) of [cfaith](#note8c) and love; and for an helmet, the [dhope](#note8d) of salvation.

9 For God hath not appointed us to [awrath](#note9a), but to obtain [bsalvation](#note9b) by our Lord Jesus Christ,

10 Who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should [alive](#note10a) together with him.

11 Wherefore [acomfort](#note11a) yourselves together, and [bedify](#note11b) one another, even as also ye do.

12 And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which [alabour](#note12a) among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you;

13 And to [aesteem](#note13a) them very highly in love for their work’s sake. And be at [bpeace](#note13b) among yourselves.

14 Now we exhort you, brethren, [awarn](#note14a) them that are [bunruly](#note14b), [ccomfort](#note14c) the [dfeebleminded](#note14d), [esupport](#note14e) the [fweak](#note14f), be patient toward all men.

15 See that none [arender](#note15a) [bevil](#note15b) for evil unto any [cman](#note15c); but ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all men.

16 [aRejoice](#note16a) evermore.

17 [aPray](#note17a) without ceasing.

18 In every thing give [athanks](#note18a): for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.

19 [aQuench](#note19a) not the [bSpirit](#note19b).

20 Despise not prophesyings.

21 [aProve](#note21a) all things; hold fast that which is good.

22 [aAbstain](#note22a) from all [bappearance](#note22b) of [cevil](#note22c).

23 And the very God of peace [asanctify](#note23a) you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

24 Faithful is he that calleth you, who also will do it.

25 Brethren, pray for us.

26 Greet all the brethren with an holy [akiss](#note26a).

27 I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.

28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

* \*

  More recent scholarship concludes that 1 and 2 Thes. were most likely written from Corinth.

# The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Thessalonians

* [2 Thessalonians 1](2-thes/1.xhtml)
* [2 Thessalonians 2](2-thes/2.xhtml)
* [2 Thessalonians 3](2-thes/3.xhtml)

¶ The second epistle to the Thessalonians was written from [Athens.\*](#note_asterisk)

* \*

  More recent scholarship concludes that 1 and 2 Thes. were most likely written from Corinth.

Chapter 1

At His Second Coming, the Lord Jesus will take vengeance upon the ungodly.

1 Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, [aunto](#note1a) the [bchurch](#note1b) of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:

2 Grace unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 We are bound to thank God always for you, brethren, as it is meet, because that your faith groweth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you all toward each other aboundeth;

4 So that we ourselves glory in you in the [achurches](#note4a) of God for your [bpatience](#note4b) and faith in all your [cpersecutions](#note4c) and tribulations that ye endure:

5 Which is a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God, that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which ye also [asuffer](#note5a):

6 Seeing it is a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you;

7 And to you who are troubled [arest](#note7a) with us, when the [bLord](#note7b) Jesus shall be [crevealed](#note7c) from heaven with his mighty [dangels](#note7d),

8 In flaming [afire](#note8a) taking [bvengeance](#note8b) on them that know not God, and that [cobey not](#note8c) the [dgospel](#note8d) of our Lord Jesus Christ:

9 Who shall be [apunished](#note9a) with [beverlasting](#note9b) [cdestruction](#note9c) from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power;

10 When he shall come to be [aglorified](#note10a) in his [bsaints](#note10b), and to be admired in all them that believe (because our testimony among you was believed) in that day.

11 Wherefore also we pray always for you, that our God would count you [aworthy](#note11a) of this calling, and fulfil all the good pleasure of his goodness, and the work of faith with power:

12 That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and ye in him, according to the [agrace](#note12a) of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.

Chapter 2

Apostasy is to precede the Second Coming—The gospel prepares men for eternal glory.

1 Now we beseech you, brethren, [aby](#note1a) the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and by our gathering together unto him,

2 That ye be not soon shaken in mind, [aor](#note2a) be troubled, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by [bletter](#note2b) as from us, as that the [cday](#note2c) of Christ is at hand.

3 Let no man deceive you by any means: [afor](#note3a) that day shall not come, except there come a [bfalling away](#note3b) first, and that [cman](#note3c) of [dsin](#note3d) be revealed, the son of perdition;

4 Who [aopposeth](#note4a) and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God.

5 Remember ye not, that, when I was yet with you, I told you these things?

6 And now ye know [awhat withholdeth](#note6a) that he might be [brevealed](#note6b) in his time.

7 [aFor](#note7a) the [bmystery](#note7b) of [ciniquity](#note7c) doth already work: only [dhe](#note7d) who now letteth will let, until he be taken out of the way.

8 And then shall that [aWicked](#note8a) be revealed, whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his [bcoming](#note8b):

9 Even him, whose coming is after the working of Satan with all [apower](#note9a) and [bsigns](#note9b) and lying wonders,

10 And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish; because they received not the love of the truth, that they might be saved.

11 And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion, that they should believe a lie:

12 That they all might be [adamned](#note12a) who [bbelieved not](#note12b) the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.

13 But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you, brethren beloved of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning [achosen](#note13a) you to salvation through [bsanctification](#note13b) of the Spirit and belief of the truth:

14 Whereunto he called you by our [agospel](#note14a), to the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.

15 Therefore, brethren, stand fast, and hold the traditions which ye have been taught, whether by word, or our epistle.

16 Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God, even our Father, which hath [aloved](#note16a) us, and hath given us everlasting [bconsolation](#note16b) and good hope through [cgrace](#note16c),

17 Comfort your hearts, and stablish you in every good word and work.

Chapter 3

Pray for the triumph of the gospel cause—Paul preaches the gospel of work—Be not weary in well-doing.

1 Finally, brethren, pray for us, that the word of the Lord [amay have free course](#note1a), and be glorified, even as it is with you:

2 And that we may be delivered from [aunreasonable](#note2a) and wicked men: for all men have not faith.

3 But the Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from [aevil](#note3a).

4 And we have [aconfidence](#note4a) in the Lord touching you, that ye both do and will do the things which we command you.

5 And the Lord [adirect](#note5a) your hearts into the [blove](#note5b) of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ.

6 Now we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye [awithdraw](#note6a) yourselves from every brother that walketh [bdisorderly](#note6b), and not after the tradition which he received of us.

7 For yourselves know how ye ought to [afollow](#note7a) us: for we [bbehaved](#note7b) not ourselves disorderly among you;

8 Neither did we eat any man’s bread [afor nought](#note8a); but wrought with [blabour](#note8b) and travail night and day, that we might not be chargeable to any of you:

9 Not because we have not [apower](#note9a), but to make ourselves an [bensample](#note9b) unto you to follow us.

10 For even when we were with you, this we commanded you, that if any would not [awork](#note10a), neither should he eat.

11 For we hear that there are some which walk among you [adisorderly](#note11a), working not at all, but are busybodies.

12 Now them that are such we command and exhort by our Lord Jesus Christ, that with quietness they work, and eat their own [abread](#note12a).

13 But ye, brethren, be not [aweary](#note13a) in well doing.

14 And if any man obey not our word by this epistle, note that man, and have no company with him, that he may be ashamed.

15 Yet count him not as an [aenemy](#note15a), but [badmonish](#note15b) him as a [cbrother](#note15c).

16 Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord be with you all.

17 The salutation of Paul with mine own [ahand](#note17a), which is the token in every epistle: so I write.

18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

* \*

  More recent scholarship concludes that 1 and 2 Thes. were most likely written from Corinth.

# The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Timothy

* [1 Timothy 1](1-tim/1.xhtml)
* [1 Timothy 2](1-tim/2.xhtml)
* [1 Timothy 3](1-tim/3.xhtml)
* [1 Timothy 4](1-tim/4.xhtml)
* [1 Timothy 5](1-tim/5.xhtml)

Chapter 1

Counsel is given to teach true doctrine only—Christ came to save repentant sinners.

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, which is our hope;

2 Unto [aTimothy](#note2a), [bmy](#note2b) [cown](#note2c) son in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.

3 As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus, when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they [ateach](#note3a) no [bother](#note3b) doctrine,

4 Neither give heed to [afables](#note4a) and endless genealogies, which [bminister](#note4b) [cquestions](#note4c), rather than godly [dedifying](#note4d) which is in faith: so do.

5 Now the end of the [acommandment](#note5a) is [bcharity](#note5b) out of a [cpure](#note5c) heart, and of a good conscience, and of faith unfeigned:

6 From which some having [aswerved](#note6a) have [bturned](#note6b) aside unto [cvain jangling](#note6c);

7 Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they [aaffirm](#note7a).

8 But we know that the law is good, if a man use it lawfully;

9 Knowing this, that the [alaw](#note9a) is not made for a [brighteous](#note9b) man, but for the [clawless](#note9c) and [ddisobedient](#note9d), for the ungodly and for sinners, for unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,

10 For [awhoremongers](#note10a), for [bthem](#note10b) that [cdefile](#note10c) themselves with mankind, for [dmenstealers](#note10d), for liars, for perjured persons, and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound [edoctrine](#note10e);

11 According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my [atrust](#note11a).

12 And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who hath [aenabled](#note12a) me, for that he counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry;

13 Who was before a [ablasphemer](#note13a), and a [bpersecutor](#note13b), and [cinjurious](#note13c): but I obtained [dmercy](#note13d), because I did it [eignorantly](#note13e) in unbelief.

14 And the [agrace](#note14a) of our Lord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

15 This is a faithful [asaying](#note15a), and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to [bsave](#note15b) [csinners](#note15c); of whom I am chief.

16 Howbeit for this cause I obtained [amercy](#note16a), that in me first Jesus Christ might shew forth all [blongsuffering](#note16b), for a [cpattern](#note16c) to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting.

17 Now unto the [aKing](#note17a) eternal, [bimmortal](#note17b), invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.

18 This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the [aprophecies](#note18a) which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare;

19 [aHolding](#note19a) faith, and a good conscience; which some having put away concerning faith have made [bshipwreck](#note19b):

20 Of whom is [aHymenæus](#note20a) and [bAlexander](#note20b); whom I have [cdelivered](#note20c) unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme.
