Chapter 2

We should pray for all people—Christ is our Mediator—Women should dress modestly—Women are blessed in childbearing and are admonished to continue in faith, charity, and holiness.

1 I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks, be made for all men;

2 For [akings](#note2a), and for all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and [bpeaceable](#note2b) [clife](#note2c) in all godliness and [dhonesty](#note2d).

3 For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;

4 [aWho](#note4a) [bwill have](#note4b) [call](#note4c) men to be [dsaved](#note4d), and to come unto the knowledge of the truth.

5 For there is [aone God](#note5a), and one [bmediator](#note5b) between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;

6 Who [agave](#note6a) himself a [bransom](#note6b) for all, to be [ctestified](#note6c) in due time.

7 Whereunto I am [aordained](#note7a) a [bpreacher](#note7b), and an [capostle](#note7c), (I speak the truth in Christ, and [dlie](#note7d) not;) a teacher of the [eGentiles](#note7e) in faith and [fverity](#note7f).

8 I [awill](#note8a) therefore that men [bpray](#note8b) every where, lifting up holy [chands](#note8c), without wrath and [ddoubting](#note8d).

9 In like manner also, that [awomen](#note9a) adorn themselves in [bmodest](#note9b) [capparel](#note9c), with [dshamefacedness](#note9d) and sobriety; not with [ebroided](#note9e) hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array;

10 But (which becometh women professing [agodliness](#note10a)) with good works.

11 Let the woman learn in [asilence](#note11a) with all subjection.

12 But I suffer not a woman to [ateach](#note12a), nor to [busurp authority](#note12b) over the man, but to be in [csilence](#note12c).

13 For Adam was first [aformed](#note13a), then Eve.

14 And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being [adeceived](#note14a) was in the [btransgression](#note14b).

15 Notwithstanding [ashe](#note15a) shall be saved in [bchildbearing](#note15b), if they continue in faith and charity and [choliness](#note15c) with [dsobriety](#note15d).

Chapter 3

Qualifications are given for bishops and deacons—Great is the mystery of godliness.

1 This is a true [asaying](#note1a), If a man desire the office of a [bbishop](#note1b), he desireth a good work.

2 A bishop then must be [ablameless](#note2a), the husband of one wife, vigilant, [bsober](#note2b), of good behaviour, given to [chospitality](#note2c), apt to [dteach](#note2d);

3 Not given to [awine](#note3a), no [bstriker](#note3b), not greedy of [cfilthy](#note3c) lucre; but patient, not a brawler, not covetous;

4 One that [aruleth](#note4a) well his own [bhouse](#note4b), having his [cchildren](#note4c) in subjection with all gravity;

5 (For if a man know not how to [arule](#note5a) his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?)

6 Not a [anovice](#note6a), lest being lifted up with [bpride](#note6b) he fall into the [ccondemnation](#note6c) of the devil.

7 Moreover he must have a good [areport](#note7a) of them which are [bwithout](#note7b); lest he fall into reproach and the [csnare](#note7c) of the devil.

8 Likewise must the [adeacons](#note8a) be [bgrave](#note8b), not [cdoubletongued](#note8c), not given to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre;

9 Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure [aconscience](#note9a).

10 And let these also first be [aproved](#note10a); then let them use the office of a deacon, being found blameless.

11 [aEven](#note11a) so must their [bwives](#note11b) be [cgrave](#note11c), not [dslanderers](#note11d), [esober](#note11e), [ffaithful](#note11f) in all things.

12 Let the deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.

13 For they that have used the office of a deacon well [apurchase](#note13a) to themselves a good degree, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.

14 These things write I unto thee, hoping to come unto thee shortly:

15 [aBut](#note15a) if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the [bchurch](#note15b) of the living God, the pillar and [cground](#note15c) of the [dtruth](#note15d).

16 And without controversy great is the [amystery](#note16a) of godliness: [bGod](#note16b) was [cmanifest](#note16c) in the [dflesh](#note16d), [ejustified in the](#note16e) Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, [freceived](#note16f) up into glory.

Chapter 4

Paul describes the latter-day apostasy—Christ is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe.

1 Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the [alatter times](#note1a) some shall [bdepart](#note1b) from the faith, giving heed to [cseducing](#note1c) spirits, and [ddoctrines of devils](#note1d);

2 Speaking [alies](#note2a) in [bhypocrisy](#note2b); having their [cconscience](#note2c) seared with a hot iron;

3 [aForbidding](#note3a) to [bmarry](#note3b), and commanding to [cabstain](#note3c) from [dmeats](#note3d), which God hath [ecreated](#note3e) to be received with [fthanksgiving](#note3f) of them which believe and know the truth.

4 For every [acreature](#note4a) of God is [bgood](#note4b), and nothing to be refused, if it be received with [cthanksgiving](#note4c):

5 For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.

6 If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, [awhereunto thou hast attained](#note6a).

7 But refuse profane and old wives’ fables, and exercise thyself rather unto godliness.

8 For bodily exercise profiteth [alittle](#note8a): but [bgodliness](#note8b) is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.

9 This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.

10 For therefore we both [alabour](#note10a) and suffer [breproach](#note10b), because we [ctrust](#note10c) in the living God, who is the [dSaviour](#note10d) of all men, specially of those that [ebelieve](#note10e).

11 These things command and teach.

12 Let no man [adespise](#note12a) thy youth; but be thou an [bexample](#note12b) of the believers, in word, in [cconversation](#note12c), in charity, in spirit, in faith, in [dpurity](#note12d).

13 Till I come, give attendance to [areading](#note13a), to exhortation, to doctrine.

14 [aNeglect](#note14a) not the [bgift](#note14b) that is in thee, which was given thee by [cprophecy](#note14c), with the [dlaying](#note14d) on of the [ehands](#note14e) of the [fpresbytery](#note14f).

15 [aMeditate](#note15a) upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy [bprofiting](#note15b) may [cappear to all](#note15c).

16 Take heed unto thyself, and unto the doctrine; continue in them: for in doing this thou shalt both [asave](#note16a) thyself, and them that hear thee.

Chapter 5

Saints are to care for their worthy poor—Policies concerning elders are given.

1 [aRebuke](#note1a) not an elder, but [bentreat](#note1b) him as a father; and the younger men as brethren;

2 The elder women as mothers; the younger as [asisters](#note2a), with all purity.

3 Honour [awidows](#note3a) that are widows indeed.

4 But if any widow have [achildren](#note4a) or [bnephews](#note4b), let them learn first to shew [cpiety](#note4c) at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.

5 Now she that is a widow indeed, and [adesolate](#note5a), trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.

6 But she that liveth [ain](#note6a) [bpleasure](#note6b) is dead while she liveth.

7 And these things [agive in charge](#note7a), that they may be blameless.

8 But if any [aprovide](#note8a) not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.

9 Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years [aold](#note9a), having been the wife of one man,

10 Well reported of for good works; if she have brought up children, if she have [alodged](#note10a) strangers, if she have [bwashed](#note10b) the saints’ [cfeet](#note10c), if she have relieved the afflicted, if she have diligently followed every good work.

11 But the younger widows refuse: for when they have begun to wax wanton against Christ, they will marry;

12 Having [adamnation](#note12a), because they have cast off their first faith.

13 And withal they learn to be [aidle](#note13a), wandering about from house to house; and not only idle, but tattlers also and [bbusybodies](#note13b), speaking things which they ought not.

14 I [awill](#note14a) therefore that the younger [bwomen](#note14b) marry, bear [cchildren](#note14c), guide the [dhouse](#note14d), give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully.

15 For some are already turned aside after Satan.

16 If any man or woman that believeth have widows, let them [arelieve](#note16a) them, and let not the church be [bcharged](#note16b); that it may relieve them that are widows indeed.

17 Let the [aelders](#note17a) that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.

18 For the scripture saith, Thou shalt not [amuzzle](#note18a) the ox that treadeth out the corn. And, The [blabourer](#note18b) is worthy of his [creward](#note18c).

19 Against an elder receive not an accusation, but before two or three [awitnesses](#note19a).

20 Them that sin [arebuke](#note20a) before all, that others also may fear.

21 I charge thee before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect [aangels](#note21a), that thou observe these things [bwithout](#note21b) preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality.

22 [aLay](#note22a) [bhands](#note22b) suddenly on no [cman](#note22c), neither be [dpartaker](#note22d) of other men’s sins: keep thyself [epure](#note22e).

23 Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach’s sake and thine often infirmities.

24 Some men’s sins are open beforehand, going before to judgment; and some men they [afollow after](#note24a).

25 Likewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise cannot be hid.

Chapter 6

The love of money is the root of all evil—Fight the good fight of faith—Do not trust in worldly riches.

1 Let as many [aservants](#note1a) as are under the [byoke](#note1b) count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and his doctrine be not blasphemed.

2 And they that have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are [abrethren](#note2a); but rather do them [bservice](#note2b), because they are faithful and beloved, partakers of the benefit. These things teach and exhort.

3 If any man [ateach](#note3a) otherwise, and consent not to [bwholesome](#note3b) words, even the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness;

4 He is [aproud](#note4a), knowing nothing, but [bdoting](#note4b) about [cquestions](#note4c) and [dstrifes](#note4d) of words, whereof cometh [eenvy](#note4e), strife, railings, [fevil surmisings](#note4f),

5 Perverse [adisputings](#note5a) of men of corrupt minds, and destitute of the truth, supposing that [bgain](#note5b) is godliness: from such withdraw thyself.

6 But [agodliness](#note6a) with [bcontentment](#note6b) is great gain.

7 For we brought [anothing](#note7a) into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out.

8 And having [afood](#note8a) and raiment let us be therewith [bcontent](#note8b).

9 But [athey](#note9a) that will be [brich](#note9b) fall into [ctemptation](#note9c) and a snare, and into many foolish and hurtful [dlusts](#note9d), which drown men in destruction and [eperdition](#note9e).

10 For the [alove](#note10a) of [bmoney](#note10b) is the root of all evil: which while some [ccoveted](#note10c) after, they have [derred](#note10d) from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many [esorrows](#note10e).

11 But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after [arighteousness](#note11a), godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.

12 Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on [aeternal life](#note12a), whereunto thou art also called, and hast [bprofessed](#note12b) a good profession before many witnesses.

13 I give thee charge in the sight of God, who [aquickeneth](#note13a) all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before [bPontius Pilate](#note13b) [cwitnessed a good confession](#note13c);

14 That thou keep this commandment without spot, unrebukeable, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ:

15 [aWhich](#note15a) in his times he shall shew, who is the blessed and only [bPotentate](#note15b), the [cKing of kings](#note15c), and Lord of lords;

16 Who only hath [aimmortality](#note16a), dwelling in the [blight](#note16b) which no man can approach unto; whom no man hath [cseen](#note16c), nor can see: to whom be honour and power everlasting. Amen.

17 Charge them that are [arich](#note17a) in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain [briches](#note17b), but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;

18 That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to [adistribute](#note18a), willing to [bcommunicate](#note18b);

19 [aLaying](#note19a) up in [bstore](#note19b) for themselves a good foundation [cagainst](#note19c) the time to come, that they may lay hold on [deternal life](#note19d).

20 O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy [atrust](#note20a), avoiding profane and vain babblings, and [boppositions](#note20b) of science falsely so called:

21 Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen.

¶ The first to Timothy was written from Laodicea, which is the chiefest city of Phrygia Pacatiana.

# The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Timothy

* [2 Timothy 1](2-tim/1.xhtml)
* [2 Timothy 2](2-tim/2.xhtml)
* [2 Timothy 3](2-tim/3.xhtml)
* [2 Timothy 4](2-tim/4.xhtml)

Chapter 1

Christ brings immortality and eternal life through the gospel—Be strong in the faith.

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the [apromise](#note1a) of life which is in Christ Jesus,

2 To Timothy, my dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

3 I thank God, whom I serve from my forefathers with pure [aconscience](#note3a), that without ceasing I have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day;

4 Greatly desiring to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy;

5 When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother [aEunice](#note5a); and I am persuaded that in thee also.

6 Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou [astir up](#note6a) the [bgift](#note6b) of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my [chands](#note6c).

7 For God hath not given us the spirit of [afear](#note7a); but of [bpower](#note7b), and of [clove](#note7c), and of a sound mind.

8 Be not thou therefore [aashamed](#note8a) of the [btestimony](#note8b) of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;

9 Who hath saved us, and called us with an holy [acalling](#note9a), not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and [bgrace](#note9b), which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began,

10 But is now made [amanifest](#note10a) by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished [bdeath](#note10b), and hath brought [clife](#note10c) and [dimmortality](#note10d) to light through the gospel:

11 Whereunto I am [aappointed](#note11a) a preacher, and an [bapostle](#note11b), and a teacher of the Gentiles.

12 For the which cause I also suffer these things: nevertheless I am not [aashamed](#note12a): for I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed unto him against that day.

13 Hold fast the [aform](#note13a) of [bsound](#note13b) words, which thou hast heard of me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

14 That good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the Holy Ghost which [adwelleth](#note14a) in us.

15 This thou knowest, that all they which are in Asia be [aturned away](#note15a) from me; of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.

16 The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft [arefreshed](#note16a) me, and was not ashamed of my chain:

17 But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found me.

18 The Lord grant unto him that he may find [amercy](#note18a) of the Lord in that day: and in how many things he ministered unto me at Ephesus, thou knowest very well.

Chapter 2

Christ gives eternal glory to the elect—Shun contention and seek godliness.

1 Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.

2 And the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses, the same commit thou to faithful men, who shall be able to [ateach](#note2a) others also.

3 Thou therefore endure [ahardness](#note3a), as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.

4 No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier.

5 And if a man also [astrive](#note5a) for [bmasteries](#note5b), yet is he not [ccrowned](#note5c), except he strive lawfully.

6 The husbandman that laboureth must be first [apartaker](#note6a) of the fruits.

7 Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.

8 Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was [araised](#note8a) from the dead according to my [bgospel](#note8b):

9 Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.

10 Therefore I endure all things for the elect’s sakes, that they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.

11 It is a faithful saying: For if we be [adead](#note11a) with him, we shall also live with him:

12 If we [asuffer](#note12a), we shall also [breign](#note12b) with him: if we [cdeny](#note12c) him, he also will deny us:

13 If we [abelieve not](#note13a), yet he abideth faithful: he cannot [bdeny](#note13b) himself.

14 Of these things put them in remembrance, charging them before the Lord that they [astrive not about words](#note14a) to no profit, but to the subverting of the hearers.

15 [aStudy](#note15a) to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, [brightly dividing](#note15b) the word of truth.

16 But shun profane and [avain babblings](#note16a): for they will increase unto more [bungodliness](#note16b).

17 And their [aword](#note17a) will eat as doth a canker: of whom is [bHymenæus](#note17b) and Philetus;

18 Who concerning the truth have [aerred](#note18a), saying that the resurrection is past already; and overthrow the faith of some.

19 Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity.

20 But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of [aearth](#note20a); and some to honour, and some to dishonour.

21 If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a [avessel](#note21a) unto honour, [bsanctified](#note21b), and [cmeet](#note21c) for the master’s use, and prepared unto every good work.

22 Flee also youthful [alusts](#note22a): but follow [brighteousness](#note22b), faith, charity, [cpeace](#note22c), with them that call on the Lord out of a [dpure](#note22d) heart.

23 But [afoolish](#note23a) and unlearned [bquestions](#note23b) avoid, knowing that they do gender [cstrifes](#note23c).

24 And the servant of the Lord must not [astrive](#note24a); but be gentle unto all men, apt to [bteach](#note24b), patient,

25 In [ameekness](#note25a) [binstructing](#note25b) those that oppose themselves; if God [cperadventure](#note25c) will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;

26 And that they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil, who are taken [acaptive](#note26a) by him at his will.

Chapter 3

Paul describes the apostasy and perilous times of the last days—The scriptures guide man to salvation.

1 This know also, that in the [alast days](#note1a) perilous [btimes](#note1b) shall come.

2 For men shall be lovers of their own selves, [acovetous](#note2a), boasters, [bproud](#note2b), blasphemers, [cdisobedient](#note2c) to parents, [dunthankful](#note2d), unholy,

3 Without [anatural](#note3a) [baffection](#note3b), [ctrucebreakers](#note3c), [dfalse accusers](#note3d), [eincontinent](#note3e), fierce, despisers of those that are good,

4 [aTraitors](#note4a), [bheady](#note4b), [chighminded](#note4c), lovers of [dpleasures](#note4d) more than lovers of God;

5 Having a [aform](#note5a) of godliness, but [bdenying](#note5b) the power thereof: from such turn away.

6 For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly [awomen](#note6a) laden with sins, led away with divers [blusts](#note6b),

7 Ever [alearning](#note7a), and never able to come to the [bknowledge](#note7b) of the [ctruth](#note7c).

8 Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also [aresist](#note8a) the truth: men of [bcorrupt](#note8b) minds, reprobate concerning the faith.

9 But they shall proceed no further: for their [afolly](#note9a) shall be manifest unto all men, as theirs also was.

10 But thou hast [afully known](#note10a) my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, charity, patience,

11 Persecutions, afflictions, which came unto me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra; what [apersecutions](#note11a) I endured: but out of them all the Lord delivered me.

12 Yea, and all that will live [agodly](#note12a) in Christ Jesus shall [bsuffer](#note12b) [cpersecution](#note12c).

13 But evil men and [aseducers](#note13a) shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived.

14 But [acontinue](#note14a) thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of, knowing of whom thou hast learned them;

15 And that from a [achild](#note15a) thou hast known the holy [bscriptures](#note15b), which are able to make thee [cwise](#note15c) unto [dsalvation](#note15d) through faith which is in Christ Jesus.

16 [aAll](#note16a) [bscripture](#note16b) is given by [cinspiration](#note16c) of God, and is [dprofitable](#note16d) for [edoctrine](#note16e), for [freproof](#note16f), for correction, for [ginstruction](#note16g) in [hrighteousness](#note16h):

17 That the man of God may be [aperfect](#note17a), throughly furnished unto all [bgood works](#note17b).

Chapter 4

Paul gives a solemn charge to preach the gospel in a day of apostasy—Paul and all Saints are assured of exaltation.

1 I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall [ajudge](#note1a) the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;

2 [aPreach](#note2a) the word; [bbe](#note2b) [cinstant](#note2c) in season, out of season; [dreprove](#note2d), rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine.

3 For the time will come when they will not [aendure](#note3a) sound [bdoctrine](#note3b); but after their own [clusts](#note3c) shall they heap to themselves [dteachers](#note3d), having itching ears;

4 And they shall turn away their ears from the [atruth](#note4a), and shall be turned unto [bfables](#note4b).

5 But [awatch](#note5a) thou in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an [bevangelist](#note5b), [cmake](#note5c) full proof of thy ministry.

6 For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.

7 I have fought a good [afight](#note7a), I have [bfinished](#note7b) my course, I have kept the faith:

8 Henceforth there is laid up for me a [acrown](#note8a) of [brighteousness](#note8b), which the Lord, the righteous [cjudge](#note8c), shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.

9 Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:

10 For [aDemas](#note10a) hath [bforsaken](#note10b) me, having loved this present [cworld](#note10c), and is departed unto Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia.

11 Only [aLuke](#note11a) is with me. Take [bMark](#note11b), and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.

12 And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.

13 The cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring with thee, and the [abooks](#note13a), but especially the parchments.

14 Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his [aworks](#note14a):

15 Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly [awithstood](#note15a) our words.

16 At my first [aanswer](#note16a) no man stood with me, but all men forsook me: I pray God that it may not be laid to their [bcharge](#note16b).

17 Notwithstanding the [aLord](#note17a) stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known, and that all the Gentiles might hear: and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.

18 And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will [apreserve](#note18a) me unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

19 Salute Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.

20 Erastus abode at Corinth: but Trophimus have I left at Miletum sick.

21 Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.

22 The [aLord Jesus Christ](#note22a) be with thy spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.

¶ The second epistle unto Timotheus, ordained the first bishop of the church of the Ephesians, was written from Rome, when Paul was brought before Nero the second time.

# The Epistle of Paul to Titus

* [Titus 1](titus/1.xhtml)
* [Titus 2](titus/2.xhtml)
* [Titus 3](titus/3.xhtml)

Chapter 1

Eternal life was promised before the world began—The qualifications of bishops are given—Unto the pure, all things are pure.

1 Paul, a [aservant](#note1a) of God, and an [bapostle](#note1b) of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s [celect](#note1c), and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;

2 In [ahope](#note2a) of [beternal life](#note2b), which God, that cannot [clie](#note2c), [dpromised](#note2d) before the world began;

3 But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;

4 To Titus, [amine own son after](#note4a) the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.

5 For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in [aorder](#note5a) the things that are wanting, and [bordain](#note5b) [celders](#note5c) in every city, as I had [dappointed](#note5d) thee:

6 If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of [ariot or unruly](#note6a).

7 For a [abishop](#note7a) must be blameless, as the [bsteward](#note7b) of God; not [cselfwilled](#note7c), not soon [dangry](#note7d), not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;

8 But a lover of [ahospitality](#note8a), a lover of [bgood men](#note8b), sober, just, holy, [ctemperate](#note8c);

9 Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to [aconvince](#note9a) the [bgainsayers](#note9b).

10 For there are many unruly and [avain talkers](#note10a) and [bdeceivers](#note10b), specially they of the [ccircumcision](#note10c):

11 Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthy [alucre’s](#note11a) sake.

12 One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians are alway liars, evil beasts, [aslow bellies](#note12a).

13 This witness is true. Wherefore [arebuke](#note13a) them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;

14 Not giving heed to Jewish [afables](#note14a), and [bcommandments](#note14b) of men, [cthat](#note14c) turn from the truth.

15 [aUnto](#note15a) the pure all things are [bpure](#note15b): but unto them that are [cdefiled](#note15c) and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their [dmind](#note15d) and [econscience](#note15e) is [fdefiled](#note15f).

16 They [aprofess](#note16a) that they [bknow](#note16b) God; but in [cworks](#note16c) they [ddeny](#note16d) him, being abominable, and [edisobedient](#note16e), and unto every good work [freprobate](#note16f).

Chapter 2

Saints should live righteously, deny ungodliness, and seek the Lord.

1 But [aspeak](#note1a) thou the things which become sound [bdoctrine](#note1b):

2 That the [aaged](#note2a) men be [bsober](#note2b), grave, [ctemperate](#note2c), sound in faith, in charity, in patience.

3 The aged women likewise, that they be in behaviour as becometh holiness, not [afalse](#note3a) [baccusers](#note3b), not given to much wine, teachers of good things;

4 That they may teach the young [awomen](#note4a) to be sober, to [blove](#note4b) their husbands, to love their children,

5 To be discreet, [achaste](#note5a), [bkeepers](#note5b) at [chome](#note5c), good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.

6 Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.

7 In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness, [agravity](#note7a), [bsincerity](#note7b),

8 [aSound](#note8a) speech, that cannot be [bcondemned](#note8b); that he that is [cof the contrary part](#note8c) may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.

9 Exhort [aservants](#note9a) to be obedient unto their own masters, and to please them well in all things; not [banswering again](#note9b);

10 Not [apurloining](#note10a), but shewing all good fidelity; that they may [badorn](#note10b) the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.

11 For the [agrace](#note11a) of God [bthat](#note11b) bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,

12 Teaching us that, denying [aungodliness](#note12a) and [bworldly](#note12b) [clusts](#note12c), we should live [dsoberly](#note12d), [erighteously](#note12e), and godly, in this present world;

13 Looking for that blessed hope, and the [aglorious](#note13a) appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;

14 Who gave [ahimself](#note14a) for us, that he might [bredeem](#note14b) us from all iniquity, and [cpurify](#note14c) unto himself a [dpeculiar people](#note14d), [ezealous](#note14e) of good works.

15 These things speak, and exhort, and [arebuke](#note15a) with all [bauthority](#note15b). Let no man [cdespise](#note15c) thee.

Chapter 3

Saints must live righteously after baptism.

1 Put them in mind to be [asubject](#note1a) to [bprincipalities](#note1b) and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,

2 To [aspeak evil](#note2a) of no man, to be [bno brawlers](#note2b), but gentle, shewing all [cmeekness](#note2c) unto all men.

3 For we ourselves also were [asometimes](#note3a) foolish, [bdisobedient](#note3b), deceived, serving divers [clusts](#note3c) and pleasures, living in [dmalice](#note3d) and envy, hateful, and hating one another.

4 But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,

5 Not by works of [arighteousness](#note5a) which we have done, but according to his [bmercy](#note5b) he saved us, by the [cwashing](#note5c) of regeneration, and [drenewing](#note5d) of the Holy Ghost;

6 Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;

7 That being [ajustified](#note7a) by his grace, we should be made [bheirs](#note7b) according to the hope of eternal life.

8 This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good [aworks](#note8a). These things are good and profitable unto men.

9 But avoid [afoolish](#note9a) questions, and genealogies, and [bcontentions](#note9b), and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.

10 A man that is an [aheretick](#note10a) after the first and second [badmonition](#note10b) reject;

11 Knowing that he that is such is [asubverted](#note11a), and sinneth, being condemned of himself.

12 When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come unto me to Nicopolis: for I have determined there to winter.

13 Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them.

14 And let [aours](#note14a) also learn to maintain [bgood works](#note14b) for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful.

15 All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.

¶ It was written to Titus, ordained the first bishop of the church of the Cretians, from Nicopolis of Macedonia.

Chapter 1

The gospel changes a servant into a brother.

1 Paul, a prisoner of Jesus Christ, and Timothy our brother, unto Philemon our dearly beloved, and fellowlabourer,

2 And to our beloved Apphia, and [aArchippus](#note2a) our fellowsoldier, and to the church in thy house:

3 Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

4 I thank my God, making mention of thee always in my prayers,

5 Hearing of thy love and faith, which thou hast toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all saints;

6 That the [acommunication](#note6a) of thy faith may become [beffectual](#note6b) by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.

7 For we have great joy and consolation in thy love, because the [abowels](#note7a) of the saints are refreshed by thee, brother.

8 Wherefore, though I might be much bold in Christ to enjoin thee that which is convenient,

9 Yet for love’s sake I rather beseech thee, being such an one as Paul the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.

10 I beseech thee for my son [aOnesimus](#note10a), whom I have begotten in my bonds:

11 Which in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable to thee and to me:

12 Whom I have sent again: thou therefore receive him, that is, mine own bowels:

13 Whom I would have retained with me, that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel:

14 But without thy [amind](#note14a) would I do nothing; that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity, but willingly.

15 For perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever;

16 Not now as a servant, but above a servant, a [abrother](#note16a) beloved, specially to me, but how much more unto thee, both in the flesh, and in the Lord?

17 If thou count me therefore a partner, receive him as myself.

18 If he hath wronged thee, or oweth thee [aought](#note18a), put that on mine account;

19 I Paul have written it with mine own hand, I will repay it: albeit I do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides.

20 Yea, brother, let me have joy of thee in the Lord: refresh my bowels in the Lord.

21 Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say.

22 But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I trust that through your [aprayers](#note22a) I shall be given unto you.

23 There salute thee [aEpaphras](#note23a), my fellowprisoner in Christ Jesus;

24 Marcus, Aristarchus, [aDemas](#note24a), Lucas, my fellowlabourers.

25 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

¶ Written from Rome to Philemon, by Onesimus a servant.

Chapter 1

The Son is in the express image of the person of the Father—Christ is the Only Begotten Son and thus above the angels.

1 God, who [aat](#note1a) sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the [bfathers](#note1b) by the [cprophets](#note1c),

2 Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his [aSon](#note2a), whom he hath [bappointed](#note2b) [cheir](#note2c) of all things, by whom also he [dmade](#note2d) the [eworlds](#note2e);

3 Who being the brightness of his [aglory](#note3a), and the express image of his [bperson](#note3b), and upholding all things by the word of his [cpower](#note3c), when he had by himself [dpurged](#note3d) our sins, sat down on the [eright hand](#note3e) of the [fMajesty](#note3f) on high;

4 Being made so much better than the angels, as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent [aname](#note4a) than they.

5 For unto which of the angels said he at any time, Thou art my [aSon](#note5a), this day have I begotten thee? And again, I will be to him a [bFather](#note5b), and he shall be to me a Son?

6 And again, when he bringeth in the [afirstbegotten](#note6a) into the world, he saith, [bAnd](#note6b) let all the angels of God [cworship](#note6c) him.

7 And of the angels he saith, Who maketh his angels spirits, and his ministers a flame of fire.

8 But unto the Son he saith, Thy [athrone](#note8a), O God, is for [bever](#note8b) and ever: a sceptre of righteousness is the sceptre of thy kingdom.

9 Thou hast loved [arighteousness](#note9a), and hated iniquity; therefore God, even thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.

10 And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the [afoundation](#note10a) of the earth; and the heavens are the works of thine [bhands](#note10b):

11 They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall [awax old](#note11a) as doth a garment;

12 And as a vesture shalt thou fold them up, and they shall be changed: but thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail.

13 But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my [aright hand](#note13a), until I make thine enemies thy footstool?

14 Are they not all [aministering](#note14a) [bspirits](#note14b), sent forth [cto minister](#note14c) for them who shall be heirs of salvation?

Chapter 2

Jesus came to suffer death and save men—He came to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.

1 Therefore we ought to give the more [aearnest](#note1a) [bheed](#note1b) to the things which we have heard, lest at any time we should let them slip.

2 For if the word spoken by angels was steadfast, and every transgression and [adisobedience](#note2a) received a [bjust](#note2b) recompence of reward;

3 How shall we [aescape](#note3a), if we neglect so great salvation; which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed unto us by them that heard him;

4 God also bearing them witness, both with [asigns](#note4a) and [bwonders](#note4b), and with divers miracles, and gifts of the Holy Ghost, according to his own [cwill](#note4c)?

5 For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.

6 But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is [aman](#note6a), that thou art mindful of him? or the son of man, that thou visitest him?

7 Thou madest him a little lower than the [aangels](#note7a); thou crownedst him with glory and honour, and didst set him over the works of thy hands:

8 Thou hast put all things in subjection under his [afeet](#note8a). For in that he put all in subjection under him, he left nothing that is not put under him. But now we see not yet all things put under him.

9 But we see Jesus, who was made a little [alower](#note9a) than the [bangels](#note9b) [cfor](#note9c) the suffering of [ddeath](#note9d), [ecrowned](#note9e) with glory and honour; that he by the [fgrace](#note9f) of God should taste [gdeath](#note9g) for every man.

10 For [ait became him](#note10a), for whom are all things, and by whom are all [bthings](#note10b), in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the [ccaptain](#note10c) of their salvation [dperfect](#note10d) through sufferings.

11 For both he that sanctifieth and they who are [asanctified](#note11a) are all of [bone](#note11b): for which cause he is not ashamed to call them [cbrethren](#note11c),

12 Saying, I will declare thy [aname](#note12a) unto my brethren, in the midst of the church will I sing praise unto thee.

13 And again, I will put my [atrust](#note13a) in him. And again, Behold I and the children which God hath given me.

14 [aForasmuch then as](#note14a) the children are partakers of [bflesh and blood](#note14b), he also himself likewise took part of the same; that through death he might [cdestroy](#note14c) him that had the power of [ddeath](#note14d), that is, the [edevil](#note14e);

15 And deliver them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to [abondage](#note15a).

16 For verily he took not on him the nature of [aangels](#note16a); but he took on him the [bseed of Abraham](#note16b).

17 Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make [areconciliation](#note17a) for the sins of the people.

18 For in that he himself hath suffered being [atempted](#note18a), he is able to [bsuccour](#note18b) them that are tempted.

Chapter 3

Christ is the Apostle and High Priest of our profession—Jesus, being the Son, is more than a servant—Now is the time and the day of our salvation.

1 Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, [aconsider](#note1a) the [bApostle](#note1b) and [cHigh Priest](#note1c) of our profession, Christ Jesus;

2 Who was faithful to him that appointed him, as also Moses was faithful in all his house.

3 For this man was counted worthy of more glory than Moses, inasmuch as he who hath builded the house hath more honour than the house.

4 For every house is builded by some man; but he that built all things is God.

5 And Moses verily was faithful in all his house, as a [aservant](#note5a), for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken after;

6 But Christ as a son over his own [ahouse](#note6a); whose [bhouse](#note6b) are we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end.

7 Wherefore (as the [aHoly Ghost](#note7a) saith, [bTo day](#note7b) if ye will hear his voice,

8 [aHarden](#note8a) not your hearts, as in the [bprovocation](#note8b), in the day of temptation in the wilderness:

9 When your fathers tempted me, [aproved](#note9a) me, and saw my works forty years.

10 Wherefore I was grieved with that [ageneration](#note10a), and said, They do alway err in their heart; and they have not known my ways.

11 So I sware in my wrath, They shall not [aenter](#note11a) into my [brest](#note11b).)

12 Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil [aheart](#note12a) of [bunbelief](#note12b), in [cdeparting from](#note12c) the living God.

13 But [aexhort](#note13a) one another daily, while it is called [bTo day](#note13b); lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin.

14 For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence [asteadfast](#note14a) unto the end;

15 While it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, [aharden](#note15a) not your hearts, as in the [bprovocation](#note15b).

16 For some, when they had heard, did provoke: howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses.

17 But with whom was he grieved forty years? was it not with them that had sinned, whose [acarcases](#note17a) fell in the wilderness?

18 And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest, but to them that believed not?

19 So we see that they could not enter in because of [aunbelief](#note19a).

Chapter 4

The gospel was offered to ancient Israel—Saints enter into the rest of the Lord—Though tempted in all points, Jesus was without sin.

1 Let us therefore fear, lest, a promise being left us of entering into his [arest](#note1a), any of you should seem to come short of it.

2 For unto us was the [agospel](#note2a) preached, as well as unto them: but the word preached did not profit them, not being mixed with [bfaith](#note2b) in them that heard it.

3 [aFor](#note3a) we which have believed do enter into rest, as he said, As I have sworn in my wrath, if they shall enter into my [brest](#note3b): although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.

4 For he spake in a certain place of the [aseventh day](#note4a) on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all his works.

5 [aAnd](#note5a) in this place again, If they shall enter into my rest.

6 Seeing therefore it remaineth that some must enter therein, and [athey](#note6a) to whom it was first preached entered not in because of [bunbelief](#note6b):

7 Again, he [alimiteth](#note7a) a certain day, saying in David, To day, after so long a time; as it is said, To day if ye will [bhear](#note7b) his voice, [charden](#note7c) not your hearts.

8 For if [aJesus](#note8a) had given them rest, then would he not afterward have spoken of another day.

9 There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.

10 For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God did from his.

11 Let us [alabour](#note11a) therefore to enter into that rest, lest any man fall after the same example of [bunbelief](#note11b).

12 For the [aword](#note12a) of God is [bquick](#note12b), and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged [csword](#note12c), [dpiercing](#note12d) even to the dividing asunder of [esoul](#note12e) and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a [fdiscerner](#note12f) of the [gthoughts](#note12g) and [hintents](#note12h) of the heart.

13 Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and opened unto the [aeyes](#note13a) of him with whom we have to do.

14 Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the [aheavens](#note14a), Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast our profession.

15 For we have not an high priest [awhich](#note15a) cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points [btempted](#note15b) like as we are, yet without [csin](#note15c).

16 Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of [agrace](#note16a), that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need.

Chapter 5

For a man to hold the priesthood, he must be called of God as was Aaron—Christ was a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek—Jesus Christ is the Author of eternal salvation.

1 For every high priest taken from among men is [aordained](#note1a) for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and [bsacrifices](#note1b) for sins:

2 Who can have [acompassion](#note2a) on the ignorant, and on them that are [bout of the way](#note2b); for that he himself also is compassed with infirmity.

3 And by reason hereof he ought, as for the people, so also for himself, to offer for [asins](#note3a).

4 And no man taketh this [ahonour](#note4a) unto himself, but he that is [bcalled](#note4b) of God, as was [cAaron](#note4c).

5 So also Christ glorified not himself to be made an high priest; but he that said unto him, Thou art my [aSon](#note5a), to day have I begotten thee.

6 As he saith also in another place, Thou art a [apriest](#note6a) [bfor ever](#note6b) after the order of [cMelchisedec](#note6c).

7 Who in the days of his flesh, when he had offered up [aprayers](#note7a) and supplications with [bstrong crying](#note7b) and tears unto him that was able to save him from death, and was heard [cin that he feared](#note7c);

8 Though he were a Son, yet [alearned](#note8a) he [bobedience](#note8b) by the things which he [csuffered](#note8c);

9 And being made [aperfect](#note9a), he became the [bauthor](#note9b) of eternal [csalvation](#note9c) unto all them that obey him;

10 [aCalled](#note10a) of God an [bhigh priest](#note10b) after the order of Melchisedec.

11 Of whom we have many things to say, and [ahard](#note11a) to be [buttered](#note11b), seeing ye are dull of [chearing](#note11c).

12 For when for the time ye ought to be teachers, ye have need that one teach you again which be the first principles of the oracles of God; and are become such as have need of [amilk](#note12a), and not of strong meat.

13 For every one that useth [amilk](#note13a) is unskilful in the word of righteousness: for he is a babe.

14 But strong meat belongeth to them that are of full age, even those who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern both good and evil.

Chapter 6

Let us go on to perfection—The sons of perdition crucify Christ anew—God swears with an oath that the faithful will be saved.

1 Therefore [aleaving](#note1a) the principles of the doctrine of Christ, let us go on unto [bperfection](#note1b); not laying again the foundation of [crepentance](#note1c) from dead works, and of faith toward God,

2 Of the [adoctrine](#note2a) of [bbaptisms](#note2b), and of [claying](#note2c) on of [dhands](#note2d), and of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.

3 [aAnd](#note3a) this will we do, if God permit.

4 For it is impossible for those who were once [aenlightened](#note4a), and have tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Ghost,

5 And have [atasted](#note5a) the good word of God, and the powers of the [bworld](#note5b) to come,

6 If they shall [afall away](#note6a), to renew them again unto repentance; seeing they [bcrucify](#note6b) to themselves the Son of God afresh, and put him to an open [cshame](#note6c).

7 For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it, and bringeth forth herbs [ameet](#note7a) for them by whom it is [bdressed](#note7b), receiveth blessing from God:

8 But that which beareth thorns and briers is rejected, and is nigh unto [acursing](#note8a); whose end is to be burned.

9 But, beloved, we are persuaded better things of you, and things that accompany salvation, though we thus speak.

10 For God is not [aunrighteous](#note10a) to forget your work and [blabour](#note10b) of [clove](#note10c), which ye have shewed toward his [dname](#note10d), in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister.

11 And we desire that every one of you do shew the same [adiligence](#note11a) to the full assurance of hope unto the end:

12 That ye be not [aslothful](#note12a), but followers of them who through [bfaith](#note12b) and [cpatience](#note12c) inherit the [dpromises](#note12d).

13 For when God made promise to Abraham, because he could [aswear by](#note13a) no greater, he [bsware](#note13b) by himself,

14 Saying, Surely blessing I will [abless](#note14a) thee, and multiplying I will multiply thee.

15 And so, after he had patiently [aendured](#note15a), he obtained the promise.

16 For men verily swear by the greater: and an [aoath](#note16a) for confirmation is to them an end of all [bstrife](#note16b).

17 Wherein God, willing more abundantly to shew unto the heirs of promise the immutability of his [acounsel](#note17a), [bconfirmed](#note17b) it by an [coath](#note17c):

18 That by two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to [alie](#note18a), we might have a strong consolation, who have fled for [brefuge](#note18b) to lay hold upon the [chope](#note18c) set before us:

19 Which hope we have as an [aanchor](#note19a) of the soul, both sure and steadfast, and which entereth into that within the [bveil](#note19b);

20 Whither the forerunner is for us entered, even Jesus, made an [ahigh priest](#note20a) for ever after the order of Melchisedec.

Chapter 7

The Melchizedek Priesthood brings exaltation and administers the gospel—It is received with an oath and covenant—The superiority of the Melchizedek Priesthood over the Aaronic Priesthood is explained—Salvation comes through the intercession of Christ.

1 For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, [apriest](#note1a) of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;

2 To whom also Abraham gave a [atenth](#note2a) part of all; first being by interpretation King of [brighteousness](#note2b), and after that also King of [cSalem](#note2c), which is, King of peace;

3 [aWithout](#note3a) father, without mother, without descent, having neither [bbeginning](#note3b) of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a [cpriest](#note3c) continually.

4 Now consider how great this man was, unto whom even the [apatriarch](#note4a) Abraham gave the tenth of the spoils.

5 And verily they that are of the sons of [aLevi](#note5a), who receive the [boffice](#note5b) of the priesthood, have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brethren, though they come out of the loins of Abraham:

6 But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.

7 And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.

8 And here men that die receive tithes; but there he receiveth them, of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.

9 And as I may so say, Levi also, who receiveth tithes, payed tithes in Abraham.

10 For he was yet in the loins of his father, when Melchisedec met him.

11 If therefore [aperfection](#note11a) were by the [bLevitical priesthood](#note11b), (for under it the people received the law,) what further need was there that another priest should rise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?

12 For the [apriesthood](#note12a) being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.

13 For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.

14 For it is evident that our Lord sprang out of [aJuda](#note14a); of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.

15 And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,

16 Who is made, not after the law of a [acarnal](#note16a) commandment, but after the power of an endless life.

17 For he testifieth, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of [aMelchisedec](#note17a).

18 For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the [aweakness](#note18a) and unprofitableness thereof.

19 [aFor](#note19a) the law made nothing [bperfect](#note19b), but the bringing in of a [cbetter](#note19c) hope did; by the which we draw nigh unto God.

20 And inasmuch as not without an [aoath](#note20a) he was made priest:

21 (For those [apriests](#note21a) were made without an oath; but this with an [boath](#note21b) by him that said unto him, The Lord sware and will not repent, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec:)

22 By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better [atestament](#note22a).

23 And they truly were many priests, because they were not suffered to continue by reason of death:

24 But this man, because he continueth ever, hath an unchangeable [apriesthood](#note24a).

25 Wherefore he is able also to save them [ato the uttermost](#note25a) that [bcome](#note25b) unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make [cintercession](#note25c) for them.

26 [aFor](#note26a) such an [bhigh priest](#note26b) became us, who is holy, harmless, undefiled, [cseparate](#note26c) from [dsinners](#note26d), and made [ehigher](#note26e) than the [fheavens](#note26f);

27 Who needeth not [adaily](#note27a), as those high priests, to offer up [bsacrifice](#note27b), first for his own sins, and then for the people’s: for this he did [conce](#note27c), when he [doffered up](#note27d) himself.

28 For the law maketh men high priests which have infirmity; but the word of the [aoath](#note28a), which was since the law, maketh the [bSon](#note28b), who is [cconsecrated](#note28c) for evermore.

Chapter 8

Christ offered Himself as a sacrifice for sin—God promised to make a new covenant with Israel.

1 Now of the things which we have spoken this is the sum: We have such an [ahigh priest](#note1a), who is set on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens;

2 A minister of the sanctuary, and of the [atrue](#note2a) [btabernacle](#note2b), which the Lord pitched, and not man.

3 For every high priest is ordained to offer gifts and sacrifices: wherefore it is of necessity that this man have somewhat also to [aoffer](#note3a).

4 [aFor](#note4a) if he were on earth, he should not be a priest, seeing that there are [bpriests](#note4b) that offer gifts according to the law:

5 Who serve unto the example and [ashadow](#note5a) of heavenly things, as Moses was admonished of God when he was about to make the [btabernacle](#note5b): for, See, saith he, that thou make all things according to the [cpattern](#note5c) shewed to thee in the mount.

6 But now hath he obtained a more excellent ministry, by how much also he is the [amediator](#note6a) of a better [bcovenant](#note6b), which was established upon better [cpromises](#note6c).

7 For if that first covenant had been [afaultless](#note7a), then should no place have been sought for the second.

8 For finding fault with them, he saith, Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of [aJudah](#note8a):

9 Not according to the [acovenant](#note9a) that I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they continued not in my covenant, and I regarded them not, saith the Lord.

10 For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, saith the Lord; I will put my [alaws](#note10a) into their [bmind](#note10b), and write them in their [chearts](#note10c): and I will be to them a God, and they shall be to me a people:

11 And they shall not teach every man his neighbour, and every man his brother, saying, Know the Lord: for all shall know me, from the least to the greatest.

12 For I will be merciful to their [aunrighteousness](#note12a), and their sins and their iniquities will I [bremember](#note12b) no more.

13 In that he saith, A [anew covenant](#note13a), he hath made the first old. Now that which decayeth and waxeth [bold](#note13b) is ready to [cvanish](#note13c) away.

Chapter 9

The Mosaic ordinances prefigured Christ’s ministry—Christ is the Mediator of the new covenant.

1 Then verily the first [acovenant](#note1a) had also [bordinances](#note1b) of divine [cservice](#note1c), and a worldly sanctuary.

2 For there was a [atabernacle](#note2a) made; the first, wherein was the [bcandlestick](#note2b), and the [ctable](#note2c), and the [dshewbread](#note2d); which is called the sanctuary.

3 And after the second [aveil](#note3a), the tabernacle which is called the [bHoliest](#note3b) of all;

4 Which had the golden censer, and the [aark](#note4a) of the covenant overlaid round about with gold, wherein was the golden [bpot](#note4b) that had manna, and [cAaron’s](#note4c) rod that budded, and the [dtables](#note4d) of the covenant;

5 And over it the [acherubims](#note5a) of glory shadowing the [bmercyseat](#note5b); of which we cannot now speak particularly.

6 Now when these things were thus [aordained](#note6a), the [bpriests](#note6b) went always into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the service of God.

7 But into the second went the [ahigh priest](#note7a) alone [bonce](#note7b) every year, not without blood, which he offered for himself, and for the errors of the people:

8 The Holy Ghost this signifying, that the way into the holiest of all was not yet made manifest, while as the first tabernacle was yet standing:

9 Which was a [afigure](#note9a) for the time then present, in which were offered both gifts and sacrifices, that could not make him that did the service [bperfect](#note9b), as pertaining to the conscience;

10 Which stood only in meats and drinks, and divers [awashings](#note10a), and [bcarnal](#note10b) [cordinances](#note10c), [dimposed](#note10d) on them until the time of reformation.

11 But Christ being come an [ahigh priest](#note11a) of good things to come, by a greater and more perfect [btabernacle](#note11b), not made with hands, that is to say, not of this building;

12 Neither by the blood of goats and calves, but by his own [ablood](#note12a) he entered in once into the [bholy place](#note12b), having obtained eternal [credemption](#note12c) for us.

13 For if the [ablood](#note13a) of bulls and of goats, and the ashes of an heifer sprinkling the unclean, sanctifieth to the [bpurifying](#note13b) of the flesh:

14 How much more shall the [ablood](#note14a) of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself [bwithout](#note14b) [cspot](#note14c) to God, [dpurge](#note14d) your [econscience](#note14e) from dead works to serve the living God?

15 And for this cause he is the [amediator](#note15a) of the [bnew](#note15b) [ctestament](#note15c), that by means of [ddeath](#note15d), for the redemption of the [etransgressions](#note15e) that were under the first [ftestament](#note15f), they which are called might receive the [gpromise](#note15g) of eternal [hinheritance](#note15h).

16 For where a [atestament](#note16a) is, there must also of necessity be the [bdeath](#note16b) of the [ctestator](#note16c).

17 For a [atestament](#note17a) is of force after men are dead: [botherwise](#note17b) it is of no strength at all while the testator liveth.

18 Whereupon neither the first testament was dedicated without blood.

19 For when Moses had [aspoken](#note19a) every precept to all the people according to the law, he took the [bblood](#note19b) of calves and of goats, with water, and scarlet wool, and hyssop, and [csprinkled](#note19c) both the book, and all the people,

20 Saying, This is the blood of the testament which God hath enjoined unto you.

21 Moreover he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle, and all the vessels of the ministry.

22 And almost all things are by the law purged with blood; and without [ashedding](#note22a) of blood is no [bremission](#note22b).

23 It was therefore necessary that the [apatterns](#note23a) of things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

24 For Christ is not entered into the holy places made with hands, which are the figures of the [atrue](#note24a); but into [bheaven](#note24b) itself, now to appear in the [cpresence](#note24c) of God for us:

25 Nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the [ahigh priest](#note25a) entereth into the holy place every year with blood of others;

26 For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once [ain](#note26a) the [bend of the world](#note26b) hath he appeared to put away sin by the [csacrifice](#note26c) of himself.

27 And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:

28 So Christ was once [aoffered](#note28a) to [bbear](#note28b) the [csins](#note28c) of many; and unto them that look for him shall he [dappear](#note28d) the second time without sin unto salvation.

Chapter 10

We are sanctified by the shedding of the blood of Christ—The superiority of His sacrifice is explained—Those who fall from grace through willful sin are damned—The just will live by faith.

1 For the [alaw](#note1a) having a [bshadow](#note1b) of good things to come, and not the very image of the things, can never with those sacrifices which they offered [cyear](#note1c) by year continually make the comers thereunto perfect.

2 For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once [apurged](#note2a) should have had no more [bconscience](#note2b) of sins.

3 But in those [asacrifices](#note3a) there is a [bremembrance](#note3b) again made of sins every year.

4 For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.

5 Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me:

6 In burnt offerings and [asacrifices](#note6a) for sin thou hast had no pleasure.

7 Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.

8 Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law;

9 Then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second.

10 By the which will we are [asanctified](#note10a) through the [boffering](#note10b) [cof](#note10c) the [dbody](#note10d) of Jesus Christ [eonce](#note10e) for all.

11 And every priest standeth [adaily](#note11a) ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away [bsins](#note11b):

12 But this man, after he had offered one [asacrifice](#note12a) for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;

13 [aFrom](#note13a) henceforth expecting till his enemies be made his footstool.

14 For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.

15 Whereof the [aHoly Ghost](#note15a) also is a [bwitness](#note15b) to us: for after that he had said before,

16 This is the [acovenant](#note16a) that I will make with them after those days, saith the Lord, I will put my laws into their hearts, and in their [bminds](#note16b) will I write them;

17 And their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.

18 Now where [aremission](#note18a) of these is, there is no more offering for sin.

19 Having therefore, brethren, [aboldness](#note19a) to enter into the [bholiest](#note19b) by the blood of Jesus,

20 By a new and living way, which he hath consecrated for us, through the [aveil](#note20a), that is to say, his flesh;

21 And having an [ahigh priest](#note21a) over the house of God;

22 Let us [adraw](#note22a) [bnear](#note22b) with a true heart in full assurance of [cfaith](#note22c), having our hearts [dsprinkled](#note22d) from an evil [econscience](#note22e), and our bodies washed with pure water.

23 Let us hold fast the [aprofession](#note23a) of our [bfaith](#note23b) without wavering; (for he is faithful that promised;)

24 And let us [aconsider](#note24a) one another to [bprovoke](#note24b) unto love and to good works:

25 Not forsaking the [aassembling](#note25a) of ourselves together, as the manner of some is; but exhorting one another: and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.

26 For if we [asin](#note26a) [bwilfully](#note26b) after that we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remaineth no more sacrifice for sins,

27 But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery [aindignation](#note27a), which shall devour the adversaries.

28 He that [adespised](#note28a) Moses’ law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:

29 Of how much sorer [apunishment](#note29a), suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done [bdespite](#note29b) unto the Spirit of grace?

30 For we know him that hath said, [aVengeance](#note30a) belongeth unto me, I will [brecompense](#note30b), saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall [cjudge](#note30c) his people.

31 It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

32 But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great [afight](#note32a) of afflictions;

33 Partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.

34 For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took [ajoyfully](#note34a) the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring [bsubstance](#note34b).

35 Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.

36 For ye have need of [apatience](#note36a), that, after ye have done the [bwill](#note36b) of God, ye might receive the [cpromise](#note36c).

37 For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.

38 Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man [adraw back](#note38a), my soul shall have no pleasure in him.

39 But we are not of them who [adraw back](#note39a) unto [bperdition](#note39b); but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.

Chapter 11

By faith we understand the word and work of God—The faith of the ancients was centered in Christ—By faith, men subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, and worked miracles.

1 Now [afaith](#note1a) is the [bsubstance](#note1b) of things [choped](#note1c) for, the [devidence](#note1d) of things not seen.

2 For by it the elders [aobtained](#note2a) a good [breport](#note2b).

3 Through faith we understand that the worlds were [aframed](#note3a) by the [bword](#note3b) of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.

4 By faith [aAbel](#note4a) offered unto God a more excellent [bsacrifice](#note4b) than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.

5 By faith [aEnoch](#note5a) was [btranslated](#note5b) that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this [ctestimony](#note5c), that he pleased God.

6 But without [afaith](#note6a) it is impossible to please him: for he that [bcometh](#note6b) to God must believe that he is, and that he is a [crewarder](#note6c) of them that [ddiligently](#note6d) [eseek](#note6e) him.

7 By faith [aNoah](#note7a), being [bwarned](#note7b) of God of things not seen as yet, [cmoved with fear](#note7c), prepared an ark to the saving of his house; by the which he condemned the world, and became heir of the [drighteousness](#note7d) which is by faith.

8 By faith [aAbraham](#note8a), when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an [binheritance](#note8b), [cobeyed](#note8c); and he went out, not [dknowing](#note8d) whither he went.

9 By faith he [asojourned](#note9a) in the [bland](#note9b) of [cpromise](#note9c), as in a strange country, dwelling in [dtabernacles](#note9d) with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise:

10 For he looked for a [acity](#note10a) which hath foundations, whose builder and maker is God.

11 Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed, and was delivered of a child when she was past age, because she judged him faithful who had promised.

12 Therefore sprang there even of one, and him as good as dead, so many as the stars of the sky in [amultitude](#note12a), and as the sand which is by the sea shore innumerable.

13 These all died in faith, not having received the [apromises](#note13a), but having seen them afar off, and were persuaded of them, and embraced them, and confessed that they were [bstrangers](#note13b) and pilgrims on the earth.

14 For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a [acountry](#note14a).

15 And truly, if they had been mindful of that country from whence they came out, they might have had opportunity to have returned.

16 But now they desire a better [acountry](#note16a), that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.

17 By [afaith](#note17a) Abraham, when he was [btried](#note17b), [coffered up](#note17c) Isaac: and he that had received the [dpromises](#note17d) offered up his only begotten son,

18 Of whom it was said, That in [aIsaac](#note18a) shall thy [bseed](#note18b) be called:

19 [aAccounting](#note19a) that God was able to raise him up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.

20 By faith Isaac [ablessed](#note20a) Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.

21 By faith [aJacob](#note21a), when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph; and worshipped, leaning upon the top of his staff.

22 By faith [aJoseph](#note22a), when he died, made mention of the departing of the children of Israel; and gave commandment concerning his bones.

23 By faith [aMoses](#note23a), when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw he was a [bproper](#note23b) child; and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.

24 By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the [ason](#note24a) of Pharaoh’s daughter;

25 Choosing rather to [asuffer](#note25a) affliction with the [bpeople](#note25b) of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;

26 Esteeming the [areproach](#note26a) of Christ greater [briches](#note26b) than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.

27 By faith he forsook Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.

28 Through faith he kept the [apassover](#note28a), and the sprinkling of [bblood](#note28b), lest he that destroyed the firstborn should touch them.

29 By faith they passed through the [aRed sea](#note29a) as by dry land: which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.

30 By faith the [awalls](#note30a) of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed about seven days.

31 By [afaith](#note31a) the harlot [bRahab](#note31b) perished not with [cthem that believed not](#note31c), when she had received the spies with peace.

32 And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and of [aBarak](#note32a), and of Samson, and of [bJephthae](#note32b); of David also, and Samuel, and of the prophets:

33 Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained [apromises](#note33a), stopped the mouths of [blions](#note33b),

34 Quenched the violence of [afire](#note34a), escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakness were made [bstrong](#note34b), waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.

35 Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting [adeliverance](#note35a); that they might obtain [ba better resurrection](#note35b):

36 And others had trial of [acruel](#note36a) [bmockings](#note36b) and scourgings, yea, moreover of bonds and imprisonment:

37 They were [astoned](#note37a), they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented;

38 (Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts, and in mountains, and in dens and caves of the earth.

39 And these all, having obtained a [agood](#note39a) [breport](#note39b) through faith, received not the promise:

40 [aGod](#note40a) having [bprovided](#note40b) some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made [cperfect](#note40c).

Chapter 12

Whom the Lord loves He chastens—God is the Father of spirits—To see God, follow peace and holiness—Exalted Saints belong to the Church of the Firstborn.

1 Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the [asin](#note1a) which doth so easily [bbeset](#note1b) us, and let us run with [cpatience](#note1c) the race that is set before us,

2 Looking unto Jesus the [aauthor](#note2a) and [bfinisher of](#note2b) our faith; who for the joy that was set before him [cendured](#note2c) the cross, despising the [dshame](#note2d), and is set down at the right hand of the [ethrone](#note2e) of God.

3 For consider him that endured such [acontradiction](#note3a) of sinners against himself, lest ye be wearied and faint in your minds.

4 Ye have not yet resisted unto blood, [astriving](#note4a) against sin.

5 And ye have forgotten the exhortation which speaketh unto you as unto children, My son, despise not thou the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art rebuked of him:

6 For whom the Lord loveth he [achasteneth](#note6a), and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth.

7 If ye [aendure](#note7a) [bchastening](#note7b), God dealeth with you as with [csons](#note7c); for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?

8 But if ye be without chastisement, whereof all are partakers, then are ye bastards, and not sons.

9 Furthermore we have had [afathers](#note9a) of our flesh which [bcorrected](#note9b) us, and we gave them [creverence](#note9c): shall we not much rather be in [dsubjection](#note9d) unto the [eFather](#note9e) of [fspirits](#note9f), and live?

10 For they verily for a few days chastened us after their own pleasure; but he for our profit, that we might be partakers of his [aholiness](#note10a).

11 Now no chastening for the present seemeth to be joyous, but grievous: nevertheless afterward it yieldeth the [apeaceable](#note11a) fruit of righteousness unto them which are [bexercised](#note11b) thereby.

12 Wherefore lift up the hands which hang down, and the [afeeble](#note12a) knees;

13 And make straight paths for your feet, lest that which is lame be turned out of the way; but let it rather be [ahealed](#note13a).

14 Follow [apeace](#note14a) with all men, and [bholiness](#note14b), without which no man shall [csee](#note14c) the Lord:

15 Looking diligently lest any man [afail of](#note15a) the [bgrace](#note15b) of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby many be defiled;

16 Lest there be any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsel of meat sold his [abirthright](#note16a).

17 For ye know how that afterward, when he would have inherited the blessing, he was rejected: for he found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with [atears](#note17a).

18 For ye are not come unto the [amount](#note18a) that might be touched, and that burned with fire, nor unto blackness, and darkness, and tempest,

19 And the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which [avoice](#note19a) they that heard entreated that the word should not be spoken to them any more:

20 (For they could not endure that which was commanded, And if so much as a beast touch the mountain, it shall be stoned, or thrust through with a dart:

21 And so terrible was the sight, that Moses said, I exceedingly fear and quake:)

22 But ye are come unto mount [aSion](#note22a), and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to an innumerable company of [bangels](#note22b),

23 To the general assembly and church of the [afirstborn](#note23a), which are [bwritten](#note23b) in heaven, and to God the [cJudge](#note23c) of all, and to the [dspirits](#note23d) of [ejust](#note23e) men made perfect,

24 And to Jesus the [amediator](#note24a) of the [bnew](#note24b) [ccovenant](#note24c), and to the [dblood](#note24d) of sprinkling, that speaketh [ebetter](#note24e) things than that of Abel.

25 See that ye refuse not him that speaketh. For if they [aescaped](#note25a) not who refused him that spake on earth, much more shall not we escape, if we turn away from him that speaketh from heaven:

26 Whose voice then shook the earth: but now he hath promised, saying, Yet once more I [ashake](#note26a) not the earth only, but also heaven.

27 And this word, Yet once more, signifieth the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remain.

28 Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, whereby we may serve God acceptably with [areverence](#note28a) and godly [bfear](#note28b):

29 For our God is a consuming fire.
