Chapter 13

Marriage is honorable—Christ is the same everlastingly—Paul explains how the Saints are to offer acceptable sacrifices.

1 Let [abrotherly](#note1a) [blove](#note1b) continue.

2 Be not forgetful to [aentertain](#note2a) [bstrangers](#note2b): for thereby some have entertained [cangels](#note2c) [dunawares](#note2d).

3 Remember them that are in [abonds](#note3a), as bound with them; and them which [bsuffer](#note3b) adversity, as being yourselves also in the body.

4 [aMarriage](#note4a) is honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but [bwhoremongers](#note4b) and [cadulterers](#note4c) God will [djudge](#note4d).

5 Let your conversation be without [acovetousness](#note5a); and be [bcontent](#note5b) with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor [cforsake](#note5c) thee.

6 So that we may boldly say, The Lord is my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.

7 Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of their conversation.

8 Jesus Christ the [asame](#note8a) yesterday, and to day, and for ever.

9 Be not [acarried](#note9a) about with divers and strange [bdoctrines](#note9b). For it is a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.

10 We have an altar, whereof they have no right to eat which serve the tabernacle.

11 For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.

12 Wherefore Jesus also, that he might [asanctify](#note12a) the people with his own [bblood](#note12b), [csuffered](#note12c) [dwithout](#note12d) the gate.

13 Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his [areproach](#note13a).

14 For here have we no continuing city, but we seek one to come.

15 By him therefore let us offer the [asacrifice](#note15a) of praise to God continually, that is, the [bfruit](#note15b) of our lips giving thanks to his name.

16 But to do good and to [acommunicate](#note16a) forget not: for with such [bsacrifices](#note16b) God is well pleased.

17 [aObey](#note17a) them that have the rule over you, and [bsubmit](#note17b) yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that is unprofitable for you.

18 Pray for us: for we trust we have a good [aconscience](#note18a), in all things willing to live [bhonestly](#note18b).

19 But I beseech you the rather to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.

20 Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great [ashepherd](#note20a) of the sheep, through the blood of the [beverlasting](#note20b) [ccovenant](#note20c),

21 Make you perfect in every good [awork](#note21a) to do his [bwill](#note21b), working in you that which is wellpleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

22 And I beseech you, brethren, suffer the word of exhortation: for I have written a letter unto you in few words.

23 Know ye that our brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.

24 Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.

25 Grace be with you all. Amen.

¶ Written to the Hebrews from Italy by Timothy.

# The General Epistle of James

* [James 1](james/1.xhtml)
* [James 2](james/2.xhtml)
* [James 3](james/3.xhtml)
* [James 4](james/4.xhtml)
* [James 5](james/5.xhtml)

Chapter 1

If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God—Resist temptation—Be doers of the word—James explains how to recognize pure religion.

1 James, a [aservant](#note1a) of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are [bscattered](#note1b) abroad, greeting.

2 My brethren, count it all joy when ye fall into [adivers](#note2a) [btemptations](#note2b);

3 Knowing this, that the [atrying](#note3a) of your faith worketh [bpatience](#note3b).

4 But let patience have her perfect work, that ye may be [aperfect](#note4a) and entire, wanting nothing.

5 [aIf](#note5a) any of you lack [bwisdom](#note5b), let him ask of God, that [cgiveth](#note5c) to all men liberally, and [dupbraideth](#note5d) not; and it shall be given him.

6 But let him [aask](#note6a) in [bfaith](#note6b), nothing [cwavering](#note6c). For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.

7 For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord.

8 A [adouble minded](#note8a) man is unstable in all his ways.

9 Let the brother of [alow](#note9a) degree rejoice in that he is exalted:

10 But the rich, in that he is made low: because as the flower of the [agrass](#note10a) he shall pass away.

11 For the sun is no sooner risen with a burning heat, but it withereth the grass, and the flower thereof falleth, and the [agrace](#note11a) of the fashion of it perisheth: so also shall the [brich](#note11b) man fade away in his ways.

12 [aBlessed](#note12a) is the man that [bendureth temptation](#note12b): for when he is [ctried](#note12c), he shall receive the [dcrown](#note12d) of life, which the Lord hath promised to them that love him.

13 Let no man say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God: for God cannot be [atempted](#note13a) with [bevil](#note13b), neither tempteth he any man:

14 But every man is [atempted](#note14a), when he is drawn away of his own [blust](#note14b), and enticed.

15 Then when lust hath conceived, it bringeth forth [asin](#note15a): and sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death.

16 Do not err, my beloved brethren.

17 Every [agood](#note17a) [bgift](#note17b) and every perfect [cgift](#note17c) is from above, and [dcometh](#note17d) down from the [eFather](#note17e) of lights, with whom is no [fvariableness](#note17f), neither shadow of turning.

18 Of his own will begat he us with the word of truth, that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures.

19 Wherefore, my beloved brethren, let every man be swift to hear, slow to [aspeak](#note19a), [bslow](#note19b) to [cwrath](#note19c):

20 For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.

21 Wherefore lay apart all [afilthiness](#note21a) and [bsuperfluity of naughtiness](#note21b), and receive with [cmeekness](#note21c) the [dengrafted](#note21d) word, which is able to save your souls.

22 But be ye [adoers](#note22a) of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.

23 For if any be a [ahearer](#note23a) of the word, and not a doer, he is like unto a man beholding his natural face in a [bglass](#note23b):

24 For he beholdeth himself, and goeth his way, and straightway forgetteth what manner of man he was.

25 But whoso looketh into the perfect [alaw](#note25a) of [bliberty](#note25b), and continueth therein, he being not a forgetful hearer, but a doer of the work, this man shall be blessed in his deed.

26 If any man among you seem to be religious, and bridleth not his [atongue](#note26a), but deceiveth his own heart, this man’s religion is [bvain](#note26b).

27 Pure [areligion](#note27a) and undefiled before God and the Father is this, To [bvisit](#note27b) the [cfatherless](#note27c) and [dwidows](#note27d) in their [eaffliction](#note27e), and to keep himself [funspotted](#note27f) from [gthe](#note27g) [hworld](#note27h).

Chapter 2

God has chosen the poor of this world who are rich in faith—Salvation is gained by keeping the whole law—Faith without works is dead.

1 My brethren, [ahave](#note1a) not the faith of our [bLord Jesus Christ](#note1b), the Lord of glory, with respect of persons.

2 For if there come unto your assembly a man with a gold ring, in goodly apparel, and there come in also a [apoor](#note2a) man in [bvile](#note2b) raiment;

3 And ye have respect to him that weareth the [agay](#note3a) clothing, and say unto him, Sit thou here in a good place; and say to the poor, Stand thou there, or sit here under my footstool:

4 [aAre](#note4a) ye not then partial in yourselves, and are become judges of evil thoughts?

5 Hearken, my beloved brethren, Hath not God chosen the [apoor](#note5a) of this world rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom which he hath promised to them that love him?

6 But ye have despised the poor. Do not rich men [aoppress](#note6a) you, and draw you before the judgment seats?

7 Do not they blaspheme that worthy [aname](#note7a) by the which ye are called?

8 If ye fulfil the royal law according to the scripture, Thou shalt [alove](#note8a) thy [bneighbour](#note8b) as thyself, ye do well:

9 But if ye have [arespect](#note9a) to persons, ye commit sin, and are convinced of the law as transgressors.

10 For whosoever shall keep the [awhole](#note10a) law, and yet [boffend](#note10b) in one point, he is [cguilty](#note10c) of all.

11 For he that said, Do not commit [aadultery](#note11a), said also, Do not kill. Now if thou commit no adultery, yet if thou kill, thou art become a transgressor of the law.

12 So speak ye, and so do, as they that shall be judged by the law of [aliberty](#note12a).

13 For he shall have [ajudgment](#note13a) without [bmercy](#note13b), that hath shewed no mercy; and mercy rejoiceth against judgment.

14 [aWhat](#note14a) doth it profit, my brethren, though a man say he hath [bfaith](#note14b), and have not works? can faith save him?

15 If a [abrother](#note15a) or sister be naked, and destitute of daily [bfood](#note15b),

16 And one of you say unto them, Depart in peace, be ye warmed and filled; notwithstanding ye [agive](#note16a) them not those things which are needful to the body; what doth it profit?

17 Even so [afaith](#note17a), if it hath not [bworks](#note17b), is dead, being alone.

18 Yea, a man may say, Thou hast faith, and I have works: shew me thy faith without thy works, and I will shew thee my [afaith](#note18a) by my [bworks](#note18b).

19 Thou believest that there is one God; thou doest well: the [adevils](#note19a) also [bbelieve](#note19b), and tremble.

20 But wilt thou know, O vain man, that [afaith](#note20a) without [bworks](#note20b) is dead?

21 Was not Abraham our father [ajustified](#note21a) by works, when he had [boffered](#note21b) Isaac his son upon the altar?

22 Seest thou how faith wrought with his works, and by [aworks](#note22a) was faith made [bperfect](#note22b)?

23 And the scripture was fulfilled which saith, Abraham [abelieved](#note23a) God, and it was imputed unto him for [brighteousness](#note23b): and he was called the [cFriend](#note23c) of God.

24 Ye see then how that by [aworks](#note24a) a man is [bjustified](#note24b), and not by faith only.

25 Likewise also was not [aRahab](#note25a) the harlot [bjustified](#note25b) by works, when she had received the messengers, and had sent them out another way?

26 For as the body without the [aspirit](#note26a) is [bdead](#note26b), so faith without [cworks](#note26c) is dead also.

Chapter 3

By governing the tongue, we gain perfection—Heavenly wisdom is pure, peaceable, and full of mercy.

1 My brethren, [abe](#note1a) not many [bmasters](#note1b), knowing that we shall [creceive](#note1c) the greater condemnation.

2 For in many things we [aoffend all](#note2a). If any man [boffend not](#note2b) in [cword](#note2c), the same is a [dperfect](#note2d) man, and able also to bridle the whole body.

3 Behold, we put bits in the horses’ mouths, that they may obey us; and we turn about their whole body.

4 Behold also the ships, which though they be so great, and are driven of fierce winds, yet are they turned about with a very small helm, whithersoever the [agovernor](#note4a) listeth.

5 Even so the tongue is a little member, and [aboasteth](#note5a) great things. Behold, how great a [bmatter](#note5b) a little fire kindleth!

6 And the [atongue](#note6a) is a fire, a world of iniquity: so is the tongue among our members, that it defileth the whole body, and setteth on fire the course of nature; and it is set on fire of hell.

7 For every kind of beasts, and of birds, and of serpents, and of things in the sea, is tamed, and hath been tamed of mankind:

8 But the [atongue](#note8a) can no man tame; it is an [bunruly](#note8b) evil, full of deadly poison.

9 Therewith bless we God, even the Father; and therewith [acurse](#note9a) we men, which are made after the [bsimilitude](#note9b) of God.

10 Out of the same [amouth](#note10a) proceedeth blessing and cursing. My brethren, these things ought not so to be.

11 Doth a [afountain](#note11a) send forth at the same place sweet water and bitter?

12 Can the fig tree, my brethren, bear olive berries? either a vine, figs? so can no fountain both yield salt water and fresh.

13 Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? let him shew out of a good [aconversation](#note13a) his works with [bmeekness](#note13b) of wisdom.

14 But if ye have bitter envying and [astrife](#note14a) in your hearts, [bglory not](#note14b), and lie not against the truth.

15 This wisdom descendeth not from above, but is earthly, sensual, devilish.

16 For where [aenvying](#note16a) and [bstrife](#note16b) is, there is confusion and every evil work.

17 But the [awisdom](#note17a) that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and [beasy to be entreated](#note17b), full of [cmercy](#note17c) and good fruits, without partiality, and without [dhypocrisy](#note17d).

18 And the fruit of [arighteousness](#note18a) is sown in [bpeace](#note18b) of them that make [cpeace](#note18c).

Chapter 4

Wars are born of lusts—The friends of the world are the enemies of God—Sin is failure to walk in the light we have received.

1 From whence come [awars](#note1a) and [bfightings](#note1b) among you? come they not hence, even of your [clusts](#note1c) that war in your members?

2 Ye lust, and have not: ye kill, and desire to have, and cannot obtain: ye fight and war, yet ye have not, because ye ask not.

3 Ye [aask](#note3a), and receive not, because ye [bask](#note3b) [camiss](#note3c), that ye may [dconsume](#note3d) it upon your [elusts](#note3e).

4 Ye [aadulterers](#note4a) and adulteresses, know ye not that the friendship of the [bworld](#note4b) is [cenmity](#note4c) with God? whosoever therefore will be a friend of the world is the [denemy](#note4d) of God.

5 Do ye think that the scripture saith in vain, The spirit that dwelleth in us lusteth to [aenvy](#note5a)?

6 But he giveth more [agrace](#note6a). Wherefore he saith, God resisteth the [bproud](#note6b), but giveth grace unto the [chumble](#note6c).

7 [aSubmit](#note7a) yourselves therefore to God. [bResist](#note7b) the [cdevil](#note7c), and he will flee from you.

8 [aDraw](#note8a) [bnigh](#note8b) to God, and he will draw nigh to you. [cCleanse](#note8c) your hands, ye sinners; and [dpurify](#note8d) your hearts, ye double minded.

9 [aBe afflicted](#note9a), and mourn, and [bweep](#note9b): let your laughter be turned to [cmourning](#note9c), and your joy to heaviness.

10 [aHumble](#note10a) yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he shall lift you up.

11 [aSpeak](#note11a) not evil one of another, brethren. He that speaketh evil of his brother, and judgeth his brother, speaketh evil of the [blaw](#note11b), and judgeth the law: but if thou judge the law, thou art not a doer of the law, but a judge.

12 There is one lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy: who art thou that [ajudgest](#note12a) another?

13 Go to now, ye that say, To day or to [amorrow](#note13a) we will go into such a city, and continue there a year, and buy and sell, and get gain:

14 Whereas ye know not what shall be on the morrow. For what is your life? It is even a [avapour](#note14a), that appeareth for a little time, and then vanisheth away.

15 For that ye ought to say, If the Lord will, we shall live, and do this, or that.

16 But now ye rejoice in your [aboastings](#note16a): all such rejoicing is evil.

17 Therefore to him that [aknoweth](#note17a) to do good, and [bdoeth](#note17b) it not, to him it is [csin](#note17c).

Chapter 5

Misery awaits the wanton rich—Await the Lord’s coming with patience—The elders are to anoint and heal the sick.

1 Go to now, ye rich men, [aweep](#note1a) and howl for your miseries that shall come upon you.

2 Your [ariches](#note2a) are corrupted, and your garments are motheaten.

3 Your [agold](#note3a) and silver is [bcankered](#note3b); and the [crust](#note3c) of them shall be a witness against you, and shall eat your flesh as it were fire. Ye have heaped treasure together for the last days.

4 Behold, the [ahire](#note4a) of the [blabourers](#note4b) who have reaped down your fields, which is of you kept back by fraud, crieth: and the cries of them which have reaped are entered into the ears of the Lord of sabaoth.

5 Ye have lived [ain pleasure](#note5a) on the earth, and been wanton; ye have nourished your hearts, as in a day of slaughter.

6 Ye have condemned and killed the just; and he doth not resist you.

7 Be patient therefore, brethren, unto the coming of the Lord. Behold, the husbandman waiteth for the precious fruit of the earth, and hath long patience for it, until he receive the early and latter rain.

8 Be ye also [apatient](#note8a); [bstablish](#note8b) your hearts: for the [ccoming](#note8c) of the Lord draweth nigh.

9 [aGrudge](#note9a) not one against another, brethren, lest ye be condemned: behold, the judge standeth before the door.

10 Take, my brethren, the prophets, who have spoken in the name of the Lord, for an [aexample](#note10a) of [bsuffering](#note10b) affliction, and of patience.

11 Behold, we count them [ahappy](#note11a) which [bendure](#note11b). Ye have heard of the patience of [cJob](#note11c), and have seen the end of the Lord; that the Lord is very [dpitiful](#note11d), and of tender [emercy](#note11e).

12 But above all things, my brethren, [aswear](#note12a) not, neither by heaven, neither by the earth, neither by any other [boath](#note12b): but let your yea be yea; and your nay, nay; lest ye fall into condemnation.

13 Is any among you [aafflicted](#note13a)? let him pray. Is any merry? let him sing psalms.

14 Is any [asick](#note14a) among you? let him call for the [belders](#note14b) of the church; and let them [cpray](#note14c) over him, [danointing](#note14d) him with oil in the name of the Lord:

15 And the [aprayer](#note15a) of [bfaith](#note15b) shall save the sick, and the Lord shall raise him up; and if he have committed sins, they shall be [cforgiven](#note15c) him.

16 [aConfess](#note16a) your faults one to another, and [bpray](#note16b) one for another, that ye may be healed. [cThe](#note16c) effectual [dfervent](#note16d) [eprayer](#note16e) of a righteous man availeth much.

17 Elias was a man subject to like [apassions](#note17a) as we are, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain: and it [brained](#note17b) not on the earth by the space of three years and six months.

18 And he prayed again, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth brought forth her fruit.

19 Brethren, if any of you do err from the truth, and one convert him;

20 Let him know, that he which [aconverteth](#note20a) the sinner from the error of his way shall [bsave](#note20b) a soul from death, and shall [chide](#note20c) a multitude of sins.

# The First Epistle General of Peter

* [1 Peter 1](1-pet/1.xhtml)
* [1 Peter 2](1-pet/2.xhtml)
* [1 Peter 3](1-pet/3.xhtml)
* [1 Peter 4](1-pet/4.xhtml)
* [1 Peter 5](1-pet/5.xhtml)

Chapter 1

The trial of our faith precedes salvation—Christ was foreordained to be the Redeemer.

1 Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the [astrangers](#note1a) scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

2 [aElect](#note2a) according to the [bforeknowledge](#note2b) of God the Father, through [csanctification](#note2c) of the Spirit, unto [dobedience](#note2d) and [esprinkling](#note2e) of the [fblood](#note2f) of Jesus Christ: Grace unto you, and peace, be multiplied.

3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant [amercy](#note3a) hath [bbegotten](#note3b) us again unto a [clively](#note3c) [dhope](#note3d) by the resurrection of [eJesus Christ](#note3e) from the dead,

4 To an [ainheritance](#note4a) incorruptible, and undefiled, and that fadeth not away, reserved in heaven for you,

5 Who are kept by the [apower](#note5a) of God through [bfaith](#note5b) unto salvation [cready](#note5c) to be revealed in the last time.

6 Wherein ye greatly rejoice, though now for a [aseason](#note6a), if need be, ye are in heaviness through manifold [btemptations](#note6b):

7 That the [atrial](#note7a) of your faith, being much more precious than of gold that perisheth, though it be tried with fire, might be found unto praise and honour and glory at the [bappearing](#note7b) of Jesus Christ:

8 Whom having not seen, ye love; in whom, though now ye see him not, yet [abelieving](#note8a), ye rejoice with joy unspeakable and full of glory:

9 Receiving the [aend](#note9a) of your [bfaith](#note9b), even the [csalvation](#note9c) of your souls.

10 Of which [asalvation](#note10a) the [bprophets](#note10b) have inquired and searched diligently, who prophesied of the grace that should come unto you:

11 Searching what, or what manner of time the Spirit of Christ which was in them did signify, when it [atestified](#note11a) beforehand the [bsufferings](#note11b) of Christ, and the [cglory](#note11c) that should follow.

12 Unto whom it was revealed, that not unto themselves, but unto us they did minister the things, which are now reported unto you by them that have [apreached](#note12a) the gospel unto you with the Holy Ghost sent down from heaven; which things the [bangels](#note12b) desire to look into.

13 Wherefore gird up the loins of your mind, be [asober](#note13a), and hope [bto the end](#note13b) for the [cgrace](#note13c) that is to be brought unto you at the revelation of Jesus Christ;

14 As obedient children, not fashioning yourselves according to the former [alusts](#note14a) in your [bignorance](#note14b):

15 But as he which hath called you is holy, so be ye [aholy](#note15a) in all manner of [bconversation](#note15b);

16 Because it is written, Be ye holy; for I am [aholy](#note16a).

17 And if ye call on the Father, who without respect of persons judgeth according to every man’s [awork](#note17a), pass the time of your sojourning here in [bfear](#note17b):

18 Forasmuch as ye know that ye were not [aredeemed](#note18a) with corruptible things, as silver and gold, from your [bvain conversation](#note18b) received by [ctradition](#note18c) from your fathers;

19 But with the precious [ablood](#note19a) of Christ, as of a [blamb](#note19b) without [cblemish](#note19c) and without spot:

20 [aWho](#note20a) verily was [bforeordained](#note20b) before the foundation of the world, but was manifest in these last times for you,

21 Who by him do believe in God, that raised him up from the dead, and gave him [aglory](#note21a); that your [bfaith](#note21b) and [chope](#note21c) might be in God.

22 Seeing ye have [apurified](#note22a) your [bsouls](#note22b) in obeying the truth through the Spirit unto unfeigned [clove](#note22c) of the brethren, see that ye [dlove](#note22d) one another with a pure heart fervently:

23 Being [aborn again](#note23a), not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and [babideth](#note23b) for ever.

24 For all [aflesh](#note24a) is as grass, and all the glory of man as the flower of grass. The grass withereth, and the flower thereof falleth away:

25 But the [aword](#note25a) of the Lord endureth for ever. And this is the word which by the gospel is preached unto you.

Chapter 2

Converts are newborn babes in Christ—He is the chief cornerstone—Saints hold a royal priesthood and are a peculiar people—Saints are in subjection to the laws of man.

1 Wherefore laying aside all [amalice](#note1a), and all [bguile](#note1b), and hypocrisies, and envies, and all evil [cspeakings](#note1c),

2 As [anewborn](#note2a) babes, desire the [bsincere](#note2b) [cmilk](#note2c) of the word, that ye may grow thereby:

3 If so be ye have [atasted](#note3a) that the Lord is gracious.

4 To whom coming, as unto a living stone, disallowed indeed of men, but [achosen](#note4a) of God, and precious,

5 Ye also, as [alively](#note5a) stones, are built up a [bspiritual](#note5b) [chouse](#note5c), an holy priesthood, to offer up [dspiritual](#note5d) [esacrifices](#note5e), acceptable to God by Jesus Christ.

6 Wherefore also it is contained in the scripture, Behold, I lay in Sion a chief [acorner](#note6a) [bstone](#note6b), elect, precious: and he that [cbelieveth](#note6c) on him shall not be [dconfounded](#note6d).

7 Unto you therefore which believe he is precious: but unto them which be [adisobedient](#note7a), the stone which the builders [bdisallowed](#note7b), the same is made the head of the corner,

8 And a [astone](#note8a) of [bstumbling](#note8b), and a [crock](#note8c) of [doffence](#note8d), even to them which [estumble](#note8e) at the word, being disobedient: whereunto also they were [fappointed](#note8f).

9 But ye are a [achosen](#note9a) generation, a [broyal](#note9b) [cpriesthood](#note9c), an [dholy](#note9d) [enation](#note9e), a [fpeculiar](#note9f) people; that ye should shew forth the praises of him who hath called you out of [gdarkness](#note9g) into his marvellous [hlight](#note9h):

10 Which in time past were not a people, but are now the people of God: which had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.

11 Dearly beloved, I beseech you as [astrangers](#note11a) and [bpilgrims](#note11b), [cabstain](#note11c) from fleshly [dlusts](#note11d), which [ewar](#note11e) against the soul;

12 Having your conversation [ahonest](#note12a) among the Gentiles: that, whereas they speak against you as evildoers, they may by your good [bworks](#note12b), which they shall behold, glorify God in the day of visitation.

13 [aSubmit](#note13a) yourselves to every [bordinance](#note13b) of man for the Lord’s sake: whether it be to the king, as [csupreme](#note13c);

14 Or unto governors, as unto them that are sent by him for the punishment of evildoers, and for the praise of them that do well.

15 For so is the will of God, that with well doing ye may put to [asilence](#note15a) the [bignorance](#note15b) of foolish men:

16 As free, and not using your [aliberty](#note16a) for a cloak of [bmaliciousness](#note16b), but as the [cservants](#note16c) of God.

17 [aHonour](#note17a) all men. Love the [bbrotherhood](#note17b). [cFear](#note17c) God. Honour the [dking](#note17d).

18 [aServants](#note18a), be subject to your [bmasters](#note18b) with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the [cfroward](#note18c).

19 For this is [athankworthy](#note19a), if a man for conscience toward God endure [bgrief](#note19b), suffering wrongfully.

20 For what glory is it, if, when ye be buffeted for your faults, ye shall take it [apatiently](#note20a)? but if, when ye do well, and [bsuffer](#note20b) for it, ye take it [cpatiently](#note20c), this is [dacceptable](#note20d) with God.

21 For even hereunto were ye called: because Christ also [asuffered](#note21a) for us, leaving us an [bexample](#note21b), that ye should follow his steps:

22 Who did no [asin](#note22a), neither was [bguile](#note22b) found in his mouth:

23 Who, when he was [areviled](#note23a), reviled not again; when he suffered, he threatened not; but [bcommitted](#note23b) himself to him that judgeth righteously:

24 Who his own self [abare](#note24a) our [bsins](#note24b) in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness: by whose [cstripes](#note24c) ye were healed.

25 For ye were as [asheep](#note25a) going astray; but are now returned unto the [bShepherd](#note25b) and [cBishop](#note25c) of your souls.

Chapter 3

Husbands and wives should honor each other—Saints should live by gospel standards—Christ preached to the spirits in prison.

1 Likewise, ye [awives](#note1a), be in subjection to your own husbands; that, if any [bobey not](#note1b) the word, they also may without the word be won by the [cconversation](#note1c) of the wives;

2 While they behold your [achaste](#note2a) [bconversation](#note2b) coupled with fear.

3 Whose adorning let it not be that outward [aadorning](#note3a) of plaiting the hair, and of wearing of gold, or of putting on of apparel;

4 But let it be the hidden man of the heart, in that which is not corruptible, even the [aornament](#note4a) of a [bmeek](#note4b) and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price.

5 For after this manner in the old time the holy [awomen](#note5a) also, who [btrusted](#note5b) in God, adorned themselves, being [cin subjection](#note5c) unto their own husbands:

6 Even as Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord: whose [adaughters](#note6a) ye are, as long as ye do well, and are not afraid with any [bamazement](#note6b).

7 Likewise, ye [ahusbands](#note7a), dwell with them according to [bknowledge](#note7b), giving [chonour](#note7c) unto the [dwife](#note7d), as unto the weaker vessel, and as being [eheirs](#note7e) together of the grace of life; that your [fprayers](#note7f) be not hindered.

8 Finally, be ye all of [aone](#note8a) mind, having [bcompassion](#note8b) one of another, love as [cbrethren](#note8c), be [dpitiful](#note8d), be [ecourteous](#note8e):

9 Not [arendering](#note9a) [bevil](#note9b) for evil, or railing for railing: but contrariwise blessing; knowing that ye are thereunto called, that ye should inherit a [cblessing](#note9c).

10 For he that will love life, and see good days, let him [arefrain](#note10a) his [btongue](#note10b) from evil, and his lips that they speak no [cguile](#note10c):

11 Let him [aeschew](#note11a) evil, and do good; let him seek [bpeace](#note11b), and [censue](#note11c) it.

12 For the [aeyes](#note12a) of the Lord are over the [brighteous](#note12b), and his ears are open unto their [cprayers](#note12c): but the face of the Lord is against them that do [devil](#note12d).

13 And who is he that will harm you, if ye be [afollowers](#note13a) of that which is good?

14 But and if ye [asuffer](#note14a) for [brighteousness’](#note14b) sake, [chappy](#note14c) are ye: and be not afraid of their terror, neither be troubled;

15 But [asanctify](#note15a) the Lord God in your hearts: and be ready always to give [ban answer](#note15b) to every man that asketh you a reason of the [chope](#note15c) that is in you with [dmeekness](#note15d) and [efear](#note15e):

16 Having a good [aconscience](#note16a); that, whereas they speak evil of you, as of evildoers, they may be ashamed that falsely accuse your good [bconversation](#note16b) in Christ.

17 For it is better, if the will of God be so, that ye [asuffer](#note17a) for well doing, than for evil doing.

18 For Christ also hath once [asuffered](#note18a) for sins, the just for the [bunjust](#note18b), that he might bring us to God, being put to [cdeath](#note18c) in the flesh, but quickened by the [dSpirit](#note18d):

19 By which also he went and [apreached](#note19a) unto the [bspirits](#note19b) in [cprison](#note19c);

20 [aWhich](#note20a) sometime were [bdisobedient](#note20b), when once the [clongsuffering](#note20c) of God waited in the days of [dNoah](#note20d), while the ark was a preparing, wherein few, that is, eight souls were [esaved](#note20e) by [fwater](#note20f).

21 The like figure whereunto even [abaptism](#note21a) doth also now save us (not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God,) by the resurrection of Jesus Christ:

22 Who is gone into [aheaven](#note22a), and is on the right hand of God; [bangels](#note22b) and [cauthorities](#note22c) and powers being made subject unto him.

Chapter 4

Peter explains why the gospel is preached to the dead—Saints should speak as the oracles of God—The righteous will be tried and tested in all things.

1 Forasmuch then as Christ hath suffered for us in the flesh, arm yourselves likewise with the same [amind](#note1a): [bfor](#note1b) he that hath suffered in the flesh hath ceased from [csin](#note1c);

2 That he no longer should live the rest of his time in the flesh to the [alusts](#note2a) of men, but to the will of God.

3 For the time past of our life may suffice us to have wrought the will of the Gentiles, when we walked in lasciviousness, lusts, [aexcess](#note3a) of wine, [brevellings](#note3b), banquetings, and [cabominable](#note3c) [didolatries](#note3d):

4 Wherein they think it strange that ye run not with them to the same excess of riot, speaking evil of you:

5 Who shall give account to him that is ready to [ajudge](#note5a) the [bquick](#note5b) and the dead.

6 [aFor](#note6a) for this cause was the [bgospel](#note6b) [cpreached](#note6c) also to them that are [ddead](#note6d), that they might be [ejudged](#note6e) according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the spirit.

7 [aBut](#note7a) the end of all things is at hand: be ye therefore sober, and [bwatch](#note7b) unto prayer.

8 And above all things have fervent charity among yourselves: [afor](#note8a) [bcharity](#note8b) shall cover the multitude of sins.

9 Use [ahospitality](#note9a) one to another without [bgrudging](#note9b).

10 As every man hath received the gift, even so [aminister](#note10a) the same one to another, as good [bstewards](#note10b) of the manifold grace of God.

11 If any man [aspeak](#note11a), let him speak as the [boracles](#note11b) of God; if any man minister, let him do it as of the ability which God giveth: that God in all things may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom be praise and dominion for ever and ever. Amen.

12 Beloved, think it not strange concerning the fiery [atrial](#note12a) which is to try you, as though some strange thing happened unto you:

13 But [arejoice](#note13a), inasmuch as ye are [bpartakers](#note13b) of Christ’s [csufferings](#note13c); that, when his glory shall be [drevealed](#note13d), ye may be glad also with exceeding joy.

14 If ye be [areproached](#note14a) for the name of Christ, [bhappy](#note14b) are ye; for the spirit of glory and of God resteth upon you: on their part he is evil spoken of, but on your part he is glorified.

15 But let none of you suffer as a murderer, or as a thief, or as an evildoer, or as a busybody in other men’s matters.

16 Yet if any man suffer as a [aChristian](#note16a), let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God on this behalf.

17 For the time is come that [ajudgment](#note17a) must begin at the house of God: and if it first begin at us, what shall the end be of them that [bobey not](#note17b) the gospel of God?

18 And if the [arighteous](#note18a) scarcely be saved, where shall the [bungodly](#note18b) and the sinner appear?

19 Wherefore let them that suffer according to the [awill](#note19a) of God commit the [bkeeping](#note19b) of their souls to him in well doing, as unto a faithful Creator.

Chapter 5

The elders are to feed the flock of God—Humility and godly graces lead to perfection.

1 The [aelders](#note1a) which are among you I exhort, who am also an elder, and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and also a [bpartaker](#note1b) of the glory that shall be revealed:

2 [aFeed](#note2a) the [bflock](#note2b) of God which is among you, taking the [coversight](#note2c) thereof, not by constraint, but [dwillingly](#note2d); not for [efilthy](#note2e) [flucre](#note2f), but of a ready mind;

3 Neither as being [alords](#note3a) over God’s heritage, but being [bensamples](#note3b) to the flock.

4 And when the chief [aShepherd](#note4a) shall appear, ye shall receive a [bcrown](#note4b) of [cglory](#note4c) that fadeth not away.

5 Likewise, ye younger, [asubmit](#note5a) yourselves unto the elder. Yea, all of you be subject one to another, and be clothed with [bhumility](#note5b): for God [cresisteth](#note5c) the [dproud](#note5d), and giveth grace to the [ehumble](#note5e).

6 [aHumble](#note6a) yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time:

7 Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.

8 Be [asober](#note8a), be [bvigilant](#note8b); because your adversary the [cdevil](#note8c), as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour:

9 Whom resist [asteadfast](#note9a) in the faith, knowing that the same afflictions are [baccomplished in](#note9b) your brethren that are in the world.

10 But the God of all [agrace](#note10a), who hath called us unto his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after that ye have suffered a while, make you [bperfect](#note10b), stablish, strengthen, settle you.

11 To him be glory and dominion for ever and ever. Amen.

12 By [aSilvanus](#note12a), a faithful brother unto you, as I suppose, I have written briefly, exhorting, and testifying that this is the true grace of God wherein ye stand.

13 The church that is at [aBabylon](#note13a), elected together with you, saluteth you; and so doth Marcus my son.

14 Greet ye one another with a kiss of charity. Peace be with you all that are in Christ Jesus. Amen.

# The Second Epistle General of Peter

* [2 Peter 1](2-pet/1.xhtml)
* [2 Peter 2](2-pet/2.xhtml)
* [2 Peter 3](2-pet/3.xhtml)

Chapter 1

Peter urges the Saints to make their calling and election sure—Prophecy comes by the power of the Holy Ghost.

1 Simon Peter, a [aservant](#note1a) and an [bapostle](#note1b) of Jesus Christ, to them that have obtained [clike precious](#note1c) faith with us through the [drighteousness](#note1d) [eof](#note1e) God and our Saviour Jesus Christ:

2 Grace and peace be multiplied unto you through the [aknowledge](#note2a) of God, and of Jesus our Lord,

3 According as his divine power hath given unto us [aall](#note3a) things that pertain unto [blife](#note3b) and [cgodliness](#note3c), through the knowledge of him that hath called us [dto](#note3d) glory and [evirtue](#note3e):

4 Whereby are given unto us exceeding great and precious [apromises](#note4a): that by these ye might be [bpartakers](#note4b) of the [cdivine](#note4c) [dnature](#note4d), having [eescaped](#note4e) the [fcorruption](#note4f) that is in the world through [glust](#note4g).

5 And beside this, giving all [adiligence](#note5a), add to your faith [bvirtue](#note5b); and to virtue [cknowledge](#note5c);

6 And to knowledge [atemperance](#note6a); and to temperance [bpatience](#note6b); and to patience [cgodliness](#note6c);

7 And to godliness [abrotherly](#note7a) [bkindness](#note7b); and to brotherly kindness [ccharity](#note7c).

8 For if these things be in you, and [aabound](#note8a), they make you that ye shall neither be [bbarren](#note8b) nor [cunfruitful](#note8c) in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.

9 But he that lacketh these things is blind, and cannot see afar off, and hath forgotten that he was purged from his old sins.

10 Wherefore the rather, brethren, give diligence to make your calling and [aelection](#note10a) sure: for if ye do these things, ye shall never [bfall](#note10b):

11 For so an entrance shall be ministered unto you abundantly into the [aeverlasting](#note11a) [bkingdom](#note11b) of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ.

12 Wherefore I will not be negligent to put you always in remembrance of these things, though ye know them, and be established in the present truth.

13 Yea, I think it [ameet](#note13a), as long as I am in this [btabernacle](#note13b), to stir you up by putting you in remembrance;

14 Knowing that shortly I must put off this my tabernacle, even as our Lord Jesus Christ hath [ashewed](#note14a) me.

15 Moreover I will endeavour that ye may be able after my decease to have these things always in remembrance.

16 For we have not followed cunningly devised [afables](#note16a), when we made known unto you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but were [beyewitnesses](#note16b) of his majesty.

17 For he received from God the Father honour and glory, when there came such a voice to him from the excellent glory, This is my [abeloved Son](#note17a), in whom I am well pleased.

18 And this [avoice](#note18a) which came from heaven we heard, when we were with him in the holy [bmount](#note18b).

19 [aWe](#note19a) have also a more [bsure](#note19b) word of [cprophecy](#note19c); whereunto ye do well that ye take heed, as unto a light that shineth in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day [dstar](#note19d) arise in your hearts:

20 Knowing this first, that [ano](#note20a) [bprophecy](#note20b) of the [cscripture](#note20c) is of any private [dinterpretation](#note20d).

21 For the [aprophecy](#note21a) came not in old time by the will of man: but holy men of God [bspake](#note21b) as they were [cmoved](#note21c) by the [dHoly Ghost](#note21d).

Chapter 2

False teachers among the Saints are damned—Lustful Saints will perish in their own corruption.

1 But there were [afalse prophets](#note1a) also among the people, even as there shall be [bfalse](#note1b) [cteachers](#note1c) among you, who privily shall bring in damnable heresies, even denying the Lord that [dbought](#note1d) them, and bring upon themselves swift destruction.

2 And many shall follow their pernicious ways; by reason of whom the way of [atruth](#note2a) shall be evil spoken of.

3 And through covetousness shall they with [afeigned](#note3a) words make merchandise of you: whose [bjudgment](#note3b) now of a long time lingereth not, and their [cdamnation](#note3c) slumbereth not.

4 For if God spared not the [aangels](#note4a) that sinned, but cast them down to [bhell](#note4b), and delivered them into chains of darkness, to be reserved unto [cjudgment](#note4c);

5 And spared not the old world, but saved [aNoah](#note5a) the eighth person, a preacher of [brighteousness](#note5b), bringing in the [cflood](#note5c) upon the world of the ungodly;

6 And turning the cities of [aSodom](#note6a) and Gomorrha into ashes condemned them with an overthrow, making them an [bensample](#note6b) unto those that after should live ungodly;

7 And delivered just [aLot](#note7a), [bvexed](#note7b) with the [cfilthy](#note7c) conversation of the wicked:

8 (For that righteous man dwelling among them, in seeing and hearing, [avexed](#note8a) his righteous soul from day to day with their unlawful deeds;)

9 The Lord knoweth how to [adeliver](#note9a) the [bgodly](#note9b) out of [ctemptations](#note9c), and to [dreserve](#note9d) the unjust unto the day of [ejudgment](#note9e) to be punished:

10 But chiefly them that [awalk](#note10a) after the flesh in the [blust](#note10b) of [cuncleanness](#note10c), and despise [dgovernment](#note10d). [ePresumptuous](#note10e) are they, selfwilled, they are not afraid to speak evil of dignities.

11 Whereas angels, which are greater in power and might, bring not railing accusation against them before the Lord.

12 But these, as [anatural](#note12a) brute beasts, made to be taken and destroyed, speak evil of the things that they understand not; and shall utterly [bperish](#note12b) in their own corruption;

13 And shall receive the [areward](#note13a) of unrighteousness, as they that count it [bpleasure](#note13b) to [criot](#note13c) in the day time. Spots they are and blemishes, sporting themselves with their own deceivings while they feast with you;

14 Having eyes full of adultery, and that cannot cease from sin; beguiling unstable souls: an heart they have exercised with covetous practices; cursed children:

15 Which have [aforsaken](#note15a) the right [bway](#note15b), and are gone [castray](#note15c), following the way of [dBalaam](#note15d) the son of Bosor, who loved the [ewages](#note15e) of unrighteousness;

16 But was rebuked for his iniquity: the dumb [aass](#note16a) speaking with man’s voice forbad the madness of the prophet.

17 These are [awells](#note17a) without water, clouds that are carried with a tempest; to whom the [bmist](#note17b) of [cdarkness](#note17c) is reserved for ever.

18 For when they [aspeak](#note18a) great swelling words of [bvanity](#note18b), they [callure](#note18c) through the [dlusts](#note18d) of the flesh, through much wantonness, those that were clean [eescaped](#note18e) from them who live in error.

19 While they promise them [aliberty](#note19a), they themselves are the [bservants](#note19b) of [ccorruption](#note19c): for of whom a man is overcome, of the same is he brought in [dbondage](#note19d).

20 For if after they have [aescaped](#note20a) the [bpollutions](#note20b) of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, they are [cagain](#note20c) [dentangled](#note20d) therein, and [eovercome](#note20e), the latter end is [fworse](#note20f) with them than the beginning.

21 For it had been better for them not to have [aknown](#note21a) the way of [brighteousness](#note21b), than, after they have known it, to [cturn](#note21c) from the holy commandment delivered unto them.

22 But it is happened unto them according to the true proverb, The dog is turned to his own [avomit](#note22a) again; and the sow that was [bwashed](#note22b) to her wallowing in the mire.

Chapter 3

Some in the latter days will doubt the Second Coming—The elements will melt at the coming of the Lord.

1 This second epistle, beloved, I now [awrite](#note1a) unto you; in both which I stir up your pure minds by way of [bremembrance](#note1b):

2 That ye may be mindful of the words which were spoken before by the holy [aprophets](#note2a), and of the commandment of us the apostles of the Lord and Saviour:

3 [aKnowing](#note3a) this first, that there shall come in the [blast days](#note3b) scoffers, walking after their own [clusts](#note3c),

4 And saying, [aWhere](#note4a) is the [bpromise](#note4b) of his [ccoming](#note4c)? for since the fathers fell asleep, all things continue as they were from the beginning of the creation.

5 For this they willingly are [aignorant](#note5a) of, that by the [bword](#note5b) of God the heavens were of old, and the [cearth](#note5c) standing out of the water and in the water:

6 Whereby the world that then was, being [aoverflowed](#note6a) with [bwater](#note6b), perished:

7 But the heavens and the earth, which are now, by the same word are kept in store, reserved unto [afire](#note7a) against the day of judgment and [bperdition](#note7b) of ungodly men.

8 But, beloved, be not ignorant of this one thing, that one day is with the Lord as a thousand [ayears](#note8a), and a thousand years as one day.

9 The Lord is not slack concerning his promise, as some men count slackness; but is [alongsuffering](#note9a) to us-ward, not willing that any should [bperish](#note9b), but that all should come to [crepentance](#note9c).

10 But the [aday](#note10a) of the Lord will [bcome](#note10b) as a thief in the night; in the which the heavens shall [cpass away](#note10c) with a great noise, and the [delements](#note10d) shall melt with fervent heat, the [eearth](#note10e) also and the works that are therein shall be burned up.

11 Seeing then that all these things shall be dissolved, what [amanner](#note11a) of persons ought ye to be in all holy [bconversation](#note11b) and godliness,

12 [aLooking](#note12a) for and [bhasting](#note12b) unto the [ccoming](#note12c) of the [dday](#note12d) of God, wherein the heavens being on fire shall be dissolved, and the elements shall melt with fervent heat?

13 Nevertheless we, according to his promise, look for new [aheavens](#note13a) and a [bnew](#note13b) [cearth](#note13c), wherein dwelleth [drighteousness](#note13d).

14 Wherefore, beloved, seeing that ye look for such things, be [adiligent](#note14a) that ye may be found of him in peace, without [bspot](#note14b), and blameless.

15 And [aaccount](#note15a) that the [blongsuffering](#note15b) of our Lord is salvation; even as our beloved brother Paul also according to the wisdom given unto him hath written unto you;

16 As also in all his epistles, speaking in them of these things; in which are some things hard to be understood, which they that are unlearned and unstable [awrest](#note16a), as they do also the other [bscriptures](#note16b), unto their own destruction.

17 Ye therefore, beloved, [aseeing](#note17a) ye know these things before, beware lest ye also, being [bled](#note17b) [caway](#note17c) with the error of the wicked, fall from your own [dsteadfastness](#note17d).

18 But grow in [agrace](#note18a), and in the knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. To him be glory both now and for ever. Amen.

# The First Epistle General of John

* [1 John 1](1-jn/1.xhtml)
* [1 John 2](1-jn/2.xhtml)
* [1 John 3](1-jn/3.xhtml)
* [1 John 4](1-jn/4.xhtml)
* [1 John 5](1-jn/5.xhtml)

Chapter 1

The Saints gain fellowship with God by obedience—We must confess our sins to gain forgiveness.

1 [aThat](#note1a) which was from the [bbeginning](#note1b), which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled, of the [cWord of life](#note1c);

2 (For the life was manifested, and we have seen it, and bear witness, and [ashew](#note2a) unto you that [beternal life](#note2b), which was with the Father, and was manifested unto us;)

3 That which we have [aseen](#note3a) and heard declare we unto you, that ye also may have fellowship with us: and truly our [bfellowship](#note3b) is with the [cFather](#note3c), and with his Son Jesus Christ.

4 And these things [awrite](#note4a) we unto you, that your joy may be full.

5 This then is the message which we have heard of him, and declare unto you, that God is [alight](#note5a), and in him is no [bdarkness](#note5b) at all.

6 If we say that we have fellowship with him, and [awalk](#note6a) in [bdarkness](#note6b), we lie, and do not the truth:

7 But if we [awalk](#note7a) in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship one with another, and the [bblood](#note7b) of Jesus Christ his Son [ccleanseth](#note7c) us from all sin.

8 If we say that we have [ano](#note8a) [bsin](#note8b), we [cdeceive](#note8c) ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

9 If we [aconfess](#note9a) our sins, he is faithful and [bjust](#note9b) to [cforgive](#note9c) us our sins, and to [dcleanse](#note9d) us from all unrighteousness.

10 If we say that we have [anot sinned](#note10a), we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.

Chapter 2

Christ is our Advocate with the Father—We know God by obedience—Love not the world—Anti-Christs will come in the last days.

1 My little children, these things write I unto you, that ye sin not. [aAnd](#note1a) if any man sin, we have an [badvocate](#note1b) with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous:

2 And he is the [apropitiation](#note2a) for our sins: and not for ours only, but also for the sins of the whole [bworld](#note2b).

3 And hereby we do know that we know him, if we [akeep](#note3a) his commandments.

4 He that saith, I know him, and keepeth not his [acommandments](#note4a), is a [bliar](#note4b), and the truth is not in him.

5 But whoso [akeepeth](#note5a) his word, in him verily is the love of God perfected: hereby know we that we are [bin him](#note5b).

6 He that saith he [aabideth](#note6a) in him ought himself also so to [bwalk](#note6b), even as he walked.

7 [aBrethren](#note7a), I write no new commandment unto you, but an old commandment which ye had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which ye have heard from the beginning.

8 Again, a new commandment I write unto you, [awhich](#note8a) thing is true in him and in you: because the [bdarkness](#note8b) is [cpast](#note8c), and the true [dlight](#note8d) now shineth.

9 He that saith he is in the light, and hateth his brother, is in darkness even until now.

10 He that [aloveth](#note10a) his [bbrother](#note10b) abideth in the light, and there is none occasion of stumbling in him.

11 But he that hateth his brother is in darkness, and [awalketh](#note11a) in darkness, and knoweth not whither he goeth, because that darkness hath blinded his eyes.

12 I [awrite](#note12a) unto you, little [bchildren](#note12b), because your sins are forgiven you [cfor](#note12c) his [dname’s](#note12d) sake.

13 I write unto you, fathers, because ye have known him that is from the beginning. I write unto you, young men, because ye have overcome the wicked one. I write unto you, little children, because ye have known the Father.

14 I have written unto you, fathers, because ye have known him that is from the beginning. I have written unto you, young men, because ye are strong, and the word of God abideth in you, and ye have overcome the wicked one.

15 Love not the [aworld](#note15a), neither the things that are in the [bworld](#note15b). If any man love the world, the [clove](#note15c) of the Father is not in him.

16 For all that is in the world, the [alust](#note16a) of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the [bpride](#note16b) of life, is not of the Father, but is of the [cworld](#note16c).

17 And the [aworld](#note17a) [bpasseth](#note17b) away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the [cwill](#note17c) of God [dabideth](#note17d) for ever.

18 Little children, it is the last time: and as ye have heard that [aantichrist](#note18a) shall come, even now are there many [bantichrists](#note18b); whereby we know that it is the last time.

19 They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would no doubt have continued with us: but they went out, that they might be made manifest that they were not all of us.

20 But ye have an [aunction](#note20a) from the [bHoly One](#note20b), and ye know all things.

21 I have not written unto you because ye know not the truth, but because ye know it, and that no lie is of the truth.

22 Who is a [aliar](#note22a) but he that denieth that Jesus is the Christ? He is [bantichrist](#note22b), that denieth the Father and the Son.

23 Whosoever denieth the Son, the same hath not the Father: [but] he that [aacknowledgeth](#note23a) the Son hath the Father also.

24 Let that therefore abide in you, which ye have heard from the beginning. If that which ye have heard from the [abeginning](#note24a) shall remain in you, ye also shall continue in the Son, and in the Father.

25 And this is the [apromise](#note25a) that he hath promised us, even [beternal life](#note25b).

26 These things have I written unto you concerning them that [aseduce](#note26a) you.

27 But the anointing which ye have received of him abideth in you, and ye need not that any man [ateach](#note27a) you: but as the same [banointing](#note27b) [cteacheth](#note27c) you of all things, and is truth, and is no lie, and even as it hath taught you, ye shall abide in him.

28 And now, little children, abide in him; that, when he shall appear, we may have confidence, and not be ashamed before him at his coming.

29 If ye know that he is righteous, ye know that every one that doeth [arighteousness](#note29a) is [bborn](#note29b) of him.

Chapter 3

The sons of God will become like Christ—Love for others is required to gain eternal life—Obedience ensures us an answer to our prayers.

1 Behold, what manner of [alove](#note1a) the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the [bsons](#note1b) of God: therefore the [cworld](#note1c) knoweth us not, because it knew him not.

2 Beloved, now are we the [asons](#note2a) of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall [bappear](#note2b), we shall be [clike](#note2c) him; for we shall [dsee](#note2d) him as he is.

3 And every man that hath this [ahope](#note3a) in him [bpurifieth](#note3b) himself, even as he is [cpure](#note3c).

4 Whosoever committeth sin [atransgresseth](#note4a) also the law: for [bsin](#note4b) is the transgression of the law.

5 And ye know that he was manifested to take away our [asins](#note5a); and in him is no sin.

6 Whosoever abideth in him [asinneth](#note6a) not: [bwhosoever](#note6b) sinneth hath not seen him, neither [cknown](#note6c) him.

7 Little children, let no man deceive you: he that doeth righteousness is righteous, even as he is righteous.

8 He that [acommitteth](#note8a) [bsin](#note8b) is of the devil; for the devil [csinneth](#note8c) from the beginning. For this purpose the Son of God was manifested, that he might destroy the works of the [ddevil](#note8d).

9 Whosoever is [aborn of God](#note9a) [bdoth](#note9b) not commit sin; for his seed remaineth in him: and he cannot sin, because he is born of God.

10 In this the children of God are [amanifest](#note10a), and the [bchildren](#note10b) of the devil: whosoever doeth not [crighteousness](#note10c) is not of God, neither he that loveth not his brother.

11 For this is the [amessage](#note11a) that ye heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.

12 Not as [aCain](#note12a), who was of that wicked one, and slew his brother. And wherefore slew he him? Because his own works were evil, and his brother’s righteous.

13 Marvel not, my brethren, if the world hate you.

14 We know that we have passed from death unto [alife](#note14a), because we love the [bbrethren](#note14b). He that [cloveth](#note14c) not his brother abideth in [ddeath](#note14d).

15 Whosoever [ahateth](#note15a) his brother is a murderer: and ye know that no [bmurderer](#note15b) hath eternal life abiding in him.

16 Hereby perceive we the [alove](#note16a) of [bGod](#note16b), because he laid down his life for us: and we ought to lay down our lives for the brethren.

17 But whoso hath this world’s good, and seeth his [abrother](#note17a) have [bneed](#note17b), and [cshutteth](#note17c) up his bowels of [dcompassion](#note17d) from him, how dwelleth the love of God in him?

18 My little children, let us not love in word, [aneither in tongue](#note18a); but in [bdeed](#note18b) and in truth.

19 And hereby we know that we are of the truth, and shall assure our hearts before him.

20 For if our heart condemn us, God is greater than our heart, and [aknoweth](#note20a) all things.

21 Beloved, if our heart condemn us not, then have we [aconfidence](#note21a) toward God.

22 And whatsoever we [aask](#note22a), we receive of him, because we [bkeep](#note22b) his [ccommandments](#note22c), and do those things that are pleasing in his sight.

23 And this is his [acommandment](#note23a), That we should [bbelieve](#note23b) on the [cname](#note23c) of his Son Jesus Christ, and [dlove](#note23d) one another, as he gave us commandment.

24 And he that keepeth his commandments [adwelleth](#note24a) in him, and he in him. And hereby we know that he [babideth](#note24b) in us, by the [cSpirit](#note24c) which he hath given us.

Chapter 4

Try the spirits—God is love and dwells in those who love Him.

1 Beloved, believe not every [aspirit](#note1a), but [btry](#note1b) the [cspirits](#note1c) whether they are of God: because many [dfalse prophets](#note1d) are gone out into the world.

2 Hereby [aknow](#note2a) ye the [bSpirit](#note2b) of God: Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the [cflesh](#note2c) is of God:

3 And every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is [anot of God](#note3a): and this is that spirit of [bantichrist](#note3b), whereof ye have heard that it should come; and even now already is it in the world.

4 Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world.

5 They are of the [aworld](#note5a): therefore speak they of the world, and the world heareth them.

6 We are of God: he that knoweth God [aheareth](#note6a) us; he that is not of God heareth not us. Hereby [bknow](#note6b) we the [cspirit](#note6c) of truth, and the spirit of [derror](#note6d).

7 Beloved, let us [alove](#note7a) one another: for [blove](#note7b) is of God; and every one that loveth is [cborn of God](#note7c), and knoweth God.

8 He that loveth not [aknoweth](#note8a) not God; for God is love.

9 In this was manifested the love of God toward us, because that God sent his only begotten Son into the world, that we might [alive](#note9a) through him.

10 Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that he [aloved](#note10a) us, and [bsent](#note10b) his Son to be the [cpropitiation](#note10c) for our sins.

11 Beloved, if God so loved us, we ought also to love one another.

12 [aNo](#note12a) man hath [bseen](#note12b) God at any time. If we love one another, [cGod](#note12c) [ddwelleth](#note12d) in us, and his love is perfected in us.

13 Hereby know we that we [adwell](#note13a) in him, and he in us, because he hath given us of his Spirit.

14 And we have seen and do [atestify](#note14a) that the Father [bsent](#note14b) the Son to be the [cSaviour](#note14c) of the [dworld](#note14d).

15 Whosoever shall [aconfess](#note15a) that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in God.

16 And we have known and believed the love that God hath to us. God is love; and he that [adwelleth](#note16a) in love dwelleth in God, and God in him.

17 Herein is our love made perfect, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment: because as he [ais](#note17a), so are we in this world.

18 There is no [afear](#note18a) in [blove](#note18b); but perfect [clove](#note18c) casteth out fear: because fear hath [dtorment](#note18d). He that feareth is not made perfect in love.

19 We [alove](#note19a) him, because he first loved us.

20 If a man say, I love God, and [ahateth](#note20a) his [bbrother](#note20b), he is a [cliar](#note20c): for he that loveth not his [dbrother](#note20d) whom he hath seen, how can he love God whom he hath not [eseen](#note20e)?

21 And this commandment have we from him, That he who [aloveth](#note21a) God love his brother also.

Chapter 5

Saints are born of God through belief in Christ—Water, blood, and the Spirit testify of Christ—Belief in Christ is required in order to gain eternal life.

1 Whosoever believeth that Jesus is the Christ is born of God: and every one that loveth him that begat loveth him also that is begotten of him.

2 By this we know that we [alove](#note2a) the [bchildren](#note2b) of God, when we love God, and keep his commandments.

3 For this is the [alove](#note3a) of God, that we [bkeep](#note3b) his commandments: and his commandments are not [cgrievous](#note3c).

4 For whatsoever is [aborn of God](#note4a) [bovercometh](#note4b) the world: and this is the [cvictory](#note4c) that overcometh the world, even our faith.

5 Who is he that [aovercometh](#note5a) the world, but he that believeth that Jesus is the Son of God?

6 This is he that [acame](#note6a) by water and blood, even Jesus Christ; not by water only, but by water and blood. And it is the [bSpirit](#note6b) that beareth [cwitness](#note6c), because the Spirit is truth.

7 For there are three that bear record in heaven, the [aFather](#note7a), the Word, and the Holy Ghost: and these three are [bone](#note7b).

8 And there are three that bear witness in earth, the [aSpirit](#note8a), and the water, and the [bblood](#note8b): and these three agree in one.

9 If we receive the [awitness](#note9a) of men, the [bwitness](#note9b) of God is greater: for this is the [cwitness](#note9c) of God which he hath testified of his Son.

10 He that [abelieveth](#note10a) on the Son of God hath the [bwitness](#note10b) in himself: he that believeth not God hath made him a liar; because he believeth not the [crecord](#note10c) that God gave of his Son.

11 And this is the record, that God hath given to us [aeternal life](#note11a), and this [blife](#note11b) is in his Son.

12 He that hath the Son hath life; and he that hath not the Son of God hath not life.

13 These things have I [awritten](#note13a) unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal [blife](#note13b), and that ye may believe on the name of the Son of God.

14 And this is the confidence that we have in him, that, if we ask any thing according to his [awill](#note14a), he [bheareth](#note14b) us:

15 And if we know that he hear us, whatsoever we [aask](#note15a), we know that we have the petitions that we [bdesired](#note15b) of him.

16 If any man see his brother sin a sin which is not unto death, he shall ask, and he shall give him life for them that sin not unto death. There is a sin unto death: I do not say that he shall pray for it.

17 All unrighteousness is [asin](#note17a): and there is a [bsin](#note17b) not unto death.

18 We know that whosoever is [aborn of God](#note18a) [bsinneth](#note18b) not; but he that is begotten of God [ckeepeth](#note18c) himself, and that [dwicked one](#note18d) toucheth him not.

19 And we know that we are of [aGod](#note19a), and the whole [bworld](#note19b) lieth in [cwickedness](#note19c).

20 And we know that the Son of God is come, and hath given us an understanding, that we may know him that is true, and we are in him that is true, even in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and [aeternal life](#note20a).

21 Little children, keep yourselves from idols. Amen.

Chapter 1

John rejoices because the children of the elect lady are true and faithful.

1 The elder unto the elect lady and her children, whom I love in the truth; and not I only, but also all they that have known the truth;

2 For the truth’s sake, which dwelleth in us, and shall be with us for ever.

3 Grace be with you, mercy, and peace, from God the Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.

4 I rejoiced greatly that I found of thy children walking in truth, as we have received a commandment from the Father.

5 And now I beseech thee, lady, not as though I wrote a new commandment unto thee, but that which we had from the beginning, that we love one another.

6 And this is [alove](#note6a), that we [bwalk](#note6b) after his commandments. This is the commandment, That, as ye have heard from the [cbeginning](#note6c), ye should walk in it.

7 For many [adeceivers](#note7a) are entered into the world, who confess not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh. This is a deceiver and an [bantichrist](#note7b).

8 Look to yourselves, that we lose not those things which we have [awrought](#note8a), but that we receive a full reward.

9 Whosoever transgresseth, and abideth not in the doctrine of Christ, hath not God. He that abideth in the doctrine of Christ, he hath both the Father and the Son.

10 If there come any unto you, and bring not this doctrine, receive him not into your [ahouse](#note10a), neither bid him God speed:

11 For he that biddeth him God speed is [apartaker](#note11a) of his evil deeds.

12 Having many things to write unto you, I would not [awrite](#note12a) with paper and ink: but I trust to come unto you, and speak face to face, that our joy may be full.

13 The children of thy elect sister greet thee. Amen.

Chapter 1

John commends Gaius for his help to those who love the truth.

1 The elder unto the wellbeloved Gaius, whom I love in the truth.

2 Beloved, I wish above all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health, even as thy soul prospereth.

3 For I rejoiced greatly, when the brethren came and testified of the truth that is in thee, even as thou walkest in the truth.

4 I have no greater [ajoy](#note4a) than to hear that my [bchildren](#note4b) [cwalk](#note4c) in truth.

5 Beloved, thou doest faithfully whatsoever thou doest to the [abrethren](#note5a), and to [bstrangers](#note5b);

6 Which have borne witness of thy charity before the church: whom if thou bring forward on their journey after a godly sort, thou shalt do well:

7 Because that for his name’s sake they went forth, taking nothing of the Gentiles.

8 We therefore ought to receive such, that we might be fellowhelpers to the truth.

9 I wrote unto the church: but Diotrephes, who loveth to have the [apreeminence](#note9a) among them, [breceiveth](#note9b) us not.

10 Wherefore, if I come, I will remember his deeds which he doeth, [aprating](#note10a) against us with malicious words: and not content therewith, neither doth he himself receive the brethren, and forbiddeth them that would, and casteth them out of the church.

11 Beloved, follow not that which is evil, but that which is good. He that doeth [agood](#note11a) is of God: but he that doeth evil hath not seen God.

12 Demetrius hath good report of all men, and of the truth itself: yea, and we also bear record; and ye know that our [arecord](#note12a) is true.

13 I had many things to write, but I will not with ink and pen [awrite](#note13a) unto thee:

14 But I trust I shall shortly see thee, and we shall speak face to face. Peace be to thee. Our friends salute thee. Greet the friends by name.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note13a)

  13a

  TG Scriptures, Writing of.

Chapter 1

Contend for the faith—Some angels kept not their first estate—Michael disputed about the body of Moses—Enoch prophesied of the Second Coming—Mockers will come in the last days.

1 [aJude](#note1a), the [bservant](#note1b) of Jesus Christ, and brother of James, to them that are sanctified by God the Father, and preserved in Jesus Christ, and called:

2 Mercy unto you, and peace, and love, be multiplied.

3 Beloved, when I gave all diligence to [awrite](#note3a) unto you of the common [bsalvation](#note3b), it was needful for me to write unto you, and exhort you that ye should [cearnestly](#note3c) [dcontend](#note3d) for the faith which was once delivered unto the saints.

4 For there are certain [amen](#note4a) crept in unawares, who were before of old ordained to this condemnation, [bungodly](#note4b) men, turning the grace of our God into [clasciviousness](#note4c), and [ddenying](#note4d) the only Lord God, and our Lord Jesus Christ.

5 I will therefore put you in remembrance, though ye once knew this, how that the Lord, having [asaved](#note5a) the people out of the land of Egypt, afterward [bdestroyed](#note5b) them that [cbelieved not](#note5c).

6 And the [aangels](#note6a) which kept not their [bfirst](#note6b) [cestate](#note6c), but left their own habitation, he hath reserved in everlasting chains under darkness unto the [djudgment](#note6d) of the great day.

7 Even as [aSodom](#note7a) and Gomorrha, and the cities about them in like manner, giving themselves over to [bfornication](#note7b), and going after [cstrange flesh](#note7c), are set forth for an [dexample](#note7d), suffering the [evengeance](#note7e) of eternal fire.

8 Likewise also these [afilthy](#note8a) dreamers [bdefile](#note8b) the flesh, despise dominion, and speak evil of dignities.

9 Yet [aMichael](#note9a) the [barchangel](#note9b), when contending with the devil he disputed about the body of [cMoses](#note9c), durst not bring against him a railing accusation, but said, The Lord rebuke thee.

10 But these speak evil of those things which they know not: but what they know naturally, as brute beasts, in those things they corrupt themselves.

11 Woe unto them! for they have gone in the way of [aCain](#note11a), and ran greedily after the error of [bBalaam](#note11b) for reward, and [cperished](#note11c) in the gainsaying of Core.

12 These are spots in your feasts of charity, when they feast with you, feeding themselves without fear: [aclouds](#note12a) they are without water, carried about of winds; trees whose fruit withereth, without fruit, twice dead, plucked up by the roots;

13 Raging waves of the sea, foaming out their own shame; wandering [astars](#note13a), to whom is reserved the blackness of darkness for ever.

14 And [aEnoch](#note14a) also, the seventh from Adam, prophesied of these, saying, Behold, the Lord [bcometh](#note14b) with ten thousands of his [csaints](#note14c),

15 To [aexecute](#note15a) [bjudgment](#note15b) upon all, and to convince all that are ungodly among them of all their ungodly deeds which they have ungodly committed, and of all their hard [cspeeches](#note15c) which ungodly sinners have spoken against him.

16 These are [amurmurers](#note16a), complainers, walking after their own [blusts](#note16b); and their mouth [cspeaketh](#note16c) great swelling words, having men’s persons in admiration because of [dadvantage](#note16d).

17 But, beloved, remember ye the words which were spoken before of the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ;

18 How that they told you there should be [amockers](#note18a) in the [blast time](#note18b), who should [cwalk](#note18c) after their own ungodly lusts.

19 These be they who [aseparate](#note19a) themselves, sensual, having not the [bSpirit](#note19b).

20 But ye, beloved, [abuilding](#note20a) up yourselves on your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Ghost,

21 Keep yourselves in the love of God, looking for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ unto eternal life.

22 And of some have [acompassion](#note22a), making a difference:

23 And others save with fear, pulling them out of the [afire](#note23a); hating even the [bgarment](#note23b) spotted by the flesh.

24 Now unto him that is able to keep you from falling, and to present you [afaultless](#note24a) before the presence of his glory with exceeding joy,

25 To the only wise God our Saviour, be glory and majesty, dominion and power, both now and ever. Amen.

Chapter 1

Christ chooses some as kings and priests unto God—Christ will come again—John sees the risen Lord.

1 [aThe](#note1a) [bRevelation](#note1b) of Jesus Christ, which God gave unto him, to shew unto his [cservants](#note1c) things which must [dshortly](#note1d) come to pass; and he sent and signified it by his [eangel](#note1e) unto his servant John:

2 Who bare record of the word of God, and of the testimony of Jesus Christ, and of all things that he saw.

3 [aBlessed](#note3a) is he that [breadeth](#note3b), and they that hear the words of this prophecy, and keep those things which are written therein: for the [ctime](#note3c) is at hand.

4 John to the [aseven](#note4a) churches which are in Asia: Grace be unto you, and peace, from him which [bis](#note4b), and which was, and which is to come; and from the seven [cSpirits](#note4c) which are before his throne;

5 And from Jesus Christ, who is the faithful witness, and the [afirst](#note5a) begotten of the dead, and the prince of the kings of the earth. Unto him that loved us, and [bwashed](#note5b) us from our sins in his own [cblood](#note5c),

6 And hath made us [akings](#note6a) and [bpriests](#note6b) unto God and his Father; to him be [cglory](#note6c) and [ddominion](#note6d) for ever and ever. Amen.

7 Behold, he [acometh](#note7a) with clouds; and every eye shall [bsee](#note7b) him, and they also which pierced him: and all [ckindreds](#note7c) of the earth shall [dwail](#note7d) because of him. Even so, Amen.

8 I am [aAlpha and Omega](#note8a), the [bbeginning](#note8b) and the ending, saith the Lord, which is, and which was, and which is to come, the [cAlmighty](#note8c).

9 I John, who also am your brother, and companion in tribulation, and in the kingdom and [apatience](#note9a) of Jesus Christ, was in the isle that is called Patmos, for the word of God, and for the testimony of Jesus Christ.

10 I was in the Spirit on the [aLord’s](#note10a) [bday](#note10b), and heard behind me a great [cvoice](#note10c), as of a trumpet,

11 Saying, I am Alpha and Omega, the [afirst](#note11a) and the last: and, What thou seest, [bwrite](#note11b) in a book, and send it unto the seven churches which are in Asia; unto Ephesus, and unto Smyrna, and unto Pergamos, and unto [cThyatira](#note11c), and unto Sardis, and unto Philadelphia, and unto [dLaodicea](#note11d).

12 And I turned to see the voice that spake with me. And being turned, I saw seven golden [acandlesticks](#note12a);

13 And in the midst of the seven candlesticks one like unto the [aSon of man](#note13a), [bclothed](#note13b) with a garment down to the foot, and girt about the paps with a golden girdle.

14 His head and his hairs were white like wool, as white as snow; and his [aeyes](#note14a) were as a flame of [bfire](#note14b);

15 And his feet like unto fine brass, as if they burned in a furnace; and his [avoice](#note15a) as the sound of many waters.

16 And he had in his right hand seven stars: and out of his mouth went a sharp twoedged [asword](#note16a): and his [bcountenance](#note16b) was as the [csun](#note16c) shineth in his strength.

17 And when I [asaw](#note17a) him, I fell at his feet as dead. And he laid his right hand upon me, saying unto me, Fear not; I am the first and the last:

18 I am he that [aliveth](#note18a), and was [bdead](#note18b); and, behold, I am alive for [cevermore](#note18c), Amen; and have the [dkeys](#note18d) of [ehell](#note18e) and of death.

19 [aWrite](#note19a) the things which thou hast seen, and the things which are, and the things which shall be hereafter;

20 The mystery of the seven stars which thou sawest in my right hand, and the seven golden candlesticks. The seven [astars](#note20a) are the [bangels](#note20b) of the seven churches: and the seven [ccandlesticks](#note20c) which thou sawest are the seven churches.
